name: SIGMA - 7Zip Compressing Dump Files
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of 7z in order to compress a file with a ".dmp"/".dump" extension, which could be a step in a process of dump file exfiltration.
false_positives: Legitimate use of 7z with a command line in which ".dmp" or ".dump" appears accidentally
Legitimate use of 7z to compress WER ".dmp" files for troubleshooting
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Description:"*7-Zip*" or (Image like~ ("*\\7z.exe", "*\\7zr.exe", "*\\7za.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("7z.exe", "7za.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.dmp*", "*.dump*", "*.hdmp*")))

name: SIGMA - Compress Data and Lock With Password for Exfiltration With 7-ZIP
author: frack113
description: An adversary may compress or encrypt data that is collected prior to exfiltration using 3rd party utilities
false_positives: Legitimate activity is expected since compressing files with a password is common.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Description:"*7-Zip*" or (Image like~ ("*\\7z.exe", "*\\7zr.exe", "*\\7za.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("7z.exe", "7za.exe"))) and CommandLine:"* -p*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* a *", "* u *")))

name: SIGMA - Potential DLL Injection Via AccCheckConsole
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution "AccCheckConsole" a command-line tool for verifying the accessibility implementation of an application's UI.
One of the tests that this checker can run are called "verification routine", which tests for things like Consistency, Navigation, etc.
The tool allows a user to provide a DLL that can contain a custom "verification routine". An attacker can build such DLLs and pass it via the CLI, which would then be loaded in the context of the "AccCheckConsole" utility.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the UI Accessibility Checker
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\AccCheckConsole.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AccCheckConsole.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -hwnd*", "* -process *", "* -window *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious AddinUtil.EXE CommandLine Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Michael McKinley (@McKinleyMike), Tony Latteri (@TheLatteri)
description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) with suspicious Addinroot or Pipelineroot paths. An adversary may execute AddinUtil.exe with uncommon Addinroot/Pipelineroot paths that point to the adversaries Addins.Store payload.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\addinutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AddInUtil.exe") and (((CommandLine like~ ("*-AddInRoot:*", "*-PipelineRoot:*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))) or ((CommandLine like~ ("*-AddInRoot:.*", "*-AddInRoot:\".\"*", "*-PipelineRoot:.*", "*-PipelineRoot:\".\"*")) and (CurrentDirectory like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of AddinUtil.EXE
author: Michael McKinley (@McKinleyMike), Tony Latteri (@TheLatteri)
description: Detects uncommon child processes of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via a custom Addins.Store payload.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\addinutil.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\conhost.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\werfault.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\werfault.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon AddinUtil.EXE CommandLine Execution
author: Michael McKinley (@McKinleyMike), Tony Latteri (@TheLatteri)
description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) with uncommon Addinroot or Pipelineroot paths. An adversary may execute AddinUtil.exe with uncommon Addinroot/Pipelineroot paths that point to the adversaries Addins.Store payload.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\addinutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AddInUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-AddInRoot:*", "*-PipelineRoot:*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*-AddInRoot:\"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\VSTA*", "*-AddInRoot:C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\VSTA*", "*-PipelineRoot:\"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\VSTA*", "*-PipelineRoot:C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\VSTA*"))))

name: SIGMA - AddinUtil.EXE Execution From Uncommon Directory
author: Michael McKinley (@McKinleyMike), Tony Latteri (@TheLatteri)
description: Detects execution of the Add-In deployment cache updating utility (AddInutil.exe) from a non-standard directory.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\addinutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AddInUtil.exe") and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Adplus.EXE Abuse
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "AdPlus.exe", a binary that is part of the Windows SDK that can be used as a LOLBIN in order to dump process memory and execute arbitrary commands.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of Adplus for debugging purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\adplus.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Adplus.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -hang *", "* -pn *", "* -pmn *", "* -p *", "* -po *", "* -c *", "* -sc *")))

name: SIGMA - AgentExecutor PowerShell Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), memory-shards
description: Detects execution of the AgentExecutor.exe binary. Which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute powershell scripts with the ExecutionPolicy "Bypass" or any binary named "powershell.exe" located in the path provided by 6th positional argument
false_positives: Legitimate use via Intune management. You exclude script paths and names to reduce FP rate
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"\\AgentExecutor.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AgentExecutor.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -powershell*", "* -remediationScript*"))) and (not ParentImage:"*\\Microsoft.Management.Services.IntuneWindowsAgent.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious AgentExecutor PowerShell Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), memory-shards
description: Detects execution of the AgentExecutor.exe binary. Which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute powershell scripts with the ExecutionPolicy "Bypass" or any binary named "powershell.exe" located in the path provided by 6th positional argument
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\AgentExecutor.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AgentExecutor.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -powershell*", "* -remediationScript*"))) and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\*")) or ParentImage:"*\\Microsoft.Management.Services.IntuneWindowsAgent.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of Appvlp.EXE
author: Sreeman
description:
Detects uncommon child processes of Appvlp.EXE
Appvlp or the Application Virtualization Utility is included with Microsoft Office. Attackers are able to abuse "AppVLP" to execute shell commands.
Normally, this binary is used for Application Virtualization, but it can also be abused to circumvent the ASR file path rule folder
or to mark a file as a system file.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\appvlp.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\rundll32.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\rundll32.exe"))) and (not ((Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Office*" and Image:"*\\msoasb.exe") or ((Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Office*" and Image:"*\\SkypeSrv\\*") and Image:"*\\SKYPESERVER.EXE") or (Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Office*" and Image:"*\\MSOUC.EXE"))))

name: SIGMA - AspNetCompiler Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects execution of "aspnet_compiler.exe" which can be abused to compile and execute C# code.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm64\\*")) and Image:"*\\aspnet_compiler.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process of AspNetCompiler
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "aspnet_compiler.exe".
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\aspnet_compiler.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\notepad.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Roaming\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious ASP.NET Compilation Via AspNetCompiler
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "aspnet_compiler.exe" with potentially suspicious paths for compilation.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm64\\*")) and Image:"*\\aspnet_compiler.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Roaming\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon  Assistive Technology Applications Execution Via AtBroker.EXE
author: Mateusz Wydra, oscd.community
description: Detects the start of a non built-in assistive technology applications via "Atbroker.EXE".
false_positives: Legitimate, non-default assistive technology applications execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\AtBroker.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AtBroker.exe") and CommandLine:"*start*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*animations*", "*audiodescription*", "*caretbrowsing*", "*caretwidth*", "*colorfiltering*", "*cursorindicator*", "*cursorscheme*", "*filterkeys*", "*focusborderheight*", "*focusborderwidth*", "*highcontrast*", "*keyboardcues*", "*keyboardpref*", "*livecaptions*", "*magnifierpane*", "*messageduration*", "*minimumhitradius*", "*mousekeys*", "*Narrator*", "*osk*", "*overlappedcontent*", "*showsounds*", "*soundsentry*", "*speechreco*", "*stickykeys*", "*togglekeys*", "*voiceaccess*", "*windowarranging*", "*windowtracking*", "*windowtrackingtimeout*", "*windowtrackingzorder*"))) and (not CommandLine:"*Oracle_JavaAccessBridge*"))

name: SIGMA - Hiding Files with Attrib.exe
author: Sami Ruohonen
description: Detects usage of attrib.exe to hide files from users.
false_positives: IgfxCUIService.exe hiding *.cui files via .bat script (attrib.exe a child of cmd.exe and igfxCUIService.exe is the parent of the cmd.exe)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\attrib.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ATTRIB.EXE") and CommandLine:"* +h *") and (not CommandLine:"*\\desktop.ini *") and (not (ParentImage:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"+R +H +S +A \\\*.cui" and ParentCommandLine:"C:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\\*.bat")))

name: SIGMA - Set Suspicious Files as System Files Using Attrib.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of attrib with the "+s" option to set scripts or executables located in suspicious locations as system files to hide them from users and make them unable to be deleted with simple rights. The rule limits the search to specific extensions and directories to avoid FPs
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\attrib.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ATTRIB.EXE") and CommandLine:"* +s*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* %*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\ProgramData\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.bat*", "*.dll*", "*.exe*", "*.hta*", "*.ps1*", "*.vbe*", "*.vbs*"))) and (not (CommandLine:"*\\Windows\\TEMP\\*" and CommandLine:"*.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Interactive AT Job
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detects an interactive AT job, which may be used as a form of privilege escalation.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\at.exe" and CommandLine:"*interactive*")

name: SIGMA - Audit Policy Tampering Via NT Resource Kit Auditpol
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Threat actors can use an older version of the auditpol binary available inside the NT resource kit to change audit policy configuration to impair detection capability.
This can be carried out by selectively disabling/removing certain audit policies as well as restoring a custom policy owned by the threat actor.
false_positives: The old auditpol utility isn't available by default on recent versions of Windows as it was replaced by a newer version. The FP rate should be very low except for tools that use a similar flag structure
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*/logon:none*", "*/system:none*", "*/sam:none*", "*/privilege:none*", "*/object:none*", "*/process:none*", "*/policy:none*"))

name: SIGMA - Audit Policy Tampering Via Auditpol
author: Janantha Marasinghe (https://github.com/blueteam0ps)
description:
Threat actors can use auditpol binary to change audit policy configuration to impair detection capability.
This can be carried out by selectively disabling/removing certain audit policies as well as restoring a custom policy owned by the threat actor.
false_positives: Administrator or administrator scripts might leverage the flags mentioned in the detection section. Either way, it should always be monitored
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\auditpol.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AUDITPOL.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*disable*", "*clear*", "*remove*", "*restore*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Autorun Registry Modified via WMI
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious activity where the WMIC process is used to create an autorun registry entry via reg.exe, which is often indicative of persistence mechanisms employed by malware.
false_positives: Legitimate administrative activity or software installations
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\wmic.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wmic.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\wmiprvse.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"* add *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run*")))) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs*", "*:\\ProgramData'*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp*", "*:\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming*", "*:\\$Recycle.bin*", "*:\\Users\\Default*", "*:\\Users\\public*", "*%temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*%Public%*", "*%AppData%*")) or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Favorites*", "*\\Favourites*", "*\\Contacts*", "*\\Music*", "*\\Pictures*", "*\\Documents*", "*\\Photos*")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious BitLocker Access Agent Update Utility Execution
author: andrewdanis, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of the BitLocker Access Agent Update Utility (baaupdate.exe) which is not a common parent process for other processes.
Suspicious child processes spawned by baaupdate.exe could indicate an attempt at lateral movement via BitLocker DCOM & COM Hijacking.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\baaupdate.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Indirect Inline Command Execution Via Bash.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Detects execution of Microsoft bash launcher with the "-c" flag.
This can be used to potentially bypass defenses and execute Linux or Windows-based binaries directly via bash.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\bash.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\bash.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"Bash.exe") and CommandLine:"* -c *")

name: SIGMA - Indirect Command Execution From Script File Via Bash.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of Microsoft bash launcher without any flags to execute the content of a bash script directly.
This can be used to potentially bypass defenses and execute Linux or Windows-based binaries directly via bash.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\bash.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\bash.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"Bash.exe") and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*bash.exe -*", "*bash -*")) or (?CommandLine == null) or CommandLine=="" or (CommandLine like~ ("bash.exe", "bash")))))

name: SIGMA - Boot Configuration Tampering Via Bcdedit.EXE
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detects the use of the bcdedit command to tamper with the boot configuration data. This technique is often times used by malware or attackers as a destructive way before launching ransomware.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bcdedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bcdedit.exe") and CommandLine:"*set*" and ((CommandLine:"*bootstatuspolicy*" and CommandLine:"*ignoreallfailures*") or (CommandLine:"*recoveryenabled*" and CommandLine:"*no*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Ransomware or Unauthorized MBR Tampering Via Bcdedit.EXE
author: @neu5ron
description: Detects potential malicious and unauthorized usage of bcdedit.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bcdedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bcdedit.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*delete*", "*deletevalue*", "*import*", "*safeboot*", "*network*")))

name: SIGMA - Data Export From MSSQL Table Via BCP.EXE
author: Omar Khaled (@beacon_exe), MahirAli Khan (in/mahiralikhan), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of the BCP utility in order to export data from the database.
Attackers were seen saving their malware to a database column or table and then later extracting it via "bcp.exe" into a file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bcp.exe" or OriginalFileName:"BCP.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* out *", "* queryout *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process Of BgInfo.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of "BgInfo.exe" which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via external VBScript
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\bginfo.exe", "*\\bginfo64.exe")) and ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\PerfLogs\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of BgInfo.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description: Detects uncommon child processes of "BgInfo.exe" which could be a sign of potential abuse of the binary to proxy execution via external VBScript
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\bginfo.exe", "*\\bginfo64.exe"))

name: SIGMA - BitLockerTogo.EXE Execution
author: Josh Nickels, mttaggart
description:
Detects the execution of "BitLockerToGo.EXE".
BitLocker To Go is BitLocker Drive Encryption on removable data drives. This feature includes the encryption of, USB flash drives, SD cards, External hard disk drives, Other drives that are formatted by using the NTFS, FAT16, FAT32, or exFAT file system.
This is a rarely used application and usage of it at all is worth investigating.
Malware such as Lumma stealer has been seen using this process as a target for process hollowing.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of BitLockerToGo.exe to encrypt portable devices.
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\BitLockerToGo.exe"

name: SIGMA - File Download Via Bitsadmin
author: Michael Haag, FPT.EagleEye
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file
false_positives: Some legitimate apps use this, but limited.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine:"* /transfer *" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and CommandLine:"*http*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Download From Direct IP Via Bitsadmin
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file using an URL that contains an IP
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /transfer *", "* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*://1*", "*://2*", "*://3*", "*://4*", "*://5*", "*://6*", "*://7*", "*://8*", "*://9*"))) and (not CommandLine:"*://7-*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Download From File-Sharing Website Via Bitsadmin
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file from a suspicious domain
false_positives: Some legitimate apps use this, but limited.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /transfer *", "* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com*", "*anonfiles.com*", "*cdn.discordapp.com*", "*ddns.net*", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com*", "*ghostbin.co*", "*glitch.me*", "*gofile.io*", "*hastebin.com*", "*mediafire.com*", "*mega.nz*", "*onrender.com*", "*pages.dev*", "*paste.ee*", "*pastebin.com*", "*pastebin.pl*", "*pastetext.net*", "*privatlab.com*", "*privatlab.net*", "*send.exploit.in*", "*sendspace.com*", "*storage.googleapis.com*", "*storjshare.io*", "*supabase.co*", "*temp.sh*", "*transfer.sh*", "*trycloudflare.com*", "*ufile.io*", "*w3spaces.com*", "*workers.dev*")))

name: SIGMA - File With Suspicious Extension Downloaded Via Bitsadmin
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file with a suspicious extension
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /transfer *", "* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.7z*", "*.asax*", "*.ashx*", "*.asmx*", "*.asp*", "*.aspx*", "*.bat*", "*.cfm*", "*.cgi*", "*.chm*", "*.cmd*", "*.dll*", "*.gif*", "*.jpeg*", "*.jpg*", "*.jsp*", "*.jspx*", "*.log*", "*.png*", "*.ps1*", "*.psm1*", "*.rar*", "*.scf*", "*.sct*", "*.txt*", "*.vbe*", "*.vbs*", "*.war*", "*.wsf*", "*.wsh*", "*.xll*", "*.zip*")))

name: SIGMA - File Download Via Bitsadmin To A Suspicious Target Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file to a suspicious target folder
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /transfer *", "* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs*", "*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*%ProgramData%*", "*%public%*")))

name: SIGMA - File Download Via Bitsadmin To An Uncommon Target Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of bitsadmin downloading a file to uncommon target folder
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /transfer *", "* /create *", "* /addfile *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*%AppData%*", "*%temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Monitoring For Persistence Via BITS
author: Sreeman
description:
BITS will allow you to schedule a command to execute after a successful download to notify you that the job is finished.
When the job runs on the system the command specified in the BITS job will be executed.
This can be abused by actors to create a backdoor within the system and for persistence.
It will be chained in a BITS job to schedule the download of malware/additional binaries and execute the program after being downloaded.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\bitsadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bitsadmin.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*/SetNotifyCmdLine*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*%COMSPEC%*", "*cmd.exe*", "*regsvr32.exe*"))) or (CommandLine:"*/Addfile*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*http:*", "*https:*", "*ftp:*", "*ftps:*")))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Data Stealing Via Chromium Headless Debugging
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects chromium based browsers starting in headless and debugging mode and pointing to a user profile. This could be a sign of data stealing or remote control
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*--remote-debugging-*" and CommandLine:"*--user-data-dir*" and CommandLine:"*--headless*")

name: SIGMA - Browser Execution In Headless Mode
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of Chromium based browser in headless mode
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--headless*")

name: SIGMA - File Download with Headless Browser
author: Sreeman, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of chromium based browser in headless mode using the "dump-dom" command line to download files
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*--headless*" and CommandLine:"*dump-dom*" and CommandLine:"*http*")) and (not (((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeWebView\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\EdgeWebView\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.MicrosoftEdge*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe", "*\\MicrosoftEdge.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--headless --disable-gpu --disable-extensions --disable-plugins --mute-audio --no-first-run --incognito --aggressive-cache-discard --dump-dom*") or ((Image like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\WindowsApps\\*", "*\\Windows\\SystemApps\\Microsoft.MicrosoftEdge*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\msedge.exe", "*\\MicrosoftEdge.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--headless --disable-gpu --disable-extensions --disable-plugins --mute-audio --no-first-run --incognito --aggressive-cache-discard --dump-dom*"))))

name: SIGMA - Chromium Browser Instance Executed With Custom Extension
author: Aedan Russell, frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a Chromium based browser process with the 'load-extension' flag to start a instance with a custom extension
false_positives: Usage of Chrome Extensions in testing tools such as BurpSuite will trigger this alert
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--load-extension=*")

name: SIGMA - Chromium Browser Headless Execution To Mockbin Like Site
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a Chromium based browser process with the "headless" flag and a URL pointing to the mockbin.org service (which can be used to exfiltrate data).
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--headless*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*://run.mocky*", "*://mockbin*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Chromium Browser Instance Executed With Custom Extension
author: Aedan Russell, frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious process spawning a Chromium based browser process with the 'load-extension' flag to start an instance with a custom extension
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*--load-extension=*")

name: SIGMA - File Download From Browser Process Via Inline URL
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of a browser process with a URL argument pointing to a file with a potentially interesting extension. This can be abused to download arbitrary files or to hide from the user for example by launching the browser in a minimized state.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*http*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*.7z", "*.dat", "*.dll", "*.exe", "*.hta", "*.ps1", "*.psm1", "*.txt", "*.vbe", "*.vbs", "*.zip")))

name: SIGMA - Browser Started with Remote Debugging
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects browsers starting with the remote debugging flags. Which is a technique often used to perform browser injection attacks
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* --remote-debugging-*" or (Image:"*\\firefox.exe" and CommandLine:"* -start-debugger-server*"))

name: SIGMA - Tor Client/Browser Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of Tor or Tor-Browser to connect to onion routing networks
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*\\tor.exe", "*\\Tor Browser\\Browser\\firefox.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Calculator Usage
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious use of 'calc.exe' with command line parameters or in a suspicious directory, which is likely caused by some PoC or detection evasion.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\calc.exe *" or (Image:"*\\calc.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via Cdb.EXE
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "cdb.exe" to launch arbitrary processes or commands from a debugger script file
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cdb.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CDB.Exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* -cf *")))

name: SIGMA - New Root Certificate Installed Via CertMgr.EXE
author: oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st
description:
Detects execution of "certmgr" with the "add" flag in order to install a new certificate on the system.
Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.
false_positives: Help Desk or IT may need to manually add a corporate Root CA on occasion. Need to test if GPO push doesn't trigger FP
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\CertMgr.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CERTMGT.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*/add*" and CommandLine:"*root*"))

name: SIGMA - File Download via CertOC.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when a user downloads a file by using CertOC.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certoc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertOC.exe") and (CommandLine:"*-GetCACAPS*" and CommandLine:"*http*"))

name: SIGMA - File Download From IP Based URL Via CertOC.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when a user downloads a file from an IP based URL using CertOC.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certoc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertOC.exe") and CommandLine regex~ ":\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}" and CommandLine:"*-GetCACAPS*")

name: SIGMA - DLL Loaded via CertOC.EXE
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects when a user installs certificates by using CertOC.exe to loads the target DLL file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certoc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertOC.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -LoadDLL *", "* /LoadDLL *", "* –LoadDLL *", "* —LoadDLL *", "* ―LoadDLL *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious DLL Loaded via CertOC.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when a user installs certificates by using CertOC.exe to load the target DLL file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certoc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertOC.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -LoadDLL *", "* /LoadDLL *", "* –LoadDLL *", "* —LoadDLL *", "* ―LoadDLL *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Appdata\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - New Root Certificate Installed Via Certutil.EXE
author: oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st
description:
Detects execution of "certutil" with the "addstore" flag in order to install a new certificate on the system.
Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.
false_positives: Help Desk or IT may need to manually add a corporate Root CA on occasion. Need to test if GPO push doesn't trigger FP
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-addstore*", "*/addstore*", "*–addstore*", "*—addstore*", "*―addstore*") and CommandLine:"*root*")

name: SIGMA - File Decoded From Base64/Hex Via Certutil.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects the execution of certutil with either the "decode" or "decodehex" flags to decode base64 or hex encoded files. This can be abused by attackers to decode an encoded payload before execution
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-decode *", "*/decode *", "*–decode *", "*—decode *", "*―decode *") or CommandLine like~ ("*-decodehex *", "*/decodehex *", "*–decodehex *", "*—decodehex *", "*―decodehex *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Download Via Certutil.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*urlcache *", "*verifyctl *")) and CommandLine:"*http*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Downloaded From Direct IP Via Certutil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files from direct IPs.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*urlcache *", "*verifyctl *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*://1*", "*://2*", "*://3*", "*://4*", "*://5*", "*://6*", "*://7*", "*://8*", "*://9*"))) and (not CommandLine:"*://7-*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Downloaded From File-Sharing Website Via Certutil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with certain flags that allow the utility to download files from file-sharing websites.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*urlcache *", "*verifyctl *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com*", "*anonfiles.com*", "*cdn.discordapp.com*", "*ddns.net*", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com*", "*ghostbin.co*", "*glitch.me*", "*gofile.io*", "*hastebin.com*", "*mediafire.com*", "*mega.nz*", "*onrender.com*", "*pages.dev*", "*paste.ee*", "*pastebin.com*", "*pastebin.pl*", "*pastetext.net*", "*privatlab.com*", "*privatlab.net*", "*send.exploit.in*", "*sendspace.com*", "*storage.googleapis.com*", "*storjshare.io*", "*supabase.co*", "*temp.sh*", "*transfer.sh*", "*trycloudflare.com*", "*ufile.io*", "*w3spaces.com*", "*workers.dev*")))

name: SIGMA - File Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64. This can be abused by threat actors and attackers for data exfiltration
false_positives: As this is a general purpose rule, legitimate usage of the encode functionality will trigger some false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-encode*", "*/encode*", "*–encode*", "*—encode*", "*―encode*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64 where the extensions of the file is suspicious
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-encode*", "*/encode*", "*–encode*", "*—encode*", "*―encode*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.acl*", "*.bat*", "*.doc*", "*.gif*", "*.jpeg*", "*.jpg*", "*.mp3*", "*.pdf*", "*.png*", "*.ppt*", "*.tmp*", "*.xls*", "*.xml*")))

name: SIGMA - File In Suspicious Location Encoded To Base64 Via Certutil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of certutil with the "encode" flag to encode a file to base64 where the files are located in potentially suspicious locations
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-encode*", "*/encode*", "*–encode*", "*—encode*", "*―encode*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*$Recycle.Bin*")))

name: SIGMA - Certificate Exported Via Certutil.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the certutil with the "exportPFX" flag which allows the utility to export certificates.
false_positives: There legitimate reasons to export certificates. Investigate the activity to determine if it's benign
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-exportPFX *", "*/exportPFX *", "*–exportPFX *", "*—exportPFX *", "*―exportPFX *"))

name: SIGMA - Potential NTLM Coercion Via Certutil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects possible NTLM coercion via certutil using the 'syncwithWU' flag
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertUtil.exe") and (CommandLine:"* -syncwithWU *" and CommandLine:"* \\\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Console CodePage Lookup Via CHCP
author: _pete_0, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects use of chcp to look up the system locale value as part of host discovery
false_positives: During Anaconda update the 'conda.exe' process will eventually execution the 'chcp' command.
Discord was seen using chcp to look up code pages
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\cmd.exe" and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *") or ParentCommandLine like~ ("* -r *", "* /r *", "* –r *", "* —r *", "* ―r *") or ParentCommandLine like~ ("* -k *", "* /k *", "* –k *", "* —k *", "* ―k *")) and Image:"*\\chcp.com" and (CommandLine like~ ("*chcp", "*chcp ", "*chcp  ")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious CodePage Switch Via CHCP
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects a code page switch in command line or batch scripts to a rare language
false_positives: Administrative activity (adjust code pages according to your organization's region)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\chcp.com" and (CommandLine like~ ("* 936", "* 1258")))

name: SIGMA - Deleted Data Overwritten Via Cipher.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Detects usage of the "cipher" built-in utility in order to overwrite deleted data from disk.
Adversaries may destroy data and files on specific systems or in large numbers on a network to interrupt availability to systems, services, and network resources.
Data destruction is likely to render stored data irrecoverable by forensic techniques through overwriting files or data on local and remote drives
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"CIPHER.EXE" or Image:"*\\cipher.exe") and CommandLine:"* /w:*")

name: SIGMA - Process Access via TrolleyExpress Exclusion
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a possible process memory dump that uses the white-listed Citrix TrolleyExpress.exe filename as a way to dump the lsass process memory
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\TrolleyExpress 7*", "*\\TrolleyExpress 8*", "*\\TrolleyExpress 9*", "*\\TrolleyExpress.exe 7*", "*\\TrolleyExpress.exe 8*", "*\\TrolleyExpress.exe 9*", "*\\TrolleyExpress.exe -ma *")) or (Image:"*\\TrolleyExpress.exe" and (not (OriginalFileName:"*CtxInstall*" or (?OriginalFileName == null)))))

name: SIGMA - Data Copied To Clipboard Via Clip.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of clip.exe in order to copy data to the clipboard. Adversaries may collect data stored in the clipboard from users copying information within or between applications.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\clip.exe" or OriginalFileName:"clip.exe")

name: SIGMA - Cloudflared Portable Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the "cloudflared" binary from a non standard location.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of Cloudflared portable versions
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\cloudflared.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\cloudflared\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\cloudflared\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Cloudflared Quick Tunnel Execution
author: Sajid Nawaz Khan
description:
Detects creation of an ad-hoc Cloudflare Quick Tunnel, which can be used to tunnel local services such as HTTP, RDP, SSH and SMB.
The free TryCloudflare Quick Tunnel will generate a random subdomain on trycloudflare[.]com, following a call to api[.]trycloudflare[.]com.
The tool has been observed in use by threat groups including Akira ransomware.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of Cloudflare Quick Tunnel

name: SIGMA - Cloudflared Tunnel Connections Cleanup
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool with the tunnel "cleanup" flag in order to cleanup tunnel connections.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"* tunnel *" and CommandLine:"*cleanup *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-config *", "*-connector-id *")))

name: SIGMA - Cloudflared Tunnel Execution
author: Janantha Marasinghe, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool to connect back to a tunnel. This was seen used by threat actors to maintain persistence and remote access to compromised networks.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"* tunnel *" and CommandLine:"* run *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-config *", "*-credentials-contents *", "*-credentials-file *", "*-token *")))

name: SIGMA - New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of "cmdkey.exe" to add generic credentials.
As an example, this can be used before connecting to an RDP session via command line interface.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmdkey.exe" or OriginalFileName:"cmdkey.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -g*", "* /g*", "* –g*", "* —g*", "* ―g*") and CommandLine like~ ("* -u*", "* /u*", "* –u*", "* —u*", "* ―u*") and CommandLine like~ ("* -p*", "* /p*", "* –p*", "* —p*", "* ―p*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE
author: jmallette, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of cmdkey to look for cached credentials on the system
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmdkey.exe" or OriginalFileName:"cmdkey.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -l*", "* /l*", "* –l*", "* —l*", "* ―l*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Arbitrary File Download Via Cmdl32.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Detects execution of Cmdl32 with the "/vpn" and "/lan" flags.
Attackers can abuse this utility in order to download arbitrary files via a configuration file.
Inspect the location and the content of the file passed as an argument in order to determine if it is suspicious.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmdl32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CMDL32.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*/vpn*" and CommandLine:"*/lan*"))

name: SIGMA - Change Default File Association Via Assoc
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Detects file association changes using the builtin "assoc" command.
When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine:"*assoc*")

name: SIGMA - Change Default File Association To Executable Via Assoc
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when a program changes the default file association of any extension to an executable.
When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine:"*assoc *" and CommandLine:"*exefile*")) and (not CommandLine:"*.exe=exefile*"))

name: SIGMA - Copy .DMP/.DUMP Files From Remote Share Via Cmd.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the copy builtin cmd command to copy files with the ".dmp"/".dump" extension from a remote share
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and ((CommandLine:"*copy *" and CommandLine:"* \\\\*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.dmp*", "*.dump*", "*.hdmp*"))))

name: SIGMA - Curl Download And Execute Combination
author: Sreeman, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Adversaries can use curl to download payloads remotely and execute them. Curl is included by default in Windows 10 build 17063 and later.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *") and (CommandLine:"*curl *" and CommandLine:"*http*" and CommandLine:"*-o*" and CommandLine:"*&*"))

name: SIGMA - File Deletion Via Del
author: frack113
description:
Detects execution of the builtin "del"/"erase" commands in order to delete files.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.
false_positives: False positives levels will differ Depending on the environment. You can use a combination of ParentImage and other keywords from the CommandLine field to filter legitimate activity
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*del *", "*erase *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -f*", "* /f*", "* –f*", "* —f*", "* ―f*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s*", "* /s*", "* –s*", "* —s*", "* ―s*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -q*", "* /q*", "* –q*", "* —q*", "* ―q*")))

name: SIGMA - Greedy File Deletion Using Del
author: frack113 , X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "del" builtin command to remove files using greedy/wildcard expression. This is often used by malware to delete content of folders that perhaps contains the initial malware infection or to delete evidence.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*del *", "*erase *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\\*.au3*", "*\\\*.dll*", "*\\\*.exe*", "*\\\*.js*")))

name: SIGMA - File And SubFolder Enumeration Via Dir Command
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of the "dir" command part of Windows CMD with the "/S" command line flag in order to enumerate files in a specified directory and all subdirectories.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*dir*-s*", "*dir*/s*", "*dir*–s*", "*dir*—s*", "*dir*―s*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Dosfuscation Activity
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects possible payload obfuscation via the commandline

name: SIGMA - Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers (js/vbs > powershell)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*http*" and CommandLine:"*://*" and CommandLine:"*%AppData%*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Privilege Escalation Using Symlink Between Osk and Cmd
author: frack113
description: Detects the creation of a symbolic link between "cmd.exe" and the accessibility on-screen keyboard binary (osk.exe) using "mklink". This technique provides an elevated command prompt to the user from the login screen without the need to log in.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine:"*mklink*" and CommandLine:"*\\osk.exe*" and CommandLine:"*\\cmd.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - VolumeShadowCopy Symlink Creation Via Mklink
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community
description: Shadow Copies storage symbolic link creation using operating systems utilities
false_positives: Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*mklink*" and CommandLine:"*HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Execution From Internet Hosted WebDav Share
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the "net use" command to mount a WebDAV server and then immediately execute some content in it. As seen being used in malicious LNK files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe*" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.EXE") and (CommandLine:"* net use http*" and CommandLine:"*& start /b *" and CommandLine:"*\\DavWWWRoot\\*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe *", "*.dll *", "*.bat *", "*.vbs *", "*.ps1 *")))

name: SIGMA - Cmd.EXE Missing Space Characters Execution Anomaly
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects Windows command lines that miss a space before or after the /c flag when running a command using the cmd.exe.
This could be a sign of obfuscation of a fat finger problem (typo by the developer).
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("*cmd.exe/c*", "*\\cmd/c*", "*\"cmd/c*", "*cmd.exe/k*", "*\\cmd/k*", "*\"cmd/k*", "*cmd.exe/r*", "*\\cmd/r*", "*\"cmd/r*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*/cwhoami*", "*/cpowershell*", "*/cschtasks*", "*/cbitsadmin*", "*/ccertutil*", "*/kwhoami*", "*/kpowershell*", "*/kschtasks*", "*/kbitsadmin*", "*/kcertutil*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd.exe /c*", "*cmd /c*", "*cmd.exe /k*", "*cmd /k*", "*cmd.exe /r*", "*cmd /r*"))) and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*cmd.exe /c *", "*cmd /c *", "*cmd.exe /k *", "*cmd /k *", "*cmd.exe /r *", "*cmd /r *")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Microsoft VS Code\\resources\\app\\node_modules*", "*cmd.exe/c .", "cmd.exe /c")))))

name: SIGMA - NtdllPipe Like Activity Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command that type the content of ntdll.dll to a different file or a pipe in order to evade AV / EDR detection. As seen being used in the POC NtdllPipe
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*type %windir%\\system32\\ntdll.dll*", "*type %systemroot%\\system32\\ntdll.dll*", "*type c:\\windows\\system32\\ntdll.dll*", "*\\ntdll.dll > \\\\.\\pipe\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential CommandLine Path Traversal Via Cmd.EXE
author: xknow @xknow_infosec, Tim Shelton
description: Detects potential path traversal attempt via cmd.exe. Could indicate possible command/argument confusion/hijacking
false_positives: Java tools are known to produce false-positive when loading libraries
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage:"*\\cmd.exe" or Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"cmd.exe") and ((ParentCommandLine like~ ("*/c*", "*/k*", "*/r*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*/c*", "*/k*", "*/r*"))) and (ParentCommandLine:"/../../" or CommandLine:"*/../../*")) and (not CommandLine:"*\\Tasktop\\keycloak\\bin\\/../../jre\\bin\\java*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Ping/Copy Command Combination
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uncommon and potentially suspicious one-liner command containing both "ping" and "copy" at the same time, which is usually used by malware.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine:"*ping*" and CommandLine:"*copy *") and CommandLine like~ ("* -n *", "* /n *", "* –n *", "* —n *", "* ―n *") and CommandLine like~ ("* -y *", "* /y *", "* –y *", "* —y *", "* ―y *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Ping/Del Command Combination
author: Ilya Krestinichev
description: Detects a method often used by ransomware. Which combines the "ping" to wait a couple of seconds and then "del" to delete the file in question. Its used to hide the file responsible for the initial infection for example
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("* -n *", "* /n *", "* –n *", "* —n *", "* ―n *") and CommandLine:"*Nul*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -f *", "* /f *", "* –f *", "* —f *", "* ―f *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -q *", "* /q *", "* –q *", "* —q *", "* ―q *")) and (CommandLine:"*ping*" and CommandLine:"*del *"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious CMD Shell Output Redirect
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects inline Windows shell commands redirecting output via the ">" symbol to a suspicious location.
This technique is sometimes used by malicious actors in order to redirect the output of reconnaissance commands such as "hostname" and "dir" to files for future exfiltration.
false_positives: Legitimate admin or third party scripts used for diagnostic collection might generate some false positives
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*>?%APPDATA%\\*", "*>?%TEMP%\\*", "*>?%TMP%\\*", "*>?%USERPROFILE%\\*", "*>?C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*>?C:\\Temp\\*", "*>?C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*>?C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* >*", "*\">*", "*'>*")) and (CommandLine:"*C:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Directory Removal Via Rmdir
author: frack113
description:
Detects execution of the builtin "rmdir" command in order to delete directories.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine:"*rmdir*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/s*", "*/q*")))

name: SIGMA - Copy From VolumeShadowCopy Via Cmd.EXE
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the builtin "copy" command that targets a shadow copy (sometimes used to copy registry hives that are in use)
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*copy *" and CommandLine:"*\\\\\?\\GLOBALROOT\\Device\\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy*")

name: SIGMA - Read Contents From Stdin Via Cmd.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect the use of "<" to read and potentially execute a file via cmd.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe" or Image:"*\\cmd.exe") and CommandLine:"*<*")

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via Sticky Key Backdoor
author: Sreeman
description:
By replacing the sticky keys executable with the local admins CMD executable, an attacker is able to access a privileged windows console session without authenticating to the system.
When the sticky keys are "activated" the privilleged shell is launched.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*copy *" and CommandLine:"*/y *" and CommandLine:"*C:\\windows\\system32\\cmd.exe C:\\windows\\system32\\sethc.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Sticky Key Like Backdoor Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), @twjackomo, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\winlogon.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\wt.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*sethc.exe*", "*utilman.exe*", "*osk.exe*", "*Magnify.exe*", "*Narrator.exe*", "*DisplaySwitch.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Download/Upload Activity Using Type Command
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "type" command to download/upload data from WebDAV server
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*type *" and CommandLine:"* > \\\\*") or (CommandLine:"*type \\\\*" and CommandLine:"* > *"))

name: SIGMA - Unusual Parent Process For Cmd.EXE
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects suspicious parent process for cmd.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\csrss.exe", "*\\ctfmon.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe", "*\\epad.exe", "*\\FlashPlayerUpdateService.exe", "*\\GoogleUpdate.exe", "*\\jucheck.exe", "*\\jusched.exe", "*\\LogonUI.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\SearchIndexer.exe", "*\\SearchProtocolHost.exe", "*\\SIHClient.exe", "*\\sihost.exe", "*\\slui.exe", "*\\spoolsv.exe", "*\\sppsvc.exe", "*\\taskhostw.exe", "*\\unsecapp.exe", "*\\WerFault.exe", "*\\wermgr.exe", "*\\wlanext.exe", "*\\WUDFHost.exe")))

name: SIGMA - CMSTP Execution Process Creation
author: Nik Seetharaman
description: Detects various indicators of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer execution
false_positives: Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\cmstp.exe"

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Detects the execution of "ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE", a binary part of Windows Defender used to manage settings in Windows Defender.
Users can configure different pilot collections for each of the co-management workloads.
It can be abused by attackers in order to upload or download files.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe*" or Image:"*\\ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp://*", "*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Powershell Executed From Headless ConHost Process
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description:
Detects the use of powershell commands from headless ConHost window.
The "--headless" flag hides the windows from the user upon execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\conhost.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CONHOST.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*--headless*" and CommandLine:"*powershell*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious High IntegrityLevel Conhost Legacy Option
author: frack113
description: ForceV1 asks for information directly from the kernel space. Conhost connects to the console application. High IntegrityLevel means the process is running with elevated privileges, such as an Administrator context.
false_positives: Very Likely, including launching cmd.exe via Run As Administrator
query: any where EventID:1 and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "S-1-16-12288")) and (CommandLine:"*conhost.exe*" and CommandLine:"*0xffffffff*" and CommandLine:"*-ForceV1*"))

name: SIGMA - Conhost.exe CommandLine Path Traversal
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: detects the usage of path traversal in conhost.exe indicating possible command/argument confusion/hijacking
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentCommandLine:"*conhost*" and CommandLine:"*/../../*")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of Conhost.EXE
author: omkar72
description: Detects uncommon "conhost" child processes. This could be a sign of "conhost" usage as a LOLBIN or potential process injection activity.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\conhost.exe" and (not (Image:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\conhost.exe" or (?Image == null) or Image=="")) and (not Provider_Name:"SystemTraceProvider-Process"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Processes Spawned by ConHost
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes related to Windows Shell utilities spawned by `conhost.exe`, which could indicate malicious activity using trusted system components.
false_positives: Legitimate administrative tasks using `conhost.exe` to spawn child processes such as `cmd.exe`, `powershell.exe`, or `regsvr32.exe`.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\conhost.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("cmd.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "powershell_ise.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.dll", "regsvr32.exe", "wscript.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Conhost Spawned By Uncommon Parent Process
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects when the Console Window Host (conhost.exe) process is spawned by an uncommon parent process, which could be indicative of potential code injection activity.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\conhost.exe" and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\explorer.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\smss.exe", "*\\spoolsv.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\userinit.exe", "*\\wininit.exe", "*\\winlogon.exe"))) and (not (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*-k apphost -s AppHostSvc*", "*-k imgsvc*", "*-k localService -p -s RemoteRegistry*", "*-k LocalSystemNetworkRestricted -p -s NgcSvc*", "*-k NetSvcs -p -s NcaSvc*", "*-k netsvcs -p -s NetSetupSvc*", "*-k netsvcs -p -s wlidsvc*", "*-k NetworkService -p -s DoSvc*", "*-k wsappx -p -s AppXSvc*", "*-k wsappx -p -s ClipSVC*", "*-k wusvcs -p -s WaaSMedicSvc*"))) and (not (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dropbox\\Client\\*", "*C:\\Program Files\\Dropbox\\Client\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Control Panel Items
author: Kyaw Min Thein, Furkan Caliskan (@caliskanfurkan_)
description: Detects the malicious use of a control panel item
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*CurrentVersion\\Control Panel\\CPLs*")) or (CommandLine:"*.cpl" and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\System32\\*", "*%System%*", "*|C:\\Windows\\system32|*")) or (CommandLine:"*regsvr32 *" and CommandLine:"* /s *" and CommandLine:"*igfxCPL.cpl*")))))

name: SIGMA - CreateDump Process Dump
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uses of the createdump.exe LOLOBIN utility to dump process memory
false_positives: Command lines that use the same flags
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\createdump.exe" or OriginalFileName:"FX_VER_INTERNALNAME_STR") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -u *", "* --full *", "* -f *", "* --name *", "*.dmp *")))

name: SIGMA - Dynamic .NET Compilation Via Csc.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "csc.exe" to compile .NET code. Attackers often leverage this to compile code on the fly and use it in other stages.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\csc.exe" and ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Contacts\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Pictures\\*")) or CommandLine regex~ "([Pp]rogram[Dd]ata|%([Ll]ocal)?[Aa]pp[Dd]ata%|\\[Aa]pp[Dd]ata\\([Ll]ocal([Ll]ow)?|[Rr]oaming))\\[^\\]{1,256}$") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*")) or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\sdiagnhost.exe" or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\inetsrv\\w3wp.exe")) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\chocolatey\\choco.exe", "C:\\ProgramData\\chocolatey\\tools\\shimgen.exe")) or ParentCommandLine:"*\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection*" or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*JwB7ACIAZgBhAGkAbABlAGQAIgA6AHQAcgB1AGUALAAiAG0AcwBnACIAOgAiAEEAbgBzAGkAYgBsAGUAIAByAGUAcQB1AGkAcgBlAHMAIABQAG8AdwBlAHIAUwBoAGUAbABsACAAdgAzAC4AMAAgAG8AcgAgAG4AZQB3AGUAcgAiAH0AJw*", "*cAewAiAGYAYQBpAGwAZQBkACIAOgB0AHIAdQBlACwAIgBtAHMAZwAiADoAIgBBAG4AcwBpAGIAbABlACAAcgBlAHEAdQBpAHIAZQBzACAAUABvAHcAZQByAFMAaABlAGwAbAAgAHYAMwAuADAAIABvAHIAIABuAGUAdwBlAHIAIgB9ACcA*", "*nAHsAIgBmAGEAaQBsAGUAZAAiADoAdAByAHUAZQAsACIAbQBzAGcAIgA6ACIAQQBuAHMAaQBiAGwAZQAgAHIAZQBxAHUAaQByAGUAcwAgAFAAbwB3AGUAcgBTAGgAZQBsAGwAIAB2ADMALgAwACAAbwByACAAbgBlAHcAZQByACIAfQAnA*")))))

name: SIGMA - Csc.EXE Execution Form Potentially Suspicious Parent
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a potentially suspicious parent of "csc.exe", which could be a sign of payload delivery.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\csc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"csc.exe") and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\excel.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\onenote.exe", "*\\outlook.exe", "*\\powerpnt.exe", "*\\winword.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*-Encoded *", "*FromBase64String*"))) or (ParentCommandLine regex~ "([Pp]rogram[Dd]ata|%([Ll]ocal)?[Aa]pp[Dd]ata%|\\[Aa]pp[Dd]ata\\([Ll]ocal([Ll]ow)?|[Rr]oaming))\\[^\\]{1,256}$" or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*")) or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Contacts\\*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Pictures\\*"))) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*")) or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\sdiagnhost.exe" or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\inetsrv\\w3wp.exe")) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\ProgramData\\chocolatey\\choco.exe" or ParentCommandLine:"*\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection*" or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*JwB7ACIAZgBhAGkAbABlAGQAIgA6AHQAcgB1AGUALAAiAG0AcwBnACIAOgAiAEEAbgBzAGkAYgBsAGUAIAByAGUAcQB1AGkAcgBlAHMAIABQAG8AdwBlAHIAUwBoAGUAbABsACAAdgAzAC4AMAAgAG8AcgAgAG4AZQB3AGUAcgAiAH0AJw*", "*cAewAiAGYAYQBpAGwAZQBkACIAOgB0AHIAdQBlACwAIgBtAHMAZwAiADoAIgBBAG4AcwBpAGIAbABlACAAcgBlAHEAdQBpAHIAZQBzACAAUABvAHcAZQByAFMAaABlAGwAbAAgAHYAMwAuADAAIABvAHIAIABuAGUAdwBlAHIAIgB9ACcA*", "*nAHsAIgBmAGEAaQBsAGUAZAAiADoAdAByAHUAZQAsACIAbQBzAGcAIgA6ACIAQQBuAHMAaQBiAGwAZQAgAHIAZQBxAHUAaQByAGUAcwAgAFAAbwB3AGUAcgBTAGgAZQBsAGwAIAB2ADMALgAwACAAbwByACAAbgBlAHcAZQByACIAfQAnA*")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Csi.exe Usage
author: Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community
description: Csi.exe is a signed binary from Microsoft that comes with Visual Studio and provides C# interactive capabilities. It can be used to run C# code from a file passed as a parameter in command line. Early version of this utility provided with Microsoft “Roslyn” Community Technology Preview was named 'rcsi.exe'
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\csi.exe", "*\\rcsi.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("csi.exe", "rcsi.exe"))) and Company:"Microsoft Corporation")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Use of CSharp Interactive Console
author: Michael R. (@nahamike01)
description: Detects the execution of CSharp interactive console by PowerShell
false_positives: Possible depending on environment. Pair with other factors such as net connections, command-line args, etc.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\csi.exe" and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe")) and OriginalFileName:"csi.exe")

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Structure Export Via Csvde.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "csvde.exe" in order to export organizational Active Directory structure.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\csvde.exe" or OriginalFileName:"csvde.exe") and CommandLine:"* -f*") and (not CommandLine:"* -i*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Cookies Session Hijacking
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "-c" flag in order to save cookie data.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and (CommandLine regex~ "\s-c\s" or CommandLine:"*--cookie-jar*"))

name: SIGMA - Curl Web Request With Potential Custom User-Agent
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with a potential custom "User-Agent". Attackers can leverage this to download or exfiltrate data via "curl" to a domain that only accept specific "User-Agent" strings
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and (CommandLine regex~ "\s-H\s" and CommandLine:"*User-Agent:*"))

name: SIGMA - File Download From IP URL Via Curl.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects file downloads directly from IP address URL using curl.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and CommandLine regex~ ":\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}" and CommandLine:"*http*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -O*", "*--remote-name*", "*--output*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*.bat", "*.bat\"", "*.dat", "*.dat\"", "*.dll", "*.dll\"", "*.exe", "*.exe\"", "*.gif", "*.gif\"", "*.hta", "*.hta\"", "*.jpeg", "*.jpeg\"", "*.log", "*.log\"", "*.msi", "*.msi\"", "*.png", "*.png\"", "*.ps1", "*.ps1\"", "*.psm1", "*.psm1\"", "*.vbe", "*.vbe\"", "*.vbs", "*.vbs\"", "*.bat'", "*.dat'", "*.dll'", "*.exe'", "*.gif'", "*.hta'", "*.jpeg'", "*.log'", "*.msi'", "*.png'", "*.ps1'", "*.psm1'", "*.vbe'", "*.vbs'"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Download From IP Via Curl.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious file downloads directly from IP addresses using curl.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and CommandLine regex~ ":\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}" and CommandLine:"*http*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -O*", "*--remote-name*", "*--output*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.bat", "*.bat\"", "*.dat", "*.dat\"", "*.dll", "*.dll\"", "*.exe", "*.exe\"", "*.gif", "*.gif\"", "*.hta", "*.hta\"", "*.jpeg", "*.jpeg\"", "*.log", "*.log\"", "*.msi", "*.msi\"", "*.png", "*.png\"", "*.ps1", "*.ps1\"", "*.psm1", "*.psm1\"", "*.vbe", "*.vbe\"", "*.vbs", "*.vbs\"", "*.bat'", "*.dat'", "*.dll'", "*.exe'", "*.gif'", "*.hta'", "*.jpeg'", "*.log'", "*.msi'", "*.png'", "*.ps1'", "*.psm1'", "*.vbe'", "*.vbs'")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Download From File Sharing Domain Via Curl.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious file download from file sharing domains using curl.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com*", "*anonfiles.com*", "*cdn.discordapp.com*", "*ddns.net*", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com*", "*ghostbin.co*", "*glitch.me*", "*gofile.io*", "*hastebin.com*", "*mediafire.com*", "*mega.nz*", "*onrender.com*", "*pages.dev*", "*paste.ee*", "*pastebin.com*", "*pastebin.pl*", "*pastetext.net*", "*pixeldrain.com*", "*privatlab.com*", "*privatlab.net*", "*send.exploit.in*", "*sendspace.com*", "*storage.googleapis.com*", "*storjshare.io*", "*supabase.co*", "*temp.sh*", "*transfer.sh*", "*trycloudflare.com*", "*ufile.io*", "*w3spaces.com*", "*workers.dev*")) and CommandLine:"*http*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -O*", "*--remote-name*", "*--output*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.ps1", "*.ps1'", "*.ps1\"", "*.dat", "*.dat'", "*.dat\"", "*.msi", "*.msi'", "*.msi\"", "*.bat", "*.bat'", "*.bat\"", "*.exe", "*.exe'", "*.exe\"", "*.vbs", "*.vbs'", "*.vbs\"", "*.vbe", "*.vbe'", "*.vbe\"", "*.hta", "*.hta'", "*.hta\"", "*.dll", "*.dll'", "*.dll\"", "*.psm1", "*.psm1'", "*.psm1\"")))

name: SIGMA - Insecure Transfer Via Curl.EXE
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "--insecure" flag.
false_positives: Access to badly maintained internal or development systems
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and (CommandLine regex~ "\s-k\s" or CommandLine:"*--insecure*"))

name: SIGMA - Insecure Proxy/DOH Transfer Via Curl.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "insecure" flag over proxy or DOH.
false_positives: Access to badly maintained internal or development systems
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*--doh-insecure*", "*--proxy-insecure*")))

name: SIGMA - Local File Read Using Curl.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "curl.exe" with the "file://" protocol handler in order to read local files.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"curl.exe") and CommandLine:"*file:///*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Curl.EXE Download
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious curl process start on Windows and outputs the requested document to a local file
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" or Product:"The curl executable") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*%AppData%*", "*%Public%*", "*%Temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*.dll", "*.gif", "*.jpeg", "*.jpg", "*.png", "*.temp", "*.tmp", "*.txt", "*.vbe", "*.vbs"))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Program Files\\Git\\usr\\bin\\sh.exe" and Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Git\\mingw64\\bin\\curl.exe" and (CommandLine:"*--silent --show-error --output *" and CommandLine:"*gfw-httpget-*" and CommandLine:"*AppData*"))))

name: SIGMA - ManageEngine Endpoint Central Dctask64.EXE Potential Abuse
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of "dctask64.exe", a signed binary by ZOHO Corporation part of ManageEngine Endpoint Central.
This binary can be abused for DLL injection, arbitrary command and process execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dctask64.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=6834B1B94E49701D77CCB3C0895E1AFD*", "*IMPHASH=1BB6F93B129F398C7C4A76BB97450BBA*", "*IMPHASH=FAA2AC19875FADE461C8D89DCF2710A3*", "*IMPHASH=F1039CED4B91572AB7847D26032E6BBF*"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("* executecmd64 *", "* invokeexe *", "* injectDll *")))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of Defaultpack.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects uncommon child processes of "DefaultPack.EXE" binary as a proxy to launch other programs
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\DefaultPack.exe"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Defender Threat Severity Default Action Set to 'Allow' or 'NoAction'
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description:
Detects the use of PowerShell to execute the 'Set-MpPreference' cmdlet to configure Windows Defender's threat severity default action to 'Allow' (value '6') or 'NoAction' (value '9').
This is a highly suspicious configuration change that effectively disables Defender's ability to automatically mitigate threats of a certain severity level.
An attacker might use this technique via the command line to bypass defenses before executing payloads.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*Set-MpPreference*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-LowThreatDefaultAction*", "*-ModerateThreatDefaultAction*", "*-HighThreatDefaultAction*", "*-SevereThreatDefaultAction*", "*-ltdefac *", "*-mtdefac *", "*-htdefac *", "*-stdefac *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Allow*", "*6*", "*NoAction*", "*9*")))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Context Menu Removed
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description:
Detects the use of reg.exe or PowerShell to delete the Windows Defender context menu handler registry keys.
This action removes the "Scan with Microsoft Defender" option from the right-click menu for files, directories, and drives.
Attackers may use this technique to hinder manual, on-demand scans and reduce the visibility of the security product.
false_positives: May be part of a system customization or "debloating" script, but this is highly unusual in a managed corporate environment.
