query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\reg.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("powershell_ise.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "reg.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*del*", "*Remove-Item*", "*ri *")) and CommandLine:"*\\shellex\\ContextMenuHandlers\\EPP*")

name: SIGMA - Remote File Download Via Desktopimgdownldr Utility
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects the desktopimgdownldr utility being used to download a remote file. An adversary may use desktopimgdownldr to download arbitrary files as an alternative to certutil.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\desktopimgdownldr.exe" and ParentImage:"*\\desktopimgdownldr.exe" and CommandLine:"*/lockscreenurl:http*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Desktopimgdownldr Command
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious Microsoft desktopimgdownldr execution with parameters used to download files from the Internet
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"* /lockscreenurl:*" and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*.jpg*", "*.jpeg*", "*.png*")))) or (CommandLine:"*reg delete*" and CommandLine:"*\\PersonalizationCSP*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential DLL Sideloading Via DeviceEnroller.EXE
author: @gott_cyber
description:
Detects the use of the PhoneDeepLink parameter to potentially sideload a DLL file that does not exist. This non-existent DLL file is named "ShellChromeAPI.dll".
Adversaries can drop their own renamed DLL and execute it via DeviceEnroller.exe using this parameter
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\deviceenroller.exe" or OriginalFileName:"deviceenroller.exe") and CommandLine:"*/PhoneDeepLink*")

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary MSI Download Via Devinit.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a certain command line flag combination used by "devinit.exe", which can be abused as a LOLBIN to download arbitrary MSI packages on a Windows system
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* -t msi-install *" and CommandLine:"* -i http*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of ClickOnce Application
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of a ClickOnce deployment application
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Apps\\2.0\\*" and (Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\explorer.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\werfault.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")))

name: SIGMA - DirLister Execution
author: frack113
description: Detect the usage of "DirLister.exe" a utility for quickly listing folder or drive contents. It was seen used by BlackCat ransomware to create a list of accessible directories and files.
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"DirLister.exe" or Image:"*\\dirlister.exe")

name: SIGMA - System Information Discovery via Registry Queries
author: lazarg
description: Detects attempts to query system information directly from the Windows Registry.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" and CommandLine:"*query*" and CommandLine like~ ("*-v*", "*/v*", "*–v*", "*—v*", "*―v*")) or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Get-ItemPropertyValue*", "*gpv*")))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\TimeZoneInformation*", "*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\Tcpip\\Parameters\\Interfaces*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Uninstall*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender*", "*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Schedule\\TaskCache\\Tasks*")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of DiskShadow.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "Diskshadow.exe". This could be an attempt to bypass parent/child relationship detection or application whitelisting rules.
false_positives: False postitve can occur in cases where admin scripts levreage the "exec" flag to execute applications
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\diskshadow.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Diskshadow Script Mode - Uncommon Script Extension Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode to execute an script with a potentially uncommon extension.
Initial baselining of the allowed extension list is required.
false_positives: False postitve might occur with legitimate or uncommon extensions used internally. Initial baseline is required.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName:"diskshadow.exe" or Image:"*\\diskshadow.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-s *", "*/s *", "*–s *", "*—s *", "*―s *")) and (not CommandLine:"*.txt*"))

name: SIGMA - Diskshadow Script Mode - Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "Diskshadow.exe" in script mode using the "/s" flag where the script is located in a potentially suspicious location.
false_positives: False positives may occur if you execute the script from one of the paths mentioned in the rule. Apply additional filters that fits your org needs.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"diskshadow.exe" or Image:"*\\diskshadow.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-s *", "*/s *", "*–s *", "*—s *", "*―s *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\ProgramData\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*")))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Web Access Feature Enabled Via DISM
author: Michael Haag
description: Detects the use of DISM to enable the PowerShell Web Access feature, which could be used for remote access and potential abuse
false_positives: Legitimate PowerShell Web Access installations by administrators
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dism.exe" or OriginalFileName:"DISM.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*WindowsPowerShellWebAccess*" and CommandLine:"*/online*" and CommandLine:"*/enable-feature*"))

name: SIGMA - Dism Remove Online Package
author: frack113
description: Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool. DISM is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\DismHost.exe" and (ParentCommandLine:"*/Online*" and ParentCommandLine:"*/Disable-Feature*")) or (Image:"*\\Dism.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/Online*" and CommandLine:"*/Disable-Feature*")))

name: SIGMA - Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a "dllhost" process spawning with no commandline arguments which is very rare to happen and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dllhost.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("dllhost.exe", "dllhost"))) and ?CommandLine != null)

name: SIGMA - DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of VMware Xfer utility (VMwareXferlogs.exe) from the non-default directory which may be an attempt to sideload arbitrary DLL
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\VMwareXferlogs.exe" and (not Image:"C:\\Program Files\\VMware\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE
author: @gott_cyber
description: Detects an attempt to leverage dnscmd.exe to enumerate the DNS zones of a domain. DNS zones used to host the DNS records for a particular domain.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\dnscmd.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/enumrecords*", "*/enumzones*", "*/ZonePrint*", "*/info*")))

name: SIGMA - New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the installation of a DNS plugin DLL via ServerLevelPluginDll parameter in registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DNS server (restart required)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\dnscmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/config*" and CommandLine:"*/serverlevelplugindll*"))

name: SIGMA - DNS Exfiltration and Tunneling Tools Execution
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Well-known DNS Exfiltration tools execution

subtechnique.name: Exfiltration Over Symmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol

query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*\\iodine.exe", "*\\dnscat2*"))

name: SIGMA - Unusual Child Process of dns.exe
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects an unexpected process spawning from dns.exe which may indicate activity related to remote code execution or other forms of exploitation as seen in CVE-2020-1350 (SigRed)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\dns.exe" and (not Image:"*\\conhost.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description:
Detects the execution of Dnx.EXE. The Dnx utility allows for the execution of C# code.
Attackers might abuse this in order to bypass application whitelisting.
false_positives: Legitimate use of dnx.exe by legitimate user
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\dnx.exe"

name: SIGMA - Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "dotnet-dump" with the "collect" flag. The execution could indicate potential process dumping of critical processes such as LSASS.
false_positives: Process dumping is the expected behavior of the tool. So false positives are expected in legitimate usage. The PID/Process Name of the process being dumped needs to be investigated
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dotnet-dump.exe" or OriginalFileName:"dotnet-dump.dll") and CommandLine:"*collect*")

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution Via Dotnet.EXE
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description: Detects execution of arbitrary DLLs or unsigned code via a ".csproj" files via Dotnet.EXE.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\dotnet.exe" or OriginalFileName:".NET Host") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.csproj", "*.csproj\"", "*.dll", "*.dll\"", "*.csproj'", "*.dll'"))) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Notepad++\\notepad++.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Notepad++\\notepad++.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*C:\\ProgramData\\CSScriptNpp\\*" and CommandLine:"*-cscs_path:*" and CommandLine:"*\\cs-script\\cscs.dll*"))))

name: SIGMA - Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE
author: Jimmy Bayne (@bohops)
description: Detects commandline arguments for executing a child process via dotnet-trace.exe
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the utility in order to debug and trace a program.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dotnet-trace.exe" or OriginalFileName:"dotnet-trace.dll") and (CommandLine:"*-- *" and CommandLine:"*collect*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers
false_positives: Legitimate usage by some scripts might trigger this as well
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*driverquery.exe" or OriginalFileName:"drvqry.exe") and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - DriverQuery.EXE Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility. Which can be used to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers
false_positives: Legitimate use by third party tools in order to investigate installed drivers
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*driverquery.exe" or OriginalFileName:"drvqry.exe") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Dsacls to grant over permissive permissions
false_positives: Legitimate administrators granting over permissive permissions to users
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dsacls.exe" or OriginalFileName:"DSACLS.EXE") and CommandLine:"* /G *" and (CommandLine like~ ("*GR*", "*GE*", "*GW*", "*GA*", "*WP*", "*WD*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects possible password spraying attempts using Dsacls
false_positives: Legitimate use of dsacls to bind to an LDAP session
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dsacls.exe" or OriginalFileName:"DSACLS.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*/user:*" and CommandLine:"*/passwd:*"))

name: SIGMA - Domain Trust Discovery Via Dsquery
author: E.M. Anhaus, Tony Lambert, oscd.community, omkar72
description: Detects execution of "dsquery.exe" for domain trust discovery
false_positives: Legitimate use of the utilities by legitimate user for legitimate reason
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dsquery.exe" or OriginalFileName:"dsquery.exe") and CommandLine:"*trustedDomain*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Kernel Dump Using Dtrace
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious way to dump the kernel on Windows systems using dtrace.exe, which is available on Windows systems since Windows 10 19H1
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\dtrace.exe" and CommandLine:"*lkd(0)*") or (CommandLine:"*syscall:::return*" and CommandLine:"*lkd(*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when a user is potentially trying to bypass the Windows Defender AV by renaming a tool to dump64.exe and placing it in the Visual Studio folder.
Currently the rule is covering only usage of procdump but other utilities can be added in order to increase coverage.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:":\\Program Files*" and Image:"*\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\*" and Image:"*\\dump64.exe") and (OriginalFileName:"procdump" or (CommandLine like~ ("* -ma *", "* -mp *"))))

name: SIGMA - DumpMinitool Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of "DumpMinitool.exe" a tool that allows the dump of process memory via the use of the "MiniDumpWriteDump"
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\DumpMinitool.exe", "*\\DumpMinitool.x86.exe", "*\\DumpMinitool.arm64.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("DumpMinitool.exe", "DumpMinitool.x86.exe", "DumpMinitool.arm64.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("* Full*", "* Mini*", "* WithHeap*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious DumpMinitool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious ways to use the "DumpMinitool.exe" binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\DumpMinitool.exe", "*\\DumpMinitool.x86.exe", "*\\DumpMinitool.arm64.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("DumpMinitool.exe", "DumpMinitool.x86.exe", "DumpMinitool.arm64.exe"))) and ((not (Image like~ ("*\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\*", "*\\Extensions\\*"))) or CommandLine:"*.txt*" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* Full*", "* Mini*", "* WithHeap*")) and (not CommandLine:"*--dumpType*"))))

name: SIGMA - New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "DXCap.EXE" with the "-c" flag, which allows a user to launch any arbitrary binary or windows package through DXCap itself. This can be abused to potentially bypass application whitelisting.
false_positives: Legitimate execution of dxcap.exe by legitimate user
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\DXCap.exe" or OriginalFileName:"DXCap.exe") and CommandLine:"* -c *")

name: SIGMA - Esentutl Gather Credentials
author: sam0x90
description: Conti recommendation to its affiliates to use esentutl to access NTDS dumped file. Trickbot also uses this utilities to get MSEdge info via its module pwgrab.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*esentutl*" and CommandLine:"* /p*")

name: SIGMA - Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying
false_positives: Copying sensitive files for legitimate use (eg. backup) or forensic investigation by legitimate incident responder or forensic investigator.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\esentutl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\esentutl.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*vss*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -m *", "* /m *", "* –m *", "* —m *", "* ―m *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -y *", "* /y *", "* –y *", "* —y *", "* ―y *"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("*\\config\\RegBack\\sam*", "*\\config\\RegBack\\security*", "*\\config\\RegBack\\system*", "*\\config\\sam*", "*\\config\\security*", "*\\config\\system *", "*\\repair\\sam*", "*\\repair\\security*", "*\\repair\\system*", "*\\windows\\ntds\\ntds.dit*")))

name: SIGMA - Esentutl Steals Browser Information
author: frack113
description: One way Qbot steals sensitive information is by extracting browser data from Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge by using the built-in utility esentutl.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\esentutl.exe" or OriginalFileName:"esentutl.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-r*", "*/r*", "*–r*", "*—r*", "*―r*") and CommandLine:"*\\Windows\\WebCache*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Event Viewer Child Process
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes of "eventvwr.exe" which might indicate a UAC bypass attempt
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\eventvwr.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\mmc.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\WerFault.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WerFault.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Process
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects attempts to disable security event logging by adding the `MiniNt` registry key.
This key is used to disable the Windows Event Log service, which collects and stores event logs from the operating system and applications.
Adversaries may want to disable this service to prevent logging of security events that could be used to detect their activities.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt*")) or (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*New-Item *", "*ni *")) and CommandLine:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion
author: Bhabesh Raj, X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the expansion or decompression of cabinet files from potentially suspicious or uncommon locations, e.g. seen in Iranian MeteorExpress related attacks
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\expand.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("*-F:*", "*/F:*", "*–F:*", "*—F:*", "*―F:*")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\ProgramData*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\Admin$\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\C$\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*")) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Contacts\\*"))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dell\\UpdateService\\ServiceShell.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\ProgramData\\Dell\\UpdateService\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Explorer Process Tree Break
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @gott_cyber
description:
Detects a command line process that uses explorer.exe to launch arbitrary commands or binaries,
which is similar to cmd.exe /c, only it breaks the process tree and makes its parent a new instance of explorer spawning from "svchost"
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*/factory,{75dff2b7-6936-4c06-a8bb-676a7b00b24b}*" or (CommandLine like~ ("*explorer.exe*") and CommandLine like~ ("* -root,*", "* /root,*", "* –root,*", "* —root,*", "* ―root,*")))

name: SIGMA - File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell
author: @Kostastsale
description: Detects the initial execution of "cmd.exe" which spawns "explorer.exe" with the appropriate command line arguments for opening the "My Computer" folder.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and Image:"*\\explorer.exe" and CommandLine:"*shell:mycomputerfolder*")

name: SIGMA - Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious starts of explorer.exe that use the /NOUACCHECK flag that allows to run all sub processes of that newly started explorer.exe without any UAC checks
false_positives: Unknown how many legitimate software products use that method
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\explorer.exe" and CommandLine:"*/NOUACCHECK*") and (not (ParentCommandLine:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p -s Schedule" or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe")))

name: SIGMA - FileFix - Suspicious Child Process from Browser File Upload Abuse
author: 0xFustang
description:
Detects potentially suspicious subprocesses such as LOLBINs spawned by web browsers. This activity could be associated with the "FileFix" social engineering technique,
where users are tricked into launching the file explorer via a browser-based phishing page and pasting malicious commands into the address bar.
The technique abuses clipboard manipulation and disguises command execution as benign file path access, resulting in covert execution of system utilities.
false_positives: Legitimate use of PowerShell or other utilities launched from browser extensions or automation tools
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\firefox.exe", "*\\msedge.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe")) and CommandLine:"*#*")

name: SIGMA - Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE
author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "findstr" with specific flags and a remote share path. This specific set of CLI flags would allow "findstr" to download the content of the file located on the remote share as described in the LOLBAS entry.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*findstr*" or Image:"*findstr.exe" or OriginalFileName:"FINDSTR.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -v *", "* /v *", "* –v *", "* —v *", "* ―v *") and CommandLine like~ ("* -l *", "* /l *", "* –l *", "* —l *", "* ―l *") and CommandLine:"*\\\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Findstr GPP Passwords
author: frack113
description: Look for the encrypted cpassword value within Group Policy Preference files on the Domain Controller. This value can be decrypted with gpp-decrypt.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and (CommandLine:"*cpassword*" and CommandLine:"*\\sysvol\\*" and CommandLine:"*.xml*"))

name: SIGMA - Findstr Launching .lnk File
author: Trent Liffick
description: Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.lnk", "*.lnk\"", "*.lnk'")))

name: SIGMA - LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects findstring commands that include the keyword lsass, which indicates recon actviity for the LSASS process PID
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and CommandLine:"*lsass*") or (CommandLine like~ ("* -i \"lsass*", "* /i \"lsass*", "* –i \"lsass*", "* —i \"lsass*", "* ―i \"lsass*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -i lsass.exe*", "* /i lsass.exe*", "* –i lsass.exe*", "* —i lsass.exe*", "* ―i lsass.exe*") or CommandLine like~ ("*findstr \"lsass*") or CommandLine like~ ("*findstr lsass*") or CommandLine like~ ("*findstr.exe \"lsass*") or CommandLine like~ ("*findstr.exe lsass*")))

name: SIGMA - Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of findstr with the "EVERYONE" or "BUILTIN" keywords.
This was seen being used in combination with "icacls" and other utilities to spot misconfigured files or folders permissions.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\"Everyone\"*", "*'Everyone'*", "*\"BUILTIN\\\"*", "*'BUILTIN\\'*"))) or (CommandLine:"*icacls *" and CommandLine:"*findstr *" and CommandLine:"*Everyone*"))

name: SIGMA - Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects the execution of a potential recon command where the results are piped to "findstr". This is meant to trigger on inline calls of "cmd.exe" via the "/c" or "/k" for example.
Attackers often time use this technique to extract specific information they require in their reconnaissance phase.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*ipconfig*|*find*", "*net*|*find*", "*netstat*|*find*", "*ping*|*find*", "*systeminfo*|*find*", "*tasklist*|*find*", "*whoami*|*find*")) and (not (CommandLine:"*cmd.exe /c TASKLIST /V |*" and CommandLine:"*FIND /I*" and CommandLine:"*\\xampp\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\catalina_start.bat*")))

name: SIGMA - Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects execution of "findstr" to search for common names of security tools. Attackers often pipe the results of recon commands such as "tasklist" or "whoami" to "findstr" in order to filter out the results.
This detection focuses on the keywords that the attacker might use as a filter.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("* avira", "* avira\"", "* cb", "* cb\"", "* cylance", "* cylance\"", "* defender", "* defender\"", "* kaspersky", "* kaspersky\"", "* kes", "* kes\"", "* mc", "* mc\"", "* sec", "* sec\"", "* sentinel", "* sentinel\"", "* symantec", "* symantec\"", "* virus", "* virus\"")))

name: SIGMA - Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE
author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of findstr with the "s" and "i" flags for a "subfolder" and "insensitive" search respectively. Attackers sometimes leverage this built-in utility to search the system for interesting files or filter through results of commands.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*findstr*" or Image:"*findstr.exe" or OriginalFileName:"FINDSTR.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -s *", "* /s *", "* –s *", "* —s *", "* ―s *") and CommandLine like~ ("* -i *", "* /i *", "* –i *", "* —i *", "* ―i *")))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of "findstr" with the argument "385201". Which could indicate potential discovery of an installed Sysinternals Sysmon service using the default driver altitude (even if the name is changed).
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\find.exe", "*\\findstr.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("FIND.EXE", "FINDSTR.EXE"))) and CommandLine:"* 385201*")

name: SIGMA - Finger.EXE Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community
description:
Detects execution of the "finger.exe" utility.
Finger.EXE or "TCPIP Finger Command" is an old utility that is still present on modern Windows installation. It Displays information about users on a specified remote computer (typically a UNIX computer) that is running the finger service or daemon.
Due to the old nature of this utility and the rareness of machines having the finger service. Any execution of "finger.exe" can be considered "suspicious" and worth investigating.
false_positives: Admin activity (unclear what they do nowadays with finger.exe)
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"finger.exe" or Image:"*\\finger.exe")

name: SIGMA - Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect filter driver unloading activity via fltmc.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\fltMC.exe" or OriginalFileName:"fltMC.exe") and CommandLine:"*unload*") and (not (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\endpoint-protection-installer-x64.tmp" and (CommandLine like~ ("*unload rtp_filesystem_filter", "*unload rtp_filter"))) or (ParentImage:"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\ManageEngine\\uems_agent\\bin\\dcfaservice64.exe" and CommandLine:"*unload DFMFilter"))))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
author: Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community
description: Detects possible Sysmon filter driver unloaded via fltmc.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\fltMC.exe" or OriginalFileName:"fltMC.exe") and (CommandLine:"*unload*" and CommandLine:"*sysmon*"))

name: SIGMA - Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati
description: Detects the execution of "forfiles" from a non-default location, in order to potentially spawn a custom "cmd.exe" from the current working directory.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentCommandLine like~ ("*.exe", "*.exe\"")) and Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"/c echo \"*") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\forfiles.exe" and (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")) and Image:"*\\cmd.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Forfiles Command Execution
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic, E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description:
Detects the execution of "forfiles" with the "/c" flag.
While this is an expected behavior of the tool, it can be abused in order to proxy execution through it with any binary.
Can be used to bypass application whitelisting.
false_positives: Legitimate use via a batch script or by an administrator.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\forfiles.exe" or OriginalFileName:"forfiles.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *"))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon FileSystem Load Attempt By Format.com
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of format.com with an uncommon filesystem selection that could indicate a defense evasion activity in which "format.com" is used to load malicious DLL files or other programs.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\format.com" and CommandLine:"*/fs:*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*/fs:exFAT*", "*/fs:FAT*", "*/fs:NTFS*", "*/fs:ReFS*", "*/fs:UDF*"))))

name: SIGMA - Use of FSharp Interpreters
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description:
Detects the execution of FSharp Interpreters "FsiAnyCpu.exe" and "FSi.exe"
Both can be used for AWL bypass and to execute F# code via scripts or inline.
false_positives: Legitimate use by a software developer.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\fsi.exe", "*\\fsianycpu.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("fsi.exe", "fsianycpu.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Fsutil Drive Enumeration
author: Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io'
description: Attackers may leverage fsutil to enumerated connected drives.
false_positives: Certain software or administrative tasks may trigger false positives.
references: Turla has used fsutil fsinfo drives to list connected drives.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\fsutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"fsutil.exe") and CommandLine:"*drives*")

name: SIGMA - Fsutil Behavior Set SymlinkEvaluation
author: frack113
description:
A symbolic link is a type of file that contains a reference to another file.
This is probably done to make sure that the ransomware is able to follow shortcuts on the machine in order to find the original file to encrypt
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\fsutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"fsutil.exe") and (CommandLine:"*behavior *" and CommandLine:"*set *" and CommandLine:"*SymlinkEvaluation*"))

name: SIGMA - Fsutil Suspicious Invocation
author: Ecco, E.M. Anhaus, oscd.community
description:
Detects suspicious parameters of fsutil (deleting USN journal, configuring it with small size, etc).
Might be used by ransomwares during the attack (seen by NotPetya and others).
false_positives: Scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\fsutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"fsutil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*deletejournal*", "*createjournal*", "*setZeroData*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Via FTP.EXE
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
description: Detects execution of "ftp.exe" script with the "-s" or "/s" flag and any child processes ran by "ftp.exe".
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\ftp.exe" or ((Image:"*\\ftp.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ftp.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-s:*", "*/s:*", "*–s:*", "*—s:*", "*―s:*")))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via GfxDownloadWrapper.EXE
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
description: Detects execution of GfxDownloadWrapper.exe with a URL as an argument to download file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\GfxDownloadWrapper.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*"))) and (not CommandLine:"*https://gameplayapi.intel.com/*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Git Clone
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "git" in order to clone a remote repository that contain suspicious keywords which might be suspicious
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\git.exe", "*\\git-remote-https.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"git.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* clone *", "*git-remote-https *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*exploit*", "*Vulns*", "*vulnerability*", "*RemoteCodeExecution*", "*Invoke-*", "*CVE-*", "*poc-*", "*ProofOfConcept*", "*proxyshell*", "*log4shell*", "*eternalblue*", "*eternal-blue*", "*MS17-*")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious GoogleUpdate Child Process
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "GoogleUpdate.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\GoogleUpdate.exe" and (not ((Image:"*\\Google*" or (Image like~ ("*\\setup.exe", "*chrome_updater.exe", "*chrome_installer.exe"))) or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - File Decryption Using Gpg4win
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to decrypt files
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\gpg.exe", "*\\gpg2.exe")) or Description:"GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool") and (CommandLine:"* -d *" and CommandLine:"*passphrase*"))

name: SIGMA - File Encryption Using Gpg4win
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt files
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\gpg.exe", "*\\gpg2.exe")) or Description:"GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool") and (CommandLine:"* -c *" and CommandLine:"*passphrase*"))

name: SIGMA - Portable Gpg.EXE Execution
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "gpg.exe" from uncommon location. Often used by ransomware and loaders to decrypt/encrypt data.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\gpg.exe", "*\\gpg2.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"gpg.exe" or Description:"GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool") and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\GNU\\GnuPG\\bin\\*", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\GnuPG VS-Desktop\\*", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\GnuPG\\bin\\*", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Gpg4win\\bin\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - File Encryption/Decryption Via Gpg4win From Suspicious Locations
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Gpg4win to encrypt/decrypt files located in potentially suspicious locations.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\gpg.exe", "*\\gpg2.exe")) or Product:"GNU Privacy Guard (GnuPG)" or Description:"GnuPG’s OpenPGP tool") and CommandLine:"*-passphrase*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Gpresult Display Group Policy Information
author: frack113
description: Detects cases in which a user uses the built-in Windows utility gpresult to display the Resultant Set of Policy (RSoP) information
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\gpresult.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/z*", "*/v*")))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary Binary Execution Using GUP Utility
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) to launch other commands or executables
false_positives: Other parent binaries using GUP not currently identified
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\gup.exe" and Image:"*\\explorer.exe") and (not ((Image:"*\\explorer.exe" and CommandLine:"*\\Notepad++\\notepad++.exe*") or ParentImage:"*\\Notepad++\\updater\\*" or (?CommandLine == null))))

name: SIGMA - File Download Using Notepad++ GUP Utility
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater (gup) from a process other than Notepad++ to download files.
false_positives: Other parent processes other than notepad++ using GUP that are not currently identified
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\GUP.exe" or OriginalFileName:"gup.exe") and (CommandLine:"* -unzipTo *" and CommandLine:"*http*")) and (not ParentImage:"*\\notepad++.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious GUP Usage
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the Notepad++ updater in a suspicious directory, which is often used in DLL side-loading attacks
false_positives: Execution of tools named GUP.exe and located in folders different than Notepad++\updater
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\GUP.exe" and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\Program Files\\Notepad++\\updater\\GUP.exe", "*\\Program Files (x86)\\Notepad++\\updater\\GUP.exe")) or (Image:"*\\Users\\*" and (Image like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Notepad++\\updater\\GUP.exe", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Notepad++\\updater\\GUP.exe"))))))

name: SIGMA - HH.EXE Execution
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Dan Beavin), oscd.community
description: Detects the execution of "hh.exe" to open ".chm" files.
false_positives: False positives are expected with legitimate ".CHM"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"HH.exe" or Image:"*\\hh.exe") and CommandLine:"*.chm*")

name: SIGMA - Remote CHM File Download/Execution Via HH.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of "hh.exe" to execute/download remotely hosted ".chm" files.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"HH.exe" or Image:"*\\hh.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*", "*\\\\*")))

name: SIGMA - HTML Help HH.EXE Suspicious Child Process
author: Maxim Pavlunin, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious child process of a Microsoft HTML Help (HH.exe)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\hh.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\CertReq.exe", "*\\CertUtil.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\installutil.exe", "*\\MSbuild.exe", "*\\MSHTA.EXE", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious HH.EXE Execution
author: Maxim Pavlunin
description: Detects a suspicious execution of a Microsoft HTML Help (HH.exe)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"HH.exe" or Image:"*\\hh.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.application*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Content.Outlook\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - ADCSPwn Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command line parameters used by ADCSPwn, a tool to escalate privileges in an active directory network by coercing authenticate from machine accounts and relaying to the certificate service
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* --adcs *" and CommandLine:"* --port *")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Bloodhound/Sharphound Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command line parameters used by Bloodhound and Sharphound hack tools
false_positives: Other programs that use these command line option and accepts an 'All' parameter
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"*SharpHound*" or Description:"*SharpHound*" or (Company like~ ("*SpecterOps*", "*evil corp*")) or (Image like~ ("*\\Bloodhound.exe*", "*\\SharpHound.exe*"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("* -CollectionMethod All *", "* --CollectionMethods Session *", "* --Loop --Loopduration *", "* --PortScanTimeout *", "*.exe -c All -d *", "*Invoke-Bloodhound*", "*Get-BloodHoundData*")) or (CommandLine:"* -JsonFolder *" and CommandLine:"* -ZipFileName *") or (CommandLine:"* DCOnly *" and CommandLine:"* --NoSaveCache *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - F-Secure C3 Load by Rundll32
author: Alfie Champion (ajpc500)
description: F-Secure C3 produces DLLs with a default exported StartNodeRelay function.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe*" and CommandLine:"*.dll*" and CommandLine:"*StartNodeRelay*")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Certify Execution
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Certify a tool for Active Directory certificate abuse based on PE metadata characteristics and common command line arguments.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\Certify.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Certify.exe" or Description:"*Certify*") or ((CommandLine like~ ("*.exe cas *", "*.exe find *", "*.exe pkiobjects *", "*.exe request *", "*.exe download *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* /vulnerable*", "* /template:*", "* /altname:*", "* /domain:*", "* /path:*", "* /ca:*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Certipy Execution
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), Sittikorn Sangrattanapitak
description: Detects Certipy execution, a tool for Active Directory Certificate Services enumeration and abuse based on PE metadata characteristics and common command line arguments.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\Certipy.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Certipy.exe" or Description:"*Certipy*") or ((CommandLine like~ ("* account *", "* auth *", "* cert *", "* find *", "* forge *", "* ptt *", "* relay *", "* req *", "* shadow *", "* template *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -bloodhound*", "* -ca-pfx *", "* -dc-ip *", "* -kirbi*", "* -old-bloodhound*", "* -pfx *", "* -target*", "* -template*", "* -username *", "* -vulnerable*", "*auth -pfx*", "*shadow auto*", "*shadow list*"))))

name: SIGMA - Operator Bloopers Cobalt Strike Commands
author: _pete_0, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects use of Cobalt Strike commands accidentally entered in the CMD shell
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe" or Image:"*\\cmd.exe") and ((CommandLine like~ ("cmd *", "cmd.exe*", "c:\\windows\\system32\\cmd.exe*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*psinject*", "*spawnas*", "*make_token*", "*remote-exec*", "*rev2self*", "*dcsync*", "*logonpasswords*", "*execute-assembly*", "*getsystem*"))))

name: SIGMA - Operator Bloopers Cobalt Strike Modules
author: _pete_0, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects Cobalt Strike module/commands accidentally entered in CMD shell
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe" or Image:"*\\cmd.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-UserHunter*", "*Invoke-ShareFinder*", "*Invoke-Kerberoast*", "*Invoke-SMBAutoBrute*", "*Invoke-Nightmare*", "*zerologon*", "*av_query*")))

name: SIGMA - CobaltStrike Load by Rundll32
author: Wojciech Lesicki
description: Rundll32 can be use by Cobalt Strike with StartW function to load DLLs from the command line.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE" or (CommandLine like~ ("*rundll32.exe*", "*rundll32 *"))) and (CommandLine:"*.dll*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* StartW", "*,StartW"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential CobaltStrike Process Patterns
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential process patterns related to Cobalt Strike beacon activity
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*cmd.exe /C whoami" and ParentImage:"C:\\Temp\\*") or ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\runonce.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*cmd.exe /c echo*" and CommandLine:"*> \\\\.\\pipe*")) or ((ParentCommandLine:"*cmd.exe /C echo*" and ParentCommandLine:"* > \\\\.\\pipe*") and CommandLine:"*conhost.exe 0xffffffff -ForceV1") or (ParentCommandLine:"*/C whoami" and CommandLine:"*conhost.exe 0xffffffff -ForceV1"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CoercedPotato Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of CoercedPotato, a tool for privilege escalation
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\CoercedPotato.exe" or CommandLine:"* --exploitId *" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=A75D7669DB6B2E107A44C4057FF7F7D6*", "*IMPHASH=F91624350E2C678C5DCBE5E1F24E22C9*", "*IMPHASH=14C81850A079A87E83D50CA41C709A15*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Covenant PowerShell Launcher
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects suspicious command lines used in Covenant luanchers
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*-Sta*" and CommandLine:"*-Nop*" and CommandLine:"*-Window*" and CommandLine:"*Hidden*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-Command*", "*-EncodedCommand*"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("*sv o (New-Object IO.MemorySteam);sv d *", "*mshta file.hta*", "*GruntHTTP*", "*-EncodedCommand cwB2ACAAbwAgA*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CrackMapExec Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This rule detect common flag combinations used by CrackMapExec in order to detect its use even if the binary has been replaced.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\crackmapexec.exe" or CommandLine:"* -M pe_inject *" or (CommandLine:"* --local-auth*" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -x *") or (CommandLine:"* --local-auth*" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -p *" and CommandLine:"* -H 'NTHASH'*") or (CommandLine:"* mssql *" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -p *" and CommandLine:"* -M *" and CommandLine:"* -d *") or (CommandLine:"* smb *" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -H *" and CommandLine:"* -M *" and CommandLine:"* -o *") or (CommandLine:"* smb *" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -p *" and CommandLine:"* --local-auth*")) or ((CommandLine:"* --local-auth*" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -p *") and (CommandLine:"* 10.*" and CommandLine:"* 192.168.*" and CommandLine:"*/24 *")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CrackMapExec Execution Patterns
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects various execution patterns of the CrackMapExec pentesting framework
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd.exe /Q /c * 1> \\\\*\\*\\* 2>&1*", "*cmd.exe /C * > \\\\*\\*\\* 2>&1*", "*cmd.exe /C * > *\\Temp\\* 2>&1*", "*powershell.exe -exec bypass -noni -nop -w 1 -C \"*", "*powershell.exe -noni -nop -w 1 -enc *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CrackMapExec Process Patterns
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process patterns found in logs when CrackMapExec is used
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*tasklist /fi *" and CommandLine:"*Imagename eq lsass.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd.exe /c *", "*cmd.exe /r *", "*cmd.exe /k *", "*cmd /c *", "*cmd /r *", "*cmd /k *")) and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*"))) or (CommandLine:"*do rundll32.exe C:\\windows\\System32\\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump*" and CommandLine:"*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"* full*" and CommandLine:"*%%B*") or (CommandLine:"*tasklist /v /fo csv*" and CommandLine:"*findstr /i \"lsass\"*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CrackMapExec PowerShell Obfuscation
author: Thomas Patzke
description: The CrachMapExec pentesting framework implements a PowerShell obfuscation with some static strings detected by this rule.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*join*split*", "*( $ShellId[1]+$ShellId[13]+'x')*", "*( $PSHome[*]+$PSHOME[*]+*", "*( $env:Public[13]+$env:Public[5]+'x')*", "*( $env:ComSpec[4,*,25]-Join'')*", "*[1,3]+'x'-Join'')*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - CreateMiniDump Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of CreateMiniDump hack tool used to dump the LSASS process memory for credential extraction on the attacker's machine
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\CreateMiniDump.exe" or Hashes:"*IMPHASH=4a07f944a83e8a7c2525efa35dd30e2f*")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - DInjector PowerShell Cradle Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the Dinject PowerShell cradle based on the specific flags
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* /am51*" and CommandLine:"* /password*")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Doppelanger LSASS Dumper Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the Doppelanger hacktool which is used to dump LSASS memory via process cloning while evading common detection methods
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Doppelganger.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=AB94D5217896ADCD765A06B2D52F0AEB*", "*IMPHASH=65F0EA61156EE0C2A35421926F0C7F78*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Dumpert Process Dumper Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of Dumpert process dumper, which dumps the lsass.exe process memory
query: any where EventID:1 and (Hashes:"*MD5=09D278F9DE118EF09163C6140255C690*" or CommandLine:"*Dumpert.dll*")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - EDRSilencer Execution
author: @gott_cyber
description: Detects the execution of EDRSilencer, a tool that leverages Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) to block Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents from reporting security events to the server based on PE metadata information.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\EDRSilencer.exe" or OriginalFileName:"EDRSilencer.exe" or Description:"*EDRSilencer*")

name: SIGMA - Hacktool - EDR-Freeze Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of EDR-Freeze, a tool that exploits the MiniDumpWriteDump function and WerFaultSecure.exe to suspend EDR and Antivirus processes on Windows.
EDR-Freeze leverages a race-condition attack to put security processes into a dormant state by suspending WerFaultSecure at the moment it freezes the target process.
This technique does not require kernel-level exploits or BYOVD, but instead abuses user-mode functionality to temporarily disable monitoring by EDR or Antimalware solutions.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\EDR-Freeze*" and Image:"*.exe") or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=1195F7935954A2CD09157390C33F8E8C*", "*IMPHASH=129F58DE3D687FB7F012BF6C3D679997*", "*IMPHASH=2C617A175D0086251642C6619F7CC8BA*", "*IMPHASH=8828F0B906F7844358FB92A899E9520F*", "*IMPHASH=AF76D95157EC554DC1EF178E4E66D447*", "*IMPHASH=E1B04316B61ACA31DD52ABBEC0A37FD5*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Empire PowerShell Launch Parameters
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious powershell command line parameters used in Empire
false_positives: Other tools that incidentally use the same command line parameters
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("* -NoP -sta -NonI -W Hidden -Enc *", "* -noP -sta -w 1 -enc *", "* -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -enc *", "* -noP -sta -w 1 -enc*", "* -enc  SQB*", "* -nop -exec bypass -EncodedCommand *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Empire PowerShell UAC Bypass
author: Ecco
description: Detects some Empire PowerShell UAC bypass methods
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("* -NoP -NonI -w Hidden -c $x=$((gp HKCU:Software\\Microsoft\\Windows Update).Update)*", "* -NoP -NonI -c $x=$((gp HKCU:Software\\Microsoft\\Windows Update).Update);*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - WinRM Access Via Evil-WinRM
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a computer using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\ruby.exe" and (CommandLine:"*-i *" and CommandLine:"*-u *" and CommandLine:"*-p *"))

name: SIGMA - Hacktool Execution - Imphash
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of different Windows based hacktools via their import hash (imphash) even if the files have been renamed
false_positives: Legitimate use of one of these tools
query: any where EventID:1 and (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=BCCA3C247B619DCD13C8CDFF5F123932*", "*IMPHASH=3A19059BD7688CB88E70005F18EFC439*", "*IMPHASH=bf6223a49e45d99094406777eb6004ba*", "*IMPHASH=23867A89C2B8FC733BE6CF5EF902F2D1*", "*IMPHASH=A37FF327F8D48E8A4D2F757E1B6E70BC*", "*IMPHASH=F9A28C458284584A93B14216308D31BD*", "*IMPHASH=6118619783FC175BC7EBECFF0769B46E*", "*IMPHASH=B50199E952C875241B9CE06C971CE3C1*"))

name: SIGMA - Hacktool Execution - PE Metadata
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of different Windows based hacktools via PE metadata (company, product, etc.) even if the files have been renamed
query: any where EventID:1 and Company:"Cube0x0"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - GMER Rootkit Detector and Remover Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution GMER tool based on image and hash fields.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\gmer.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=E9DC058440D321AA17D0600B3CA0AB04*", "*SHA1=539C228B6B332F5AA523E5CE358C16647D8BBE57*", "*SHA256=E8A3E804A96C716A3E9B69195DB6FFB0D33E2433AF871E4D4E1EAB3097237173*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - HandleKatz LSASS Dumper Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of HandleKatz, a tool that demonstrates the usage of cloned handles to Lsass in order to create an obfuscated memory dump of the same
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\loader.exe" and CommandLine:"*--pid:*") or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=38D9E015591BBFD4929E0D0F47FA0055*", "*IMPHASH=0E2216679CA6E1094D63322E3412D650*")) or ((CommandLine:"*--pid:*" and CommandLine:"*--outfile:*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.dmp*", "*lsass*", "*.obf*", "*dump*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Hashcat Password Cracker Execution
author: frack113
description: Execute Hashcat.exe with provided SAM file from registry of Windows and Password list to crack against
false_positives: Tools that use similar command line flags and values
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\hashcat.exe" or (CommandLine:"*-a *" and CommandLine:"*-m 1000 *" and CommandLine:"*-r *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - HollowReaper Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of HollowReaper, a process hollowing shellcode launcher used for stealth payload execution through process hollowing.
It replaces the memory of a legitimate process with custom shellcode, allowing the attacker to execute payloads under the guise of trusted binaries.
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\HollowReaper.exe"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Htran/NATBypass Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects executable names or flags used by Htran or Htran-like tools (e.g. NATBypass)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\htran.exe", "*\\lcx.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe -tran *", "*.exe -slave *")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Hydra Password Bruteforce Execution
author: Vasiliy Burov
description: Detects command line parameters used by Hydra password guessing hack tool
false_positives: Software that uses the caret encased keywords PASS and USER in its command line
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*-u *" and CommandLine:"*-p *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*^USER^*", "*^PASS^*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Potential Impacket Lateral Movement Activity
author: Ecco, oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Tim Rauch
description: Detects wmiexec/dcomexec/atexec/smbexec from Impacket framework
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\wmiprvse.exe", "*\\mmc.exe", "*\\explorer.exe", "*\\services.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*cmd.exe*" and CommandLine:"*/Q*" and CommandLine:"*/c*" and CommandLine:"*\\\\127.0.0.1\\*" and CommandLine:"*&1*")) or ((ParentCommandLine like~ ("*svchost.exe -k netsvcs*", "*taskeng.exe*")) and (CommandLine:"*cmd.exe*" and CommandLine:"*/C*" and CommandLine:"*Windows\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"*&1*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Impacket Tools Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of different compiled Windows binaries of the impacket toolset (based on names or part of their names - could lead to false positives)
false_positives: Legitimate use of the impacket tools
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\goldenPac*", "*\\karmaSMB*", "*\\kintercept*", "*\\ntlmrelayx*", "*\\rpcdump*", "*\\samrdump*", "*\\secretsdump*", "*\\smbexec*", "*\\smbrelayx*", "*\\wmiexec*", "*\\wmipersist*")) or (Image like~ ("*\\atexec_windows.exe", "*\\dcomexec_windows.exe", "*\\dpapi_windows.exe", "*\\findDelegation_windows.exe", "*\\GetADUsers_windows.exe", "*\\GetNPUsers_windows.exe", "*\\getPac_windows.exe", "*\\getST_windows.exe", "*\\getTGT_windows.exe", "*\\GetUserSPNs_windows.exe", "*\\ifmap_windows.exe", "*\\mimikatz_windows.exe", "*\\netview_windows.exe", "*\\nmapAnswerMachine_windows.exe", "*\\opdump_windows.exe", "*\\psexec_windows.exe", "*\\rdp_check_windows.exe", "*\\sambaPipe_windows.exe", "*\\smbclient_windows.exe", "*\\smbserver_windows.exe", "*\\sniff_windows.exe", "*\\sniffer_windows.exe", "*\\split_windows.exe", "*\\ticketer_windows.exe")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Impersonate Execution
author: Sai Prashanth Pulisetti @pulisettis
description: Detects execution of the Impersonate tool. Which can be used to manipulate tokens on a Windows computers remotely (PsExec/WmiExec) or interactively
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*impersonate.exe*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* list *", "* exec *", "* adduser *"))) or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=9520714AB576B0ED01D1513691377D01*", "*SHA256=E81CC96E2118DC4FBFE5BAD1604E0AC7681960143E2101E1A024D52264BB0A8A*", "*IMPHASH=0A358FFC1697B7A07D0E817AC740DF62*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Inveigh Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of Inveigh a cross-platform .NET IPv4/IPv6 machine-in-the-middle tool
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Inveigh.exe" or (OriginalFileName like~ ("\\Inveigh.exe", "\\Inveigh.dll")) or Description:"Inveigh" or (CommandLine like~ ("* -SpooferIP*", "* -ReplyToIPs *", "* -ReplyToDomains *", "* -ReplyToMACs *", "* -SnifferIP*")))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*cmd*" and CommandLine:"*&&*" and CommandLine:"*clipboard]::*" and CommandLine:"*-f*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/c*", "*/r*")))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation
author: Daniel Bohannon (@Mandiant/@FireEye), oscd.community
description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the following code block
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine regex~ "\$PSHome\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$PSHome\[" or CommandLine regex~ "\$ShellId\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$ShellId\[" or CommandLine regex~ "\$env:Public\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$env:Public\[" or CommandLine regex~ "\$env:ComSpec\[(\s*\d{1,3}\s*,){2}" or CommandLine regex~ "\*mdr\*\W\s*\)\.Name" or CommandLine regex~ "\$VerbosePreference\.ToString\(" or CommandLine regex~ "\[String\]\s*\$VerbosePreference")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine regex~ "cmd.{0,5}(?:\/c|\/r).+powershell.+(?:\$\{?input\}?|noexit).+\""

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*new-object*" and CommandLine:"*text.encoding]::ascii*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*system.io.compression.deflatestream*", "*system.io.streamreader*", "*readtoend(*")))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine regex~ "(?i)(set).*&&\s?set.*(environment|invoke|\$\{?input).*&&.*""

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine regex~ "(?i)echo.*clip.*&&.*(Clipboard|i`?n`?v`?o`?k`?e`?)"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*&&*" and CommandLine:"*mshta*" and CommandLine:"*vbscript:createobject*" and CommandLine:"*.run*" and CommandLine:"*(window.close)*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*&&set*" and CommandLine:"*cmd*" and CommandLine:"*/c*" and CommandLine:"*-f*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*{0}*", "*{1}*", "*{2}*", "*{3}*", "*{4}*", "*{5}*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Jlaive In-Memory Assembly Execution
author: Jose Luis Sanchez Martinez (@Joseliyo_Jstnk)
description: Detects the use of Jlaive to execute assemblies in a copied PowerShell
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\cmd.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*.bat") and ((Image:"*\\xcopy.exe" and (CommandLine:"*powershell.exe*" and CommandLine:"*.bat.exe*")) or (Image:"*\\xcopy.exe" and (CommandLine:"*pwsh.exe*" and CommandLine:"*.bat.exe*")) or (Image:"*\\attrib.exe" and (CommandLine:"*+s*" and CommandLine:"*+h*" and CommandLine:"*.bat.exe*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Koadic Execution
author: wagga, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects command line parameters used by Koadic hack tool
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and (CommandLine:"*/q*" and CommandLine:"*/c*" and CommandLine:"*chcp*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - KrbRelay Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of KrbRelay, a Kerberos relaying tool
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\KrbRelay.exe" or OriginalFileName:"KrbRelay.exe") or (CommandLine:"* -spn *" and CommandLine:"* -clsid *" and CommandLine:"* -rbcd *") or (CommandLine:"*shadowcred*" and CommandLine:"*clsid*" and CommandLine:"*spn*") or (CommandLine:"*spn *" and CommandLine:"*session *" and CommandLine:"*clsid *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - KrbRelayUp Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects KrbRelayUp used to perform a universal no-fix local privilege escalation in Windows domain environments where LDAP signing is not enforced
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\KrbRelayUp.exe" or OriginalFileName:"KrbRelayUp.exe") or (CommandLine:"* relay *" and CommandLine:"* -Domain *" and CommandLine:"* -ComputerName *") or (CommandLine:"* krbscm *" and CommandLine:"* -sc *") or (CommandLine:"* spawn *" and CommandLine:"* -d *" and CommandLine:"* -cn *" and CommandLine:"* -cp *"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - RemoteKrbRelay Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of RemoteKrbRelay, a Kerberos relaying tool via CommandLine flags and PE metadata.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\RemoteKrbRelay.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RemoteKrbRelay.exe") or (CommandLine:"* -clsid *" and CommandLine:"* -target *" and CommandLine:"* -victim *") or (CommandLine:"*-rbcd *" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-cn *", "*--computername *"))) or (CommandLine:"*-chp *" and (CommandLine:"*-chpPass *" and CommandLine:"*-chpUser *")) or (CommandLine:"*-addgroupmember *" and CommandLine:"*-group *" and CommandLine:"*-groupuser *") or ((CommandLine:"*-smb *" and CommandLine:"*--smbkeyword *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*interactive*", "*secrets*", "*service-add*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - LaZagne Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of the LaZagne. A utility used to retrieve multiple types of passwords stored on a local computer.
LaZagne has been leveraged multiple times by threat actors in order to dump credentials.
false_positives: Some false positive is expected from tools with similar command line flags.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\lazagne.exe" or ((Image like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Tmp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\$Recycle.bin*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Favorites\\*", "*\\Links\\*", "*\\Music\\*", "*\\Photos\\*", "*\\Pictures\\*", "*\\Saved Games\\*", "*\\Searches\\*", "*\\Users\\Contacts\\*", "*\\Users\\Default\\*", "*\\Users\\Searches\\*", "*\\Videos\\*", "*\\Windows\\addins\\*", "*\\Windows\\Fonts\\*", "*\\Windows\\IME\\*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe all", "*.exe browsers", "*.exe chats", "*.exe databases", "*.exe games", "*.exe git", "*.exe mails", "*.exe maven", "*.exe memory", "*.exe multimedia", "*.exe sysadmin", "*.exe unused", "*.exe wifi", "*.exe windows")))) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* all *", "* browsers *", "* chats *", "* databases *", "* games *", "* mails *", "* maven *", "* memory *", "* multimedia *", "* php *", "* svn *", "* sysadmin *", "* unused *", "* wifi *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-1Password*", "*-apachedirectorystudio*", "*-autologon*", "*-ChromiumBased*", "*-coreftp*", "*-credfiles*", "*-credman*", "*-cyberduck*", "*-dbvis*", "*-EyeCon*", "*-filezilla*", "*-filezillaserver*", "*-ftpnavigator*", "*-galconfusion*", "*-gitforwindows*", "*-hashdump*", "*-iisapppool*", "*-IISCentralCertP*", "*-kalypsomedia*", "*-keepass*", "*-keepassconfig*", "*-lsa_secrets*", "*-mavenrepositories*", "*-memory_dump*", "*-Mozilla*", "*-mRemoteNG*", "*-mscache*", "*-opensshforwindows*", "*-openvpn*", "*-outlook*", "*-pidgin*", "*-postgresql*", "*-psi-im*", "*-puttycm*", "*-pypykatz*", "*-Rclone*", "*-rdpmanager*", "*-robomongo*", "*-roguestale*", "*-skype*", "*-SQLDeveloper*", "*-squirrel*", "*-tortoise*", "*-turba*", "*-UCBrowser*", "*-unattended*", "*-vault*", "*-vaultfiles*", "*-vnc*", "*-winscp*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - LocalPotato Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the LocalPotato POC based on basic PE metadata information and default CLI examples
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\LocalPotato.exe" or (CommandLine:"*.exe -i C:\\*" and CommandLine:"*-o Windows\\*") or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=E1742EE971D6549E8D4D81115F88F1FC*", "*IMPHASH=DD82066EFBA94D7556EF582F247C8BB5*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Meterpreter/CobaltStrike Activity
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Ecco, Florian Roth
description: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service starting
false_positives: Commandlines containing components like cmd accidentally
Jobs and services started with cmd
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\services.exe" and (((CommandLine:"*/c*" and CommandLine:"*echo*" and CommandLine:"*\\pipe\\*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd*", "*%COMSPEC%*"))) or (CommandLine:"*rundll32*" and CommandLine:"*.dll,a*" and CommandLine:"*/p:*")) and (not CommandLine:"*MpCmdRun*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Mimikatz Execution
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community, David ANDRE (additional keywords), Tim Shelton
description: Detection well-known mimikatz command line arguments
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*DumpCreds*", "*mimikatz*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*::aadcookie*", "*::detours*", "*::memssp*", "*::mflt*", "*::ncroutemon*", "*::ngcsign*", "*::printnightmare*", "*::skeleton*", "*::preshutdown*", "*::mstsc*", "*::multirdp*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*rpc::*", "*token::*", "*crypto::*", "*dpapi::*", "*sekurlsa::*", "*kerberos::*", "*lsadump::*", "*privilege::*", "*process::*", "*vault::*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - PCHunter Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects suspicious use of PCHunter, a tool like Process Hacker to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level stuff
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\PCHunter64.exe", "*\\PCHunter32.exe")) or (OriginalFileName:"PCHunter.exe" or Description:"Epoolsoft Windows Information View Tools") or (Hashes like~ ("*SHA1=5F1CBC3D99558307BC1250D084FA968521482025*", "*MD5=987B65CD9B9F4E9A1AFD8F8B48CF64A7*", "*SHA256=2B214BDDAAB130C274DE6204AF6DBA5AEEC7433DA99AA950022FA306421A6D32*", "*IMPHASH=444D210CEA1FF8112F256A4997EED7FF*", "*SHA1=3FB89787CB97D902780DA080545584D97FB1C2EB*", "*MD5=228DD0C2E6287547E26FFBD973A40F14*", "*SHA256=55F041BF4E78E9BFA6D4EE68BE40E496CE3A1353E1CA4306598589E19802522C*", "*IMPHASH=0479F44DF47CFA2EF1CCC4416A538663*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Default PowerSploit/Empire Scheduled Task Creation
author: Markus Neis, @Karneades
description: Detects the creation of a schtask via PowerSploit or Empire Default Configuration.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/Create*" and CommandLine:"*powershell.exe -NonI*" and CommandLine:"*/TN Updater /TR*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/SC ONLOGON*", "*/SC DAILY /ST*", "*/SC ONIDLE*", "*/SC HOURLY*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - PowerTool Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the tool PowerTool which has the ability to kill a process, delete its process file, unload drivers, and delete the driver files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\PowerTool.exe", "*\\PowerTool64.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"PowerTool.exe")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - PurpleSharp Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the PurpleSharp adversary simulation tool
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\purplesharp*" or OriginalFileName:"PurpleSharp.exe") or (CommandLine like~ ("*xyz123456.exe*", "*PurpleSharp*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Pypykatz Credentials Dumping Activity
author: frack113
description: Detects the usage of "pypykatz" to obtain stored credentials. Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the Security Account Manager (SAM) database through Windows registry where the SAM database is stored
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\pypykatz.exe", "*\\python.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*live*" and CommandLine:"*registry*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Quarks PwDump Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Quarks PwDump tool via commandline arguments
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\QuarksPwDump.exe" or (CommandLine like~ (" -dhl", " --dump-hash-local", " -dhdc", " --dump-hash-domain-cached", " --dump-bitlocker", " -dhd ", " --dump-hash-domain ", "--ntds-file")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - RedMimicry Winnti Playbook Execution
author: Alexander Rausch
description: Detects actions caused by the RedMimicry Winnti playbook a automated breach emulations utility
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*gthread-3.6.dll*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\tmp.bat*", "*sigcmm-2.4.dll*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential SMB Relay Attack Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects different hacktools used for relay attacks on Windows for privilege escalation
false_positives: Legitimate files with these rare hacktool names
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*PetitPotam*", "*RottenPotato*", "*HotPotato*", "*JuicyPotato*", "*\\just_dce_*", "*Juicy Potato*", "*\\temp\\rot.exe*", "*\\Potato.exe*", "*\\SpoolSample.exe*", "*\\Responder.exe*", "*\\smbrelayx*", "*\\ntlmrelayx*", "*\\LocalPotato*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-Tater*", "* smbrelay*", "* ntlmrelay*", "*cme smb *", "* /ntlm:NTLMhash *", "*Invoke-PetitPotam*", "*.exe -t * -p *")) or (CommandLine:"*.exe -c \"{*" and CommandLine:"*}\" -z")) and (not (Image like~ ("*HotPotatoes6*", "*HotPotatoes7*", "*HotPotatoes *"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Rubeus Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the hacktool Rubeus via PE information of command line parameters
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Rubeus.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Rubeus.exe" or Description:"Rubeus" or (CommandLine like~ ("*asreproast *", "*dump /service:krbtgt *", "*dump /luid:0x*", "*kerberoast *", "*createnetonly /program:*", "*ptt /ticket:*", "*/impersonateuser:*", "*renew /ticket:*", "*asktgt /user:*", "*harvest /interval:*", "*s4u /user:*", "*s4u /ticket:*", "*hash /password:*", "*golden /aes256:*", "*silver /user:*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SafetyKatz Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the hacktool SafetyKatz via PE information and default Image name
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SafetyKatz.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SafetyKatz.exe" or Description:"SafetyKatz")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SecurityXploded Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of SecurityXploded Tools
query: any where EventID:1 and (Company:"SecurityXploded" or Image:"*PasswordDump.exe" or OriginalFileName:"*PasswordDump.exe")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - PPID Spoofing SelectMyParent Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of parent process ID spoofing tools like Didier Stevens tool SelectMyParent
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SelectMyParent.exe" or (CommandLine like~ ("*PPID-spoof*", "*ppid_spoof*", "*spoof-ppid*", "*spoof_ppid*", "*ppidspoof*", "*spoofppid*", "*spoofedppid*", "* -spawnto *")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("*PPID-spoof*", "*ppid_spoof*", "*spoof-ppid*", "*spoof_ppid*", "*ppidspoof*", "*spoofppid*", "*spoofedppid*")) or Description:"SelectMyParent" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=04D974875BD225F00902B4CAD9AF3FBC*", "*IMPHASH=A782AF154C9E743DDF3F3EB2B8F3D16E*", "*IMPHASH=89059503D7FBF470E68F7E63313DA3AD*", "*IMPHASH=CA28337632625C8281AB8A130B3D6BAD*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharPersist Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the hacktool SharPersist - used to deploy various different kinds of persistence mechanisms
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SharPersist.exe" or Product:"SharPersist") or (CommandLine like~ ("* -t schtask -c *", "* -t startupfolder -c *")) or (CommandLine:"* -t reg -c *" and CommandLine:"* -m add*") or (CommandLine:"* -t service -c *" and CommandLine:"* -m add*") or (CommandLine:"* -t schtask -c *" and CommandLine:"* -m add*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpEvtMute Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of SharpEvtHook, a tool that tampers with the Windows event logs
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SharpEvtMute.exe" or Description:"SharpEvtMute" or (CommandLine like~ ("*--Filter \"rule *", "*--Encoded --Filter \\\"*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpLdapWhoami Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects SharpLdapWhoami, a whoami alternative that queries the LDAP service on a domain controller
false_positives: Programs that use the same command line flags
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SharpLdapWhoami.exe" or (OriginalFileName:"*SharpLdapWhoami*" or Product:"SharpLdapWhoami") or (CommandLine like~ ("* /method:ntlm", "* /method:kerb", "* /method:nego", "* /m:nego", "* /m:ntlm", "* /m:kerb")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpMove Tool Execution
author: Luca Di Bartolomeo (CrimpSec)
description: Detects the execution of SharpMove, a .NET utility performing multiple tasks such as "Task Creation", "SCM" query, VBScript execution using WMI via its PE metadata and command line options.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SharpMove.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SharpMove.exe") or (CommandLine:"*computername=*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*action=create*", "*action=dcom*", "*action=executevbs*", "*action=hijackdcom*", "*action=modschtask*", "*action=modsvc*", "*action=query*", "*action=scm*", "*action=startservice*", "*action=taskscheduler*"))))

name: SIGMA - HKTL - SharpSuccessor Privilege Escalation Tool Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of SharpSuccessor, a tool used to exploit the BadSuccessor attack for privilege escalation in WinServer 2025 Active Directory environments.
Successful usage of this tool can let the attackers gain the domain admin privileges by exploiting the BadSuccessor vulnerability.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SharpSuccessor.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SharpSuccessor.exe" or CommandLine:"*SharpSuccessor*" or (CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"* /impersonate*" and CommandLine:"* /path*" and CommandLine:"* /account*" and CommandLine:"* /name*"))
