name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpUp PrivEsc Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of SharpUp, a tool for local privilege escalation
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SharpUp.exe" or Description:"SharpUp" or (CommandLine like~ ("*HijackablePaths*", "*UnquotedServicePath*", "*ProcessDLLHijack*", "*ModifiableServiceBinaries*", "*ModifiableScheduledTask*", "*DomainGPPPassword*", "*CachedGPPPassword*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpView Execution
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"SharpView.exe" or Image:"*\\SharpView.exe" or (CommandLine like~ ("*Add-RemoteConnection*", "*Convert-ADName*", "*ConvertFrom-SID*", "*ConvertFrom-UACValue*", "*Convert-SidToName*", "*Export-PowerViewCSV*", "*Find-DomainObjectPropertyOutlier*", "*Find-DomainProcess*", "*Find-DomainShare*", "*Find-DomainUserEvent*", "*Find-DomainUserLocation*", "*Find-ForeignGroup*", "*Find-ForeignUser*", "*Find-GPOComputerAdmin*", "*Find-GPOLocation*", "*Find-Interesting*", "*Find-LocalAdminAccess*", "*Find-ManagedSecurityGroups*", "*Get-CachedRDPConnection*", "*Get-DFSshare*", "*Get-DomainComputer*", "*Get-DomainController*", "*Get-DomainDFSShare*", "*Get-DomainDNSRecord*", "*Get-DomainFileServer*", "*Get-DomainForeign*", "*Get-DomainGPO*", "*Get-DomainGroup*", "*Get-DomainGUIDMap*", "*Get-DomainManagedSecurityGroup*", "*Get-DomainObject*", "*Get-DomainOU*", "*Get-DomainPolicy*", "*Get-DomainSID*", "*Get-DomainSite*", "*Get-DomainSPNTicket*", "*Get-DomainSubnet*", "*Get-DomainTrust*", "*Get-DomainUserEvent*", "*Get-ForestDomain*", "*Get-ForestGlobalCatalog*", "*Get-ForestTrust*", "*Get-GptTmpl*", "*Get-GroupsXML*", "*Get-LastLoggedOn*", "*Get-LoggedOnLocal*", "*Get-NetComputer*", "*Get-NetDomain*", "*Get-NetFileServer*", "*Get-NetForest*", "*Get-NetGPO*", "*Get-NetGroupMember*", "*Get-NetLocalGroup*", "*Get-NetLoggedon*", "*Get-NetOU*", "*Get-NetProcess*", "*Get-NetRDPSession*", "*Get-NetSession*", "*Get-NetShare*", "*Get-NetSite*", "*Get-NetSubnet*", "*Get-NetUser*", "*Get-PathAcl*", "*Get-PrincipalContext*", "*Get-RegistryMountedDrive*", "*Get-RegLoggedOn*", "*Get-WMIRegCachedRDPConnection*", "*Get-WMIRegLastLoggedOn*", "*Get-WMIRegMountedDrive*", "*Get-WMIRegProxy*", "*Invoke-ACLScanner*", "*Invoke-CheckLocalAdminAccess*", "*Invoke-Kerberoast*", "*Invoke-MapDomainTrust*", "*Invoke-RevertToSelf*", "*Invoke-Sharefinder*", "*Invoke-UserImpersonation*", "*Remove-DomainObjectAcl*", "*Remove-RemoteConnection*", "*Request-SPNTicket*", "*Set-DomainObject*", "*Test-AdminAccess*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpWSUS/WSUSpendu Execution
author: @Kostastsale, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of SharpWSUS or WSUSpendu, utilities that allow for lateral movement through WSUS.
Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) is a critical component of Windows systems and is frequently configured in a way that allows an attacker to circumvent internal networking limitations.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"* -Inject *" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -PayloadArgs *", "* -PayloadFile *"))) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* approve *", "* create *", "* check *", "* delete *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* /payload:*", "* /payload=*", "* /updateid:*", "* /updateid=*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpChisel Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Sharp Chisel via the commandline arguments
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SharpChisel.exe" or Product:"SharpChisel")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpDPAPI Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of the SharpDPAPI tool based on CommandLine flags and PE metadata.
SharpDPAPI is a C# port of some DPAPI functionality from the Mimikatz project.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SharpDPAPI.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SharpDPAPI.exe") or ((CommandLine like~ ("* backupkey *", "* blob *", "* certificates *", "* credentials *", "* keepass *", "* masterkeys *", "* rdg *", "* vaults *")) and ((CommandLine:"* {*" and CommandLine:"*}:*") or (CommandLine like~ ("* /file:*", "* /machine*", "* /mkfile:*", "* /password:*", "* /pvk:*", "* /server:*", "* /target:*", "* /unprotect*")))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpImpersonation Execution
author: Sai Prashanth Pulisetti @pulisettis, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the SharpImpersonation tool. Which can be used to manipulate tokens on a Windows computers remotely (PsExec/WmiExec) or interactively
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SharpImpersonation.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SharpImpersonation.exe") or ((CommandLine:"* user:*" and CommandLine:"* binary:*") or (CommandLine:"* user:*" and CommandLine:"* shellcode:*") or (CommandLine like~ ("* technique:CreateProcessAsUserW*", "* technique:ImpersonateLoggedOnuser*"))))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SharpLDAPmonitor Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the SharpLDAPmonitor. Which can monitor the creation, deletion and changes to LDAP objects.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SharpLDAPmonitor.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SharpLDAPmonitor.exe") or (CommandLine:"*/user:*" and CommandLine:"*/pass:*" and CommandLine:"*/dcip:*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SILENTTRINITY Stager Execution
author: Aleksey Potapov, oscd.community
description: Detects SILENTTRINITY stager use via PE metadata
query: any where EventID:1 and Description:"*st2stager*"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Sliver C2 Implant Activity Pattern
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects process activity patterns as seen being used by Sliver C2 framework implants
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*-NoExit -Command [Console]::OutputEncoding=[Text.UTF8Encoding]::UTF8*"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SOAPHound Execution
author: @kostastsale
description: Detects the execution of SOAPHound, a .NET tool for collecting Active Directory data, using specific command-line arguments that may indicate an attempt to extract sensitive AD information.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* --buildcache *", "* --bhdump *", "* --certdump *", "* --dnsdump *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* --cachefilename *", "* -o *", "* --outputdirectory*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Stracciatella Execution
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Stracciatella which executes a Powershell runspace from within C# (aka SharpPick technique) with AMSI, ETW and Script Block Logging disabled based on PE metadata characteristics.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Stracciatella.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Stracciatella.exe" or Description:"Stracciatella" or (Hashes like~ ("*SHA256=9d25e61ec1527e2a69d7c2a4e3fe2fe15890710c198a66a9f25d99fdf6c7b956*", "*SHA256=fd16609bd9830c63b9413671678bb159b89c357d21942ddbb6b93add808d121a*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - SysmonEOP Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the PoC that can be used to exploit Sysmon CVE-2022-41120
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SysmonEOP.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=22F4089EB8ABA31E1BB162C6D9BF72E5*", "*IMPHASH=5123FA4C4384D431CD0D893EEB49BBEC*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - TruffleSnout Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of TruffleSnout.exe an iterative AD discovery toolkit for offensive operators, situational awareness and targeted low noise enumeration.
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"TruffleSnout.exe" or Image:"*\\TruffleSnout.exe")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - UACMe Akagi Execution
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of UACMe, a tool used for UAC bypasses, via default PE metadata
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"UACMe" or (Company like~ ("REvol Corp", "APT 92", "UG North", "Hazardous Environments", "CD Project Rekt")) or (Description like~ ("UACMe main module", "Pentesting utility")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Akagi.exe", "Akagi64.exe"))) or (Image like~ ("*\\Akagi64.exe", "*\\Akagi.exe")) or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=767637C23BB42CD5D7397CF58B0BE688*", "*IMPHASH=14C4E4C72BA075E9069EE67F39188AD8*", "*IMPHASH=3C782813D4AFCE07BBFC5A9772ACDBDC*", "*IMPHASH=7D010C6BB6A3726F327F7E239166D127*", "*IMPHASH=89159BA4DD04E4CE5559F132A9964EB3*", "*IMPHASH=6F33F4A5FC42B8CEC7314947BD13F30F*", "*IMPHASH=5834ED4291BDEB928270428EBBAF7604*", "*IMPHASH=5A8A8A43F25485E7EE1B201EDCBC7A38*", "*IMPHASH=DC7D30B90B2D8ABF664FBED2B1B59894*", "*IMPHASH=41923EA1F824FE63EA5BEB84DB7A3E74*", "*IMPHASH=3DE09703C8E79ED2CA3F01074719906B*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Windows Credential Editor (WCE) Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of Windows Credential Editor (WCE), a popular post-exploitation tool used to extract plaintext passwords, hash, PIN code and Kerberos tickets from memory.
It is often used by threat actors for credential dumping and lateral movement within compromised networks.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\WCE.exe", "*\\WCE64.exe")) or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=136F0A8572C058A96436C82E541E4C41*", "*IMPHASH=589657C64DDE88533186C39F82FA1F50*", "*IMPHASH=6BFE09EFCB4FFDE061EBDBAFC4DB84CF*", "*IMPHASH=7D490037BF450877E6D0287BDCFF8D2E*", "*IMPHASH=8AB93B061287C79F3088C5BC7E7D97ED*", "*IMPHASH=A53A02B997935FD8EEDCB5F7ABAB9B9F*", "*IMPHASH=BA434A7A729EEC20E136CA4C32D6C740*", "*IMPHASH=BD1D1547DA13C0FCB6C15E86217D5EB8*", "*IMPHASH=E96A73C7BF33A464C510EDE582318BF2*")))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - winPEAS Execution
author: Georg Lauenstein (sure[secure])
description: WinPEAS is a script that search for possible paths to escalate privileges on Windows hosts. The checks are explained on book.hacktricks.xyz
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"winPEAS.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\winPEASany_ofs.exe", "*\\winPEASany.exe", "*\\winPEASx64_ofs.exe", "*\\winPEASx64.exe", "*\\winPEASx86_ofs.exe", "*\\winPEASx86.exe"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("* applicationsinfo*", "* browserinfo*", "* eventsinfo*", "* fileanalysis*", "* filesinfo*", "* processinfo*", "* servicesinfo*", "* windowscreds*")) or CommandLine:"*https://github.com/carlospolop/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/*" or (ParentCommandLine:"* -linpeas" or CommandLine:"* -linpeas"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - WinPwn Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects commandline keywords indicative of potential usge of the tool WinPwn. A tool for Windows and Active Directory reconnaissance and exploitation.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*Offline_Winpwn*", "*WinPwn *", "*WinPwn.exe*", "*WinPwn.ps1*"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Wmiexec Default Powershell Command
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with a specific flag sequence that is used by the Wmiexec script
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*-NoP -NoL -sta -NonI -W Hidden -Exec Bypass -Enc*"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - XORDump Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious use of XORDump process memory dumping utility
false_positives: Another tool that uses the command line switches of XORdump
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\xordump.exe" or (CommandLine like~ ("* -process lsass.exe *", "* -m comsvcs *", "* -m dbghelp *", "* -m dbgcore *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious ZipExec Execution
author: frack113
description: ZipExec is a Proof-of-Concept (POC) tool to wrap binary-based tools into a password-protected zip file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*/generic:Microsoft_Windows_Shell_ZipFolder:filename=*" and CommandLine:"*.zip*" and CommandLine:"*/pass:*" and CommandLine:"*/user:*") or (CommandLine:"*/delete*" and CommandLine:"*Microsoft_Windows_Shell_ZipFolder:filename=*" and CommandLine:"*.zip*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution of Hostname
author: frack113
description: Use of hostname to get information
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\HOSTNAME.EXE"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious HWP Sub Processes
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious Hangul Word Processor (Hanword) sub processes that could indicate an exploitation
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\Hwp.exe" and Image:"*\\gbb.exe")

name: SIGMA - Potential Fake Instance Of Hxtsr.EXE Executed
author: Sreeman
description:
HxTsr.exe is a Microsoft compressed executable file called Microsoft Outlook Communications.
HxTsr.exe is part of Outlook apps, because it resides in a hidden "WindowsApps" subfolder of "C:\Program Files".
Any instances of hxtsr.exe not in this folder may be malware camouflaging itself as HxTsr.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\hxtsr.exe" and (not (Image:"*:\\program files\\windowsapps\\microsoft.windowscommunicationsapps_*" and Image:"*\\hxtsr.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Use Icacls to Hide File to Everyone
author: frack113
description: Detect use of icacls to deny access for everyone in Users folder sometimes used to hide malicious files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"iCACLS.EXE" or Image:"*\\icacls.exe") and (CommandLine:"*/deny*" and CommandLine:"*S-1-1-0:*"))

name: SIGMA - File Download And Execution Via IEExec.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the IEExec utility to download and execute files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\IEExec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"IEExec.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Self Extracting Package Creation Via Iexpress.EXE From Potentially Suspicious Location
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of iexpress.exe to create binaries via Self Extraction Directive (SED) files located in potentially suspicious locations.
This behavior has been observed in-the-wild by different threat actors.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\iexpress.exe" or OriginalFileName:"IEXPRESS.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -n *", "* /n *", "* –n *", "* —n *", "* ―n *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Disable Windows IIS HTTP Logging
author: frack113
description: Disables HTTP logging on a Windows IIS web server as seen by Threat Group 3390 (Bronze Union)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\appcmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"appcmd.exe") and (CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*config*" and CommandLine:"*section:httplogging*" and CommandLine:"*dontLog:true*"))

name: SIGMA - Microsoft IIS Service Account Password Dumped
author: Tim Rauch, Janantha Marasinghe, Elastic (original idea)
description: Detects the Internet Information Services (IIS) command-line tool, AppCmd, being used to list passwords
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\appcmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"appcmd.exe") and CommandLine:"*list *") and ((CommandLine like~ ("* /config*", "* /xml*", "* -config*", "* -xml*")) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* /@t*", "* /text*", "* /show*", "* -@t*", "* -text*", "* -show*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\**", "*password*")))))

name: SIGMA - IIS Native-Code Module Command Line Installation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious IIS native-code module installations via command line
false_positives: Unknown as it may vary from organisation to organisation how admins use to install IIS modules
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\appcmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"appcmd.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*install*" and CommandLine:"*module*") and CommandLine like~ ("*-name:*", "*/name:*", "*–name:*", "*—name:*", "*―name:*"))) and (not ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\inetsrv\\iissetup.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious IIS URL GlobalRules Rewrite Via AppCmd
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "appcmd" to create new global URL rewrite rules. This behaviour has been observed being used by threat actors to add new rules so they can access their webshells.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of appcmd to add new URL rewrite rules
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\appcmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"appcmd.exe") and (CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*config*" and CommandLine:"*section:system.webServer/rewrite/globalRules*" and CommandLine:"*commit:*"))

name: SIGMA - Microsoft IIS Connection Strings Decryption
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects use of aspnet_regiis to decrypt Microsoft IIS connection strings. An attacker with Microsoft IIS web server access via a webshell or alike can decrypt and dump any hardcoded connection strings, such as the MSSQL service account password using aspnet_regiis command.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\aspnet_regiis.exe" or OriginalFileName:"aspnet_regiis.exe") and (CommandLine:"*connectionStrings*" and CommandLine:"* -pdf*"))

name: SIGMA - IIS WebServer Log Deletion via CommandLine Utilities
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects attempts to delete Internet Information Services (IIS) log files via command line utilities, which is a common defense evasion technique used by attackers to cover their tracks.
Threat actors often abuse vulnerabilities in web applications hosted on IIS servers to gain initial access and later delete IIS logs to evade detection.
false_positives: Deletion of IIS logs that are older than a certain retention period as part of regular maintenance activities.
Legitimate schedule tasks or scripts that clean up log files regularly.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "powershell_ise.exe", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*del *", "*erase *", "*rm *", "*remove-item *", "*rmdir *")) and CommandLine:"*\\inetpub\\logs\\*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious IIS Module Registration
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Microsoft (idea)
description: Detects a suspicious IIS module registration as described in Microsoft threat report on IIS backdoors
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\w3wp.exe" and (CommandLine:"*appcmd.exe add module*" or (CommandLine:"* system.enterpriseservices.internal.publish*" and Image:"*\\powershell.exe") or (CommandLine:"*gacutil*" and CommandLine:"* /I*")))

name: SIGMA - C# IL Code Compilation Via Ilasm.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of "Ilasm.EXE" in order to compile C# intermediate (IL) code to EXE or DLL.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ilasm.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ilasm.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /dll*", "* /exe*")))

name: SIGMA - ImagingDevices Unusual Parent/Child Processes
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects unusual parent or children of the ImagingDevices.exe (Windows Contacts) process as seen being used with Bumblebee activity
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\WmiPrvSE.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe")) and Image:"*\\ImagingDevices.exe") or ParentImage:"*\\ImagingDevices.exe")

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via IMEWDBLD.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects usage of "IMEWDBLD.exe" to download arbitrary files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\IMEWDBLD.exe" or OriginalFileName:"imewdbld.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - InfDefaultInstall.exe .inf Execution
author: frack113
description: Executes SCT script using scrobj.dll from a command in entered into a specially prepared INF file.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*InfDefaultInstall.exe *" and CommandLine:"*.inf*")

name: SIGMA - File Download Via InstallUtil.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects use of .NET InstallUtil.exe in order to download arbitrary files. The files will be written to "%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\IE\"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\InstallUtil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"InstallUtil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp://*", "*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution of InstallUtil Without Log
author: frack113
description: Uses the .NET InstallUtil.exe application in order to execute image without log
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\InstallUtil.exe" and Image:"*Microsoft.NET\\Framework*" and (CommandLine:"*/logfile= *" and CommandLine:"*/LogToConsole=false*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Shells Spawn by Java Utility Keytool
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects suspicious shell spawn from Java utility keytool process (e.g. adselfservice plus exploitation)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\keytool.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\bash.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\whoami.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\scrcons.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\hh.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\forfiles.exe", "*\\scriptrunner.exe", "*\\mftrace.exe", "*\\AppVLP.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\query.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process Of Manage Engine ServiceDesk
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of the "Manage Engine ServiceDesk Plus" Java web service
false_positives: Legitimate sub processes started by Manage Engine ServiceDesk Pro
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage:"*\\ManageEngine\\ServiceDesk\\*" and ParentImage:"*\\java.exe*") and (Image like~ ("*\\AppVLP.exe", "*\\bash.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\calc.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\curl.exe", "*\\forfiles.exe", "*\\mftrace.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\query.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\scrcons.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\whoami.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))) and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe")) and CommandLine:"* stop*")))

name: SIGMA - Java Running with Remote Debugging
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a JAVA process running with remote debugging allowing more than just localhost to connect
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*transport=dt_socket,address=*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*jre1.*", "*jdk1.*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*address=127.0.0.1*", "*address=localhost*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Processes Spawned by Java.EXE
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), Florian Roth
description: Detects suspicious processes spawned from a Java host process which could indicate a sign of exploitation (e.g. log4j)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\java.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\AppVLP.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\curl.exe", "*\\forfiles.exe", "*\\hh.exe", "*\\mftrace.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\query.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\scrcons.exe", "*\\scriptrunner.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\whoami.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Shell Process Spawned by Java.EXE
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects shell spawned from Java host process, which could be a sign of exploitation (e.g. log4j exploitation)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\java.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\bash.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (not (ParentImage:"*build*" and CommandLine:"*build*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious SysAidServer Child
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of SysAidServer (as seen in MERCURY threat actor intrusions)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\java.exe", "*\\javaw.exe")) and ParentCommandLine:"*SysAidServer*")

name: SIGMA - JScript Compiler Execution
author: frack113
description:
Detects the execution of the "jsc.exe" (JScript Compiler).
Attacker might abuse this in order to compile JScript files on the fly and bypassing application whitelisting.
false_positives: Legitimate use to compile JScript by developers.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\jsc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"jsc.exe")

name: SIGMA - Kavremover Dropped Binary LOLBIN Usage
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a signed binary dropped by Kaspersky Lab Products Remover (kavremover) which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute arbitrary commands and binaries.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* run run-cmd *" and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*\\cleanapi.exe", "*\\kavremover.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Windows Kernel Debugger Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the Windows Kernel Debugger "kd.exe".
false_positives: Rare occasions of legitimate cases where kernel debugging is necessary in production. Investigation is required
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\kd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"kd.exe")

name: SIGMA - Attempts of Kerberos Coercion Via DNS SPN Spoofing
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the presence of "UWhRC....AAYBAAAA" pattern in command line.
The pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA..BAAAA" is a base64-encoded signature that corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure.
Attackers can use this technique to coerce authentication from victim systems to attacker-controlled hosts.
It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack, where adversaries manipulate DNS records
to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like in CVE-2025-33073.
If you see this pattern in the command line, it is likely an attempt to add spoofed Service Principal Names (SPNs) to DNS records,
or checking for the presence of such records through the `nslookup` command.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*UWhRCA*" and CommandLine:"*BAAAA*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Process of KeyScrambler.exe
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of KeyScrambler.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\KeyScrambler.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "regsvr32.exe", "RUNDLL32.EXE", "wscript.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Computer Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects password change for the computer's domain account or host principal via "ksetup.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ksetup.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ksetup.exe") and CommandLine:"* /setcomputerpassword *")

name: SIGMA - Logged-On User Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects password change for the logged-on user's via "ksetup.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ksetup.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ksetup.exe") and CommandLine:"* /ChangePassword *")

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Structure Export Via Ldifde.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "ldifde.exe" in order to export organizational Active Directory structure.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\ldifde.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ldifde.exe") and CommandLine:"*-f*") and (not CommandLine:"* -i*"))

name: SIGMA - Import LDAP Data Interchange Format File Via Ldifde.EXE
author: @gott_cyber
description: Detects the execution of "Ldifde.exe" with the import flag "-i". The can be abused to include HTTP-based arguments which will allow the arbitrary download of files from a remote server.
false_positives: Since the content of the files are unknown, false positives are expected
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ldifde.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ldifde.exe") and (CommandLine:"*-i*" and CommandLine:"*-f*"))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Link.EXE Parent Process
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects an uncommon parent process of "LINK.EXE".
Link.EXE in Microsoft incremental linker. Its a utility usually bundled with Visual Studio installation.
Multiple utilities often found in the same folder (editbin.exe, dumpbin.exe, lib.exe, etc) have a hardcode call to the "LINK.EXE" binary without checking its validity.
This would allow an attacker to sideload any binary with the name "link.exe" if one of the aforementioned tools get executed from a different location.
By filtering the known locations of such utilities we can spot uncommon parent process of LINK.EXE that might be suspicious or malicious.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\link.exe" and CommandLine:"*LINK /*") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\*")) and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\VC\\bin\\*", "*\\VC\\Tools\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Rebuild Performance Counter Values Via Lodctr.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "lodctr.exe" to rebuild the performance counter registry values. This can be abused by attackers by providing a malicious config file to overwrite performance counter configuration to confuse and evade monitoring and security solutions.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\lodctr.exe" and OriginalFileName:"LODCTR.EXE") and CommandLine like~ ("* -r*", "* /r*", "* –r*", "* —r*", "* ―r*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Windows Trace ETW Session Tamper Via Logman.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of "logman" utility in order to disable or delete Windows trace sessions
false_positives: Installer tools that disable services, e.g. before log collection agent installation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\logman.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Logman.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*stop *", "*delete *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Circular Kernel Context Logger*", "*EventLog-*", "*SYSMON TRACE*", "*SysmonDnsEtwSession*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious CustomShellHost Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of CustomShellHost binary where the child isn't located in 'C:\Windows\explorer.exe'
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\CustomShellHost.exe" and (not Image:"C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe"))

name: SIGMA - LOLBAS Data Exfiltration by DataSvcUtil.exe
author: Ialle Teixeira @teixeira0xfffff, Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects when a user performs data exfiltration by using DataSvcUtil.exe
false_positives: DataSvcUtil.exe being used may be performed by a system administrator.
Verify whether the user identity, user agent, and/or hostname should be making changes in your environment.
DataSvcUtil.exe being executed from unfamiliar users should be investigated. If known behavior is causing false positives, it can be exempted from the rule.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*/in:*", "*/out:*", "*/uri:*")) and (Image:"*\\DataSvcUtil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"DataSvcUtil.exe"))

name: SIGMA - DeviceCredentialDeployment Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of DeviceCredentialDeployment to hide a process from view
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\DeviceCredentialDeployment.exe"

name: SIGMA - Devtoolslauncher.exe Executes Specified Binary
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community (rule), @_felamos (idea)
description: The Devtoolslauncher.exe executes other binary
false_positives: Legitimate use of devtoolslauncher.exe by legitimate user
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\devtoolslauncher.exe" and CommandLine:"*LaunchForDeploy*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Diantz Alternate Data Stream Execution
author: frack113
description: Compress target file into a cab file stored in the Alternate Data Stream (ADS) of the target file.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*diantz.exe*" and CommandLine:"*.cab*") and CommandLine regex~ ":[^\\]")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Diantz Download and Compress Into a CAB File
author: frack113
description: Download and compress a remote file and store it in a cab file on local machine.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*diantz.exe*" and CommandLine:"* \\\\*" and CommandLine:"*.cab*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Extrac32 Execution
author: frack113
description: Download or Copy file with Extrac32
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*extrac32.exe*" or Image:"*\\extrac32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"extrac32.exe") and CommandLine:"*.cab*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/C*", "*/Y*", "* \\\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Extrac32 Alternate Data Stream Execution
author: frack113
description: Extract data from cab file and hide it in an alternate data stream
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*extrac32.exe*" and CommandLine:"*.cab*") and CommandLine regex~ ":[^\\]")

name: SIGMA - Potential Reconnaissance Activity Via GatherNetworkInfo.VBS
author: blueteamer8699
description: Detects execution of the built-in script located in "C:\Windows\System32\gatherNetworkInfo.vbs". Which can be used to gather information about the target machine
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("cscript.exe", "wscript.exe"))) and CommandLine:"*gatherNetworkInfo.vbs*")

name: SIGMA - Gpscript Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of the LOLBIN gpscript, which executes logon or startup scripts configured in Group Policy
false_positives: Legitimate uses of logon scripts distributed via group policy
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\gpscript.exe" or OriginalFileName:"GPSCRIPT.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /logon*", "* /startup*"))) and (not ParentCommandLine:"C:\\windows\\system32\\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p -s gpsvc"))

name: SIGMA - Ie4uinit Lolbin Use From Invalid Path
author: frack113
description: Detect use of ie4uinit.exe to execute commands from a specially prepared ie4uinit.inf file from a directory other than the usual directories
false_positives: ViberPC updater calls this binary with the following commandline "ie4uinit.exe -ClearIconCache"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ie4uinit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"IE4UINIT.EXE") and (not ((CurrentDirectory like~ ("c:\\windows\\system32\\", "c:\\windows\\sysWOW64\\")) or (?CurrentDirectory == null))))

name: SIGMA - Launch-VsDevShell.PS1 Proxy Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the 'Launch-VsDevShell.ps1' Microsoft signed script to execute commands.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the script by a developer
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*Launch-VsDevShell.ps1*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*VsWherePath *", "*VsInstallationPath *")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Manage-bde.wsf Abuse To Proxy Execution
author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential abuse of the "manage-bde.wsf" script as a LOLBIN to proxy execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\wscript.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wscript.exe") and CommandLine:"*manage-bde.wsf*") or (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and ParentCommandLine:"*manage-bde.wsf*") and (not Image:"*\\cmd.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Mavinject Inject DLL Into Running Process
author: frack113, Florian Roth
description: Detects process injection using the signed Windows tool "Mavinject" via the "INJECTRUNNING" flag
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* /INJECTRUNNING *" and (not ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\AppVClient.exe"))

name: SIGMA - MpiExec Lolbin
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a certain command line flag combination used by mpiexec.exe LOLBIN from HPC pack that can be used to execute any other binary
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mpiexec.exe" or Hashes:"*IMPHASH=d8b52ef6aaa3a81501bdfff9dbb96217*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /n 1 *", "* -n 1 *")))

name: SIGMA - Execute Files with Msdeploy.exe
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description: Detects file execution using the msdeploy.exe lolbin
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*verb:sync*" and CommandLine:"*-source:RunCommand*" and CommandLine:"*-dest:runCommand*") and Image:"*\\msdeploy.exe")

name: SIGMA - Execute MSDT Via Answer File
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "msdt.exe" using an answer file which is simulating the legitimate way of calling msdt via "pcwrun.exe" (For example from the compatibility tab)
false_positives: Possible undocumented parents of "msdt" other than "pcwrun"
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\msdt.exe" and CommandLine:"*\\WINDOWS\\diagnostics\\index\\PCWDiagnostic.xml*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -af *", "* /af *"))) and (not ParentImage:"*\\pcwrun.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Use of OpenConsole
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of OpenConsole binary as a LOLBIN to launch other binaries to bypass application Whitelisting
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"OpenConsole.exe" or Image:"*\\OpenConsole.exe") and (not Image:"C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.WindowsTerminal*"))

name: SIGMA - OpenWith.exe Executes Specified Binary
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community (rule), @harr0ey (idea)
description: The OpenWith.exe executes other binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\OpenWith.exe" and CommandLine:"*/c*")

name: SIGMA - Use of Pcalua For Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detects execition of commands and binaries from the context of The program compatibility assistant (Pcalua.exe). This can be used as a LOLBIN in order to bypass application whitelisting.
false_positives: Legitimate use by a via a batch script or by an administrator.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\pcalua.exe" and CommandLine:"* -a*")

name: SIGMA - Indirect Command Execution By Program Compatibility Wizard
author: A. Sungurov , oscd.community
description: Detect indirect command execution via Program Compatibility Assistant pcwrun.exe
false_positives: Need to use extra processing with 'unique_count' / 'filter' to focus on outliers as opposed to commonly seen artifacts
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\pcwrun.exe"

name: SIGMA - Execute Pcwrun.EXE To Leverage Follina
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects indirect command execution via Program Compatibility Assistant "pcwrun.exe" leveraging the follina (CVE-2022-30190) vulnerability
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\pcwrun.exe" and CommandLine:"*../*")

name: SIGMA - Code Execution via Pcwutl.dll
author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community
description: Detects launch of executable by calling the LaunchApplication function from pcwutl.dll library.
false_positives: Use of Program Compatibility Troubleshooter Helper
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*pcwutl*" and CommandLine:"*LaunchApplication*"))

name: SIGMA - Execute Code with Pester.bat as Parent
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects code execution via Pester.bat (Pester - Powershell Modulte for testing)
false_positives: Legitimate use of Pester for writing tests for Powershell scripts and modules
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and ParentCommandLine:"*\\WindowsPowerShell\\Modules\\Pester\\*") and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*{ Invoke-Pester -EnableExit ;*", "*{ Get-Help \"*")))

name: SIGMA - Execute Code with Pester.bat
author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community
description: Detects code execution via Pester.bat (Pester - Powershell Modulte for testing)
false_positives: Legitimate use of Pester for writing tests for Powershell scripts and modules
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*Pester*" and CommandLine:"*Get-Help*")) or ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*pester*" and CommandLine:"*;*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*help*", "*\?*"))))

name: SIGMA - PrintBrm ZIP Creation of Extraction
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of the LOLBIN PrintBrm.exe, which can be used to create or extract ZIP files. PrintBrm.exe should not be run on a normal workstation.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\PrintBrm.exe" and (CommandLine:"* -f*" and CommandLine:"*.zip*"))

name: SIGMA - Pubprn.vbs Proxy Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of the 'Pubprn.vbs' Microsoft signed script to execute commands.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\pubprn.vbs*" and CommandLine:"*script:*")

name: SIGMA - DLL Execution via Rasautou.exe
author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community
description: Detects using Rasautou.exe for loading arbitrary .DLL specified in -d option and executes the export specified in -p.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rasautou.exe" or OriginalFileName:"rasdlui.exe") and (CommandLine:"* -d *" and CommandLine:"* -p *"))

name: SIGMA - REGISTER_APP.VBS Proxy Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of a Microsoft signed script 'REGISTER_APP.VBS' to register a VSS/VDS Provider as a COM+ application.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the script. Always investigate what's being registered to confirm if it's benign
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\register_app.vbs*" and CommandLine:"*-register*")

name: SIGMA - Use of Remote.exe
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: Remote.exe is part of WinDbg in the Windows SDK and can be used for AWL bypass and running remote files.
false_positives: Approved installs of Windows SDK with Debugging Tools for Windows (WinDbg).
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\remote.exe" or OriginalFileName:"remote.exe")

name: SIGMA - Replace.exe Usage
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of Replace.exe which can be used to replace file with another file
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\replace.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("*-a*", "*/a*", "*–a*", "*—a*", "*―a*"))

name: SIGMA - Lolbin Runexehelper Use As Proxy
author: frack113
description: Detect usage of the "runexehelper.exe" binary as a proxy to launch other programs
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\runexehelper.exe"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Runscripthelper.exe
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
description: Detects execution of powershell scripts via Runscripthelper.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Runscripthelper.exe" and CommandLine:"*surfacecheck*")

name: SIGMA - Use of Scriptrunner.exe
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: The "ScriptRunner.exe" binary can be abused to proxy execution through it and bypass possible whitelisting
false_positives: Legitimate use when App-v is deployed
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ScriptRunner.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ScriptRunner.exe") and CommandLine:"* -appvscript *")

name: SIGMA - Using SettingSyncHost.exe as LOLBin
author: Anton Kutepov, oscd.community
description: Detects using SettingSyncHost.exe to run hijacked binary

subtechnique.name: Path Interception by Search Order Hijacking

query: any where EventID:1 and ((not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*"))) and (ParentCommandLine:"*cmd.exe /c*" and ParentCommandLine:"*RoamDiag.cmd*" and ParentCommandLine:"*-outputpath*"))

name: SIGMA - Use Of The SFTP.EXE Binary As A LOLBIN
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of the "sftp.exe" binary as a LOLBIN by abusing the "-D" flag
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\sftp.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -D ..*", "* -D C:\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Certreq Command to Download
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious certreq execution taken from the LOLBAS examples, which can be abused to download (small) files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\certreq.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CertReq.exe") and (CommandLine:"* -Post *" and CommandLine:"* -config *" and CommandLine:"* http*" and CommandLine:"* C:\\windows\\win.ini *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Driver Install by pnputil.exe
author: Hai Vaknin @LuxNoBulIshit, Avihay eldad  @aloneliassaf, Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects when a possible suspicious driver is being installed via pnputil.exe lolbin
false_positives: Pnputil.exe being used may be performed by a system administrator.
Verify whether the user identity, user agent, and/or hostname should be making changes in your environment.
Pnputil.exe being executed from unfamiliar users should be investigated. If known behavior is causing false positives, it can be exempted from the rule.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*-i*", "*/install*", "*-a*", "*/add-driver*", "*.inf*")) and Image:"*\\pnputil.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious GrpConv Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the suspicious execution of a utility to convert Windows 3.x .grp files or for persistence purposes by malicious software or actors
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*grpconv.exe -o*", "*grpconv -o*"))

name: SIGMA - Dumping Process via Sqldumper.exe
author: Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community
description: Detects process dump via legitimate sqldumper.exe binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\sqldumper.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*0x0110*", "*0x01100:40*")))

name: SIGMA - SyncAppvPublishingServer Execute Arbitrary PowerShell Code
author: frack113
description: Executes arbitrary PowerShell code using SyncAppvPublishingServer.exe.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SyncAppvPublishingServer.exe" or OriginalFileName:"syncappvpublishingserver.exe") and CommandLine:"*\"n; *")

name: SIGMA - SyncAppvPublishingServer VBS Execute Arbitrary PowerShell Code
author: frack113
description: Executes arbitrary PowerShell code using SyncAppvPublishingServer.vbs
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\SyncAppvPublishingServer.vbs*" and CommandLine:"*;*")

name: SIGMA - Potential DLL Injection Or Execution Using Tracker.exe
author: Avneet Singh @v3t0_, oscd.community
description: Detects potential DLL injection and execution using "Tracker.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\tracker.exe" or Description:"Tracker") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /d *", "* /c *"))) and (not (CommandLine:"* /ERRORREPORT:PROMPT *" or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\Msbuild\\Current\\Bin\\MSBuild.exe", "*\\Msbuild\\Current\\Bin\\amd64\\MSBuild.exe")))))

name: SIGMA - Use of TTDInject.exe
author: frack113
description: Detects the executiob of TTDInject.exe, which is used by Windows 10 v1809 and newer to debug time travel (underlying call of tttracer.exe)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*ttdinject.exe" or OriginalFileName:"TTDInject.EXE")

name: SIGMA - Time Travel Debugging Utility Usage
author: Ensar Şamil, @sblmsrsn, @oscd_initiative
description: Detects usage of Time Travel Debugging Utility. Adversaries can execute malicious processes and dump processes, such as lsass.exe, via tttracer.exe.
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\tttracer.exe"

name: SIGMA - Lolbin Unregmp2.exe Use As Proxy
author: frack113
description: Detect usage of the "unregmp2.exe" binary as a proxy to launch a custom version of "wmpnscfg.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\unregmp2.exe" or OriginalFileName:"unregmp2.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -HideWMP*", "* /HideWMP*", "* –HideWMP*", "* —HideWMP*", "* ―HideWMP*"))

name: SIGMA - UtilityFunctions.ps1 Proxy Dll
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of a Microsoft signed script executing a managed DLL with PowerShell.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*UtilityFunctions.ps1*", "*RegSnapin *"))

name: SIGMA - Use of VisualUiaVerifyNative.exe
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: VisualUiaVerifyNative.exe is a Windows SDK that can be used for AWL bypass and is listed in Microsoft's recommended block rules.
false_positives: Legitimate testing of Microsoft UI parts.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\VisualUiaVerifyNative.exe" or OriginalFileName:"VisualUiaVerifyNative.exe")

name: SIGMA - Visual Basic Command Line Compiler Usage
author: Ensar Şamil, @sblmsrsn, @oscd_initiative
description: Detects successful code compilation via Visual Basic Command Line Compiler that utilizes Windows Resource to Object Converter.
false_positives: Utilization of this tool should not be seen in enterprise environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\vbc.exe" and Image:"*\\cvtres.exe")

name: SIGMA - Use of VSIISExeLauncher.exe
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: The "VSIISExeLauncher.exe" binary part of the Visual Studio/VS Code can be used to execute arbitrary binaries
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\VSIISExeLauncher.exe" or OriginalFileName:"VSIISExeLauncher.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -p *", "* -a *")))

name: SIGMA - Use of Wfc.exe
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: The Workflow Command-line Compiler can be used for AWL bypass and is listed in Microsoft's recommended block rules.
false_positives: Legitimate use by a software developer
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\wfc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wfc.exe")

name: SIGMA - Potential Register_App.Vbs LOLScript Abuse
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects potential abuse of the "register_app.vbs" script that is part of the Windows SDK. The script offers the capability to register new VSS/VDS Provider as a COM+ application. Attackers can use this to install malicious DLLs for persistence and execution.
false_positives: Other VB scripts that leverage the same starting command line flags
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("cscript.exe", "wscript.exe"))) and CommandLine:"*.vbs -register *")

name: SIGMA - Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS Process Clone
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects a suspicious LSASS process process clone that could be a sign of credential dumping activity
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe" and Image:"*\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe")

name: SIGMA - Potential Mftrace.EXE Abuse
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects child processes of the "Trace log generation tool for Media Foundation Tools" (Mftrace.exe) which can abused to execute arbitrary binaries.
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\mftrace.exe"

name: SIGMA - MMC20 Lateral Movement
author: @2xxeformyshirt (Security Risk Advisors) - rule; Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea)
description: Detects MMC20.Application Lateral Movement; specifically looks for the spawning of the parent MMC.exe with a command line of "-Embedding" as a child of svchost.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\svchost.exe" and Image:"*\\mmc.exe" and CommandLine:"*-Embedding*")

name: SIGMA - MMC Executing Files with Reversed Extensions Using RTLO Abuse
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects malicious behavior where the MMC utility (`mmc.exe`) executes files with reversed extensions caused by Right-to-Left Override (RLO) abuse, disguising them as document formats.
false_positives: Legitimate administrative actions using MMC to execute misnamed `.msc` files.
Unconventional but non-malicious usage of RLO or reversed extensions.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mmc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MMC.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*cod.msc*", "*fdp.msc*", "*ftr.msc*", "*lmth.msc*", "*slx.msc*", "*tdo.msc*", "*xcod.msc*", "*xslx.msc*", "*xtpp.msc*")))

name: SIGMA - MMC Spawning Windows Shell
author: Karneades, Swisscom CSIRT
description: Detects a Windows command line executable started from MMC
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\mmc.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\bash.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe")) or Image:"*\\BITSADMIN*"))

name: SIGMA - CodePage Modification Via MODE.COM To Russian Language
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description:
Detects a CodePage modification using the "mode.com" utility to Russian language.
This behavior has been used by threat actors behind Dharma ransomware.
false_positives: Russian speaking people changing the CodePage
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mode.com" or OriginalFileName:"MODE.COM") and ((CommandLine:"* con *" and CommandLine:"* cp *" and CommandLine:"* select=*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*=1251", "*=866"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Mofcomp Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of the "mofcomp" utility as a child of a suspicious shell or script running utility or by having a suspicious path in the commandline.
The "mofcomp" utility parses a file containing MOF statements and adds the classes and class instances defined in the file to the WMI repository.
Attackers abuse this utility to install malicious MOF scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\mofcomp.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mofcomp.exe") and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wsl.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\WINDOWS\\Temp\\*", "*%temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*%appdata%*")))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\wbem\\WmiPrvSE.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*" and CommandLine:"*.mof")) and (not (CommandLine:"*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*" and CommandLine:"*.mof")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via Defender Binaries
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects potential sideloading of "mpclient.dll" by Windows Defender processes ("MpCmdRun" and "NisSrv") from their non-default directory.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\MpCmdRun.exe", "*\\NisSrv.exe")) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Defender\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Security Client\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*", "C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - File Download Via Windows Defender MpCmpRun.EXE
author: Matthew Matchen
description: Detects the use of Windows Defender MpCmdRun.EXE to download files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"MpCmdRun.exe" or Image:"*\\MpCmdRun.exe" or CommandLine:"*MpCmdRun.exe*" or Description:"Microsoft Malware Protection Command Line Utility") and (CommandLine:"*DownloadFile*" and CommandLine:"*url*"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Definition Files Removed
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by removing Windows Defender Definition Files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\MpCmdRun.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MpCmdRun.exe") and (CommandLine:"* -RemoveDefinitions*" and CommandLine:"* -All*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Msbuild Execution By Uncommon Parent Process
author: frack113
description: Detects suspicious execution of 'Msbuild.exe' by a uncommon parent process
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\MSBuild.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSBuild.exe") and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*\\devenv.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\msbuild.exe", "*\\python.exe", "*\\explorer.exe", "*\\nuget.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Using Msdt.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects processes leveraging the "ms-msdt" handler or the "msdt.exe" binary to execute arbitrary commands as seen in the follina (CVE-2022-30190) vulnerability
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msdt.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msdt.exe") and (CommandLine:"*IT_BrowseForFile=*" or (CommandLine:"* PCWDiagnostic*" and CommandLine like~ ("* -af *", "* /af *", "* –af *", "* —af *", "* ―af *"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Cabinet File Execution Via Msdt.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), GossiTheDog, frack113
description: Detects execution of msdt.exe using the "cab" flag which could indicates suspicious diagcab files with embedded answer files leveraging CVE-2022-30190
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msdt.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msdt.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -cab *", "* /cab *", "* –cab *", "* —cab *", "* ―cab *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious MSDT Parent Process
author: Nextron Systems
description: Detects msdt.exe executed by a suspicious parent as seen in CVE-2022-30190 / Follina exploitation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\wsl.exe")) and (Image:"*\\msdt.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msdt.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via MSEDGE_PROXY.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects usage of "msedge_proxy.exe" to download arbitrary files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msedge_proxy.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msedge_proxy.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Remotely Hosted HTA File Executed Via Mshta.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "mshta" utility with an argument containing the "http" keyword, which could indicate that an attacker is executing a remotely hosted malicious hta file
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mshta.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSHTA.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*", "*ftp://*")))

name: SIGMA - Wscript Shell Run In CommandLine
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the presence of the keywords "Wscript", "Shell" and "Run" in the command, which could indicate a suspicious activity
false_positives: Inline scripting can be used by some rare third party applications or administrators. Investigate and apply additional filters accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*Wscript.*" and CommandLine:"*.Shell*" and CommandLine:"*.Run*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious JavaScript Execution Via Mshta.EXE
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detects execution of javascript code using "mshta.exe".
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mshta.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSHTA.EXE") and CommandLine:"*javascript*")

name: SIGMA - Potential LethalHTA Technique Execution
author: Markus Neis
description: Detects potential LethalHTA technique where the "mshta.exe" is spawned by an "svchost.exe" process
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\svchost.exe" and Image:"*\\mshta.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious MSHTA Child Process
author: Michael Haag
description: Detects a suspicious process spawning from an "mshta.exe" process, which could be indicative of a malicious HTA script execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\mshta.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\bash.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "Bash.exe", "reg.exe", "REGSVR32.EXE", "bitsadmin.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - MSHTA Execution with Suspicious File Extensions
author: Diego Perez (@darkquassar), Markus Neis, Swisscom (Improve Rule), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of mshta.exe with file types that looks like they do not typically represent HTA (HTML Application) content,
such as .png, .jpg, .zip, .pdf, and others, which are often polyglots. MSHTA is a legitimate Windows utility for executing HTML Applications
containing VBScript or JScript. Threat actors often abuse this lolbin utility to download and
execute malicious scripts disguised as benign files or hosted under misleading extensions to evade detection.
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mshta.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mshta.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.7z*", "*.avi*", "*.bat*", "*.bmp*", "*.conf*", "*.csv*", "*.dll*", "*.doc*", "*.gif*", "*.gz*", "*.ini*", "*.jpe*", "*.jpg*", "*.json*", "*.lnk*", "*.log*", "*.mkv*", "*.mp3*", "*.mp4*", "*.pdf*", "*.png*", "*.ppt*", "*.rar*", "*.rtf*", "*.svg*", "*.tar*", "*.tmp*", "*.txt*", "*.xls*", "*.xml*", "*.yaml*", "*.yml*", "*.zip*", "*vbscript*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Mshta.EXE Execution Patterns
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious mshta process execution patterns
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\mshta.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSHTA.EXE") and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))) or ((Image:"*\\mshta.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSHTA.EXE") and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*.htm*", "*.hta*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*mshta.exe", "*mshta"))))))

name: SIGMA - DllUnregisterServer Function Call Via Msiexec.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects MsiExec loading a DLL and calling its DllUnregisterServer function
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\msiexec.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -z *", "* /z *", "* –z *", "* —z *", "* ―z *") and CommandLine:"*.dll*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious MsiExec Embedding Parent
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy the execution of malicious payloads
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) and (ParentCommandLine:"*MsiExec.exe*" and ParentCommandLine:"*-Embedding *")) and (not ((Image:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\Program Files\\SplunkUniversalForwarder\\bin\\*") or (CommandLine:"*\\DismFoDInstall.cmd*" or (ParentCommandLine:"*\\MsiExec.exe -Embedding *" and ParentCommandLine:"*Global\\MSI0000*")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Msiexec Execute Arbitrary DLL
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy execution of malicious payloads.
Msiexec.exe is the command-line utility for the Windows Installer and is thus commonly associated with executing installation packages (.msi)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("* -y*", "* /y*", "* –y*", "* —y*", "* ―y*")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y \"C:\\Program Files\\Bonjour\\mdnsNSP.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Bonjour\\mdnsNSP.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Apple Software Update\\ScriptingObjectModel.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Apple Software Update\\SoftwareUpdateAdmin.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y \"C:\\Windows\\CCM\\*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" /Y C:\\Windows\\CCM\\*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y \"C:\\Program Files\\Bonjour\\mdnsNSP.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Bonjour\\mdnsNSP.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Apple Software Update\\ScriptingObjectModel.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Apple Software Update\\SoftwareUpdateAdmin.dll*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y \"C:\\Windows\\CCM\\*", "*\\MsiExec.exe\" -Y C:\\Windows\\CCM\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Msiexec Quiet Installation
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy execution of malicious payloads.
Msiexec.exe is the command-line utility for the Windows Installer and is thus commonly associated with executing installation packages (.msi)
false_positives: WindowsApps installing updates via the quiet flag
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msiexec.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-i*", "*/i*", "*–i*", "*—i*", "*―i*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-package*", "*/package*", "*–package*", "*—package*", "*―package*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-a*", "*/a*", "*–a*", "*—a*", "*―a*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-j*", "*/j*", "*–j*", "*—j*", "*―j*")) and CommandLine like~ ("*-q*", "*/q*", "*–q*", "*—q*", "*―q*")) and (not ((ParentImage:"C:\\Users\\*" and ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") or ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*" or (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\CCM\\Ccm32BitLauncher.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384"))))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Msiexec Quiet Install From Remote Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Msiexec.exe to install packages hosted remotely quietly
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"msiexec.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-i*", "*/i*", "*–i*", "*—i*", "*―i*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-package*", "*/package*", "*–package*", "*—package*", "*―package*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-a*", "*/a*", "*–a*", "*—a*", "*―a*") or CommandLine like~ ("*-j*", "*/j*", "*–j*", "*—j*", "*―j*")) and CommandLine like~ ("*-q*", "*/q*", "*–q*", "*—q*", "*―q*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http*", "*\\\\*"))) and (not (CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\OpenOffice*" and CommandLine:"*Installation Files\\openoffice*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential MsiExec Masquerading
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of msiexec.exe from an uncommon directory

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\msiexec.exe") and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - MsiExec Web Install
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious msiexec process starts with web addresses as parameter
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* msiexec*" and CommandLine:"*://*")

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via MSOHTMED.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "MSOHTMED" to download arbitrary files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\MSOHTMED.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MsoHtmEd.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp://*", "*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via MSPUB.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "MSPUB" (Microsoft Publisher) to download arbitrary files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\MSPUB.exe" or OriginalFileName:"MSPUB.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp://*", "*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Process Injection Via Msra.EXE
author: Alexander McDonald
description: Detects potential process injection via Microsoft Remote Asssistance (Msra.exe) by looking at suspicious child processes spawned from the aforementioned process. It has been a target used by many threat actors and used for discovery and persistence tactics
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\msra.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*msra.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\arp.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\netstat.exe", "*\\nslookup.exe", "*\\route.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\whoami.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Detection of PowerShell Execution via Sqlps.exe
author: Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community
description:
This rule detects execution of a PowerShell code through the sqlps.exe utility, which is included in the standard set of utilities supplied with the MSSQL Server.
Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.
false_positives: Direct PS command execution through SQLPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqlps.exe spawned by sqlagent.exe is a legitimate action.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\sqlps.exe" or ((Image:"*\\sqlps.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sqlps.exe") and (not ParentImage:"*\\sqlagent.exe")))

name: SIGMA - SQL Client Tools PowerShell Session Detection
author: Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.communitly
description:
This rule detects execution of a PowerShell code through the sqltoolsps.exe utility, which is included in the standard set of utilities supplied with the Microsoft SQL Server Management studio.
Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.
false_positives: Direct PS command execution through SQLToolsPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqltoolsps.exe spawned by smss.exe is a legitimate action.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sqltoolsps.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\sqltoolsps.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\sqltoolsps.exe") and (not ParentImage:"*\\smss.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process Of SQL Server
author: FPT.EagleEye Team, wagga
description: Detects suspicious child processes of the SQLServer process. This could indicate potential RCE or SQL Injection.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\sqlservr.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\bash.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\netstat.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\ping.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\tasklist.exe", "*\\wsl.exe"))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft SQL Server\\*" and ParentImage:"*DATEV_DBENGINE\\MSSQL\\Binn\\sqlservr.exe" and Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\cmd.exe\" *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process Of Veeam Dabatase
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of the Veeam service process. This could indicate potential RCE or SQL Injection.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\sqlservr.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*VEEAMSQL*") and (((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wsl.exe", "*\\wt.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-ex *", "*bypass*", "*cscript*", "*DownloadString*", "*http://*", "*https://*", "*mshta*", "*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*wscript*", "*copy *"))) or (Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\netstat.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\ping.exe", "*\\tasklist.exe", "*\\whoami.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential MSTSC Shadowing Activity
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects RDP session hijacking by using MSTSC shadowing
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*noconsentprompt*" and CommandLine:"*shadow:*")

name: SIGMA - New Remote Desktop Connection Initiated Via Mstsc.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Detects the usage of "mstsc.exe" with the "/v" flag to initiate a connection to a remote server.
Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a computer using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\mstsc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mstsc.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -v:*", "* /v:*", "* –v:*", "* —v:*", "* ―v:*")) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\lxss\\wslhost.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\WSL\\wslg.rdp*")))

name: SIGMA - Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Christopher Peacock @securepeacock
description: Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file
false_positives: Likely with legitimate usage of ".rdp" files
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\mstsc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mstsc.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.rdp", "*.rdp\""))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\lxss\\wslhost.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\WSL\\wslg.rdp*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file located in suspicious locations.
false_positives: Likelihood is related to how often the paths are used in the environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\mstsc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mstsc.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.rdp", "*.rdp\"")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\drivers\\color*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks_Migrated *", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tracing\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Mstsc.EXE Execution From Uncommon Parent
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file located in suspicious locations.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\CCleanerBrowser.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\chromium.exe", "*\\firefox.exe", "*\\iexplore.exe", "*\\microsoftedge.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe", "*\\whale.exe", "*\\outlook.exe")) and (Image:"*\\mstsc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"mstsc.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Msxsl.EXE Execution
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Detects the execution of the MSXSL utility. This can be used to execute Extensible Stylesheet Language (XSL) files. These files are commonly used to describe the processing and rendering of data within XML files.
Adversaries can abuse this functionality to execute arbitrary files while potentially bypassing application whitelisting defenses.
false_positives: Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\msxsl.exe"

name: SIGMA - Remote XSL Execution Via Msxsl.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects the execution of the "msxsl" binary with an "http" keyword in the command line. This might indicate a potential remote execution of XSL files.
false_positives: Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\msxsl.exe" and CommandLine:"*http*")

name: SIGMA - New Firewall Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
author: Markus Neis, Sander Wiebing
description: Detects the addition of a new rule to the Windows firewall via netsh
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and (CommandLine:"* firewall *" and CommandLine:"* add *")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow \"program=?:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dropbox\\Client\\Dropbox.exe\" enable=yes profile=Any*", "*advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow \"program=?:\\Program Files\\Dropbox\\Client\\Dropbox.exe\" enable=yes profile=Any*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Program Location Whitelisted In Firewall Via Netsh.EXE
author: Sander Wiebing, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects Netsh command execution that whitelists a program located in a suspicious location in the Windows Firewall
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*firewall*" and CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*allowedprogram*") or (CommandLine:"*advfirewall*" and CommandLine:"*firewall*" and CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*rule*" and CommandLine:"*action=allow*" and CommandLine:"*program=*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\$Recycle.bin\\*", "*:\\RECYCLER.BIN\\*", "*:\\RECYCLERS.BIN\\*", "*:\\SystemVolumeInformation\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Default\\*", "*:\\Users\\Desktop\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\addins\\*", "*:\\Windows\\cursors\\*", "*:\\Windows\\debug\\*", "*:\\Windows\\drivers\\*", "*:\\Windows\\fonts\\*", "*:\\Windows\\help\\*", "*:\\Windows\\system32\\tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Local Settings\\Temporary Internet Files\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet Files\\Content.Outlook\\*", "*%Public%\\*", "*%TEMP%*", "*%TMP%*")))

name: SIGMA - RDP Connection Allowed Via Netsh.EXE
author: Sander Wiebing
description: Detects usage of the netsh command to open and allow connections to port 3389 (RDP). As seen used by Sarwent Malware
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*firewall *" and CommandLine:"*add *" and CommandLine:"*tcp *" and CommandLine:"*3389*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*portopening*", "*allow*"))))

name: SIGMA - Firewall Rule Deleted Via Netsh.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects the removal of a port or application rule in the Windows Firewall configuration using netsh
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and (CommandLine:"*firewall*" and CommandLine:"*delete *")) and (not ((ParentImage:"*\\Dropbox.exe" and CommandLine:"*name=Dropbox*") or (ParentImage:"*\\instup.exe" and CommandLine:"*advfirewall firewall delete rule name=\"Avast Antivirus Admin Client\"*"))))

name: SIGMA - Firewall Disabled via Netsh.EXE
author: Fatih Sirin
description: Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*firewall*" and CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*opmode*" and CommandLine:"*disable*") or (CommandLine:"*advfirewall*" and CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*state*" and CommandLine:"*off*")))

name: SIGMA - Netsh Allow Group Policy on Microsoft Defender Firewall
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and (CommandLine:"*advfirewall*" and CommandLine:"*firewall*" and CommandLine:"*set*" and CommandLine:"*rule*" and CommandLine:"*group=*" and CommandLine:"*new*" and CommandLine:"*enable=Yes*"))

name: SIGMA - Firewall Configuration Discovery Via Netsh.EXE
author: frack113, Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io'
description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\netsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"netsh.exe") and ((CommandLine:"*netsh*" and CommandLine:"*show *" and CommandLine:"*firewall *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*config *", "*state *", "*rule *", "*name=all*"))))
