query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("powershell_ise.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*curl *", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "* irm *", "*iwr *", "*wget *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*://1*", "*://2*", "*://3*", "*://4*", "*://5*", "*://6*", "*://7*", "*://8*", "*://9*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Invoke-WebRequest Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious call to Invoke-WebRequest cmdlet where the and output is located in a suspicious location
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("powershell_ise.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*curl *", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*iwr *", "*wget *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -ur*", "* -o*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*%AppData%*", "*%Public%*", "*%Temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*:\\Windows\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Mailbox Export to Share
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the powerShell New-MailboxExportRequest Cmdlet to exports a mailbox to a remote or local share, as used in ProxyShell exploitations
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*New-MailboxExportRequest*" and CommandLine:"* -Mailbox *" and CommandLine:"* -FilePath \\\\*")

name: SIGMA - Malicious PowerShell Commandlets - ProcessCreation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*Add-Exfiltration*", "*Add-Persistence*", "*Add-RegBackdoor*", "*Add-RemoteRegBackdoor*", "*Add-ScrnSaveBackdoor*", "*Check-VM*", "*ConvertTo-Rc4ByteStream*", "*Decrypt-Hash*", "*Disable-ADIDNSNode*", "*Disable-MachineAccount*", "*Do-Exfiltration*", "*Enable-ADIDNSNode*", "*Enable-MachineAccount*", "*Enabled-DuplicateToken*", "*Exploit-Jboss*", "*Export-ADR*", "*Export-ADRCSV*", "*Export-ADRExcel*", "*Export-ADRHTML*", "*Export-ADRJSON*", "*Export-ADRXML*", "*Find-Fruit*", "*Find-GPOLocation*", "*Find-TrustedDocuments*", "*Get-ADIDNS*", "*Get-ApplicationHost*", "*Get-ChromeDump*", "*Get-ClipboardContents*", "*Get-FoxDump*", "*Get-GPPPassword*", "*Get-IndexedItem*", "*Get-KerberosAESKey*", "*Get-Keystrokes*", "*Get-LSASecret*", "*Get-MachineAccountAttribute*", "*Get-MachineAccountCreator*", "*Get-PassHashes*", "*Get-RegAlwaysInstallElevated*", "*Get-RegAutoLogon*", "*Get-RemoteBootKey*", "*Invoke-winPEAS*", "*Invoke-WireTap*", "*Invoke-WmiCommand*", "*Invoke-WMIExec*", "*Invoke-WScriptBypassUAC*", "*Invoke-Zerologon*", "*MailRaider*", "*New-ADIDNSNode*", "*New-DNSRecordArray*", "*Remove-ADIDNSNode*", "*Remove-MachineAccount*", "*Remove-Update*", "*Rename-ADIDNSNode*", "*Revoke-ADIDNSPermission*", "*Set-ADIDNSNode*", "*Set-MacAttribute*", "*Set-MachineAccountAttribute*", "*Set-Wallpaper*", "*Show-TargetScreen*", "*Start-CaptureServer*", "*Start-Dnscat2*", "*Start-WebcamRecorder*", "*Veeam-Get-Creds*", "*VolumeShadowCopyTools*"))

name: SIGMA - MSExchange Transport Agent Installation
author: Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the Installation of a Exchange Transport Agent
false_positives: Legitimate installations of exchange TransportAgents. AssemblyPath is a good indicator for this.
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*Install-TransportAgent*"

name: SIGMA - Non Interactive PowerShell Process Spawned
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g (rule), oscd.community (improvements)
description: Detects non-interactive PowerShell activity by looking at the "powershell" process with a non-user GUI process such as "explorer.exe" as a parent.
false_positives: Likely. Many admin scripts and tools leverage PowerShell in their BAT or VB scripts which may trigger this rule often. It is best to add additional filters or use this to hunt for anomalies
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*:\\Windows\\explorer.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\CompatTelRunner.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\explorer.exe")) or ParentImage:":\\$WINDOWS.~BT\\Sources\\SetupHost.exe")) and (not ((ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Microsoft VS Code\\Code.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"* --ms-enable-electron-run-as-node *") or (ParentImage:"*:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.WindowsTerminal_*" and ParentImage:"*\\WindowsTerminal.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential PowerShell Obfuscation Via WCHAR/CHAR
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious encoded character syntax often used for defense evasion
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*[char]0x*", "*(WCHAR)0x*"))

name: SIGMA - Execution of Powershell Script in Public Folder
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: This rule detects execution of PowerShell scripts located in the "C:\Users\Public" folder
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-f C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-f \"C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-f %Public%*", "*-fi C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-fi \"C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-fi %Public%*", "*-fil C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-fil \"C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-fil %Public%*", "*-file C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-file \"C:\\Users\\Public*", "*-file %Public%*")))

name: SIGMA - RemoteFXvGPUDisablement Abuse Via AtomicTestHarnesses
author: frack113
description: Detects calls to the AtomicTestHarnesses "Invoke-ATHRemoteFXvGPUDisablementCommand" which is designed to abuse the "RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.exe" binary to run custom PowerShell code via module load-order hijacking.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-ATHRemoteFXvGPUDisablementCommand*", "*Invoke-ATHRemoteFXvGPUDisableme*"))

name: SIGMA - Tamper Windows Defender Remove-MpPreference
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects attempts to remove Windows Defender configurations using the 'MpPreference' cmdlet
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*Remove-MpPreference*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-ControlledFolderAccessProtectedFolders *", "*-AttackSurfaceReductionRules_Ids *", "*-AttackSurfaceReductionRules_Actions *", "*-CheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningScan *")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Powershell ReverseShell Connection
author: FPT.EagleEye, wagga, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "TcpClient" class. Which can be abused to establish remote connections and reverse-shells. As seen used by the Nishang "Invoke-PowerShellTcpOneLine" reverse shell and other.
false_positives: In rare administrative cases, this function might be used to check network connectivity
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (CommandLine:"* Net.Sockets.TCPClient*" and CommandLine:"*.GetStream(*" and CommandLine:"*.Write(*"))

name: SIGMA - Run PowerShell Script from ADS
author: Sergey Soldatov, Kaspersky Lab, oscd.community
description: Detects PowerShell script execution from Alternate Data Stream (ADS)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine:"*Get-Content*" and CommandLine:"*-Stream*"))

name: SIGMA - Run PowerShell Script from Redirected Input Stream
author: Moriarty Meng (idea), Anton Kutepov (rule), oscd.community
description: Detects PowerShell script execution via input stream redirect
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and CommandLine regex~ "\s-\s*<")

name: SIGMA - PowerShell SAM Copy
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell scripts accessing SAM hives
false_positives: PowerShell scripts fixing HiveNightmare / SeriousSAM ACLs
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*\\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy*" and CommandLine:"*System32\\config\\sam*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Copy-Item*", "*cp $_.*", "*cpi $_.*", "*copy $_.*", "*.File]::Copy(*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Invocation From Script Engines
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious powershell invocations from interpreters or unusual programs
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (not CurrentDirectory:"*\\Health Service State\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service DACL Modification Via Set-Service Cmdlet
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications via the "Set-Service" cmdlet using the "SecurityDescriptorSddl" flag (Only available with PowerShell 7) that can be used to hide services or make them unstopable
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\pwsh.exe" or OriginalFileName:"pwsh.dll") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-SecurityDescriptorSddl *", "*-sd *")) and ((CommandLine:"*Set-Service *" and CommandLine:"*D;;*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*;;;IU*", "*;;;SU*", "*;;;BA*", "*;;;SY*", "*;;;WD*"))))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Script Change Permission Via Set-Acl
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell execution to set the ACL of a file or a folder
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (CommandLine:"*Set-Acl *" and CommandLine:"*-AclObject *" and CommandLine:"*-Path *"))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Set-Acl On Windows Folder
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts to set the ACL to a file in the Windows folder
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (CommandLine:"*Set-Acl *" and CommandLine:"*-AclObject *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-Path \"C:\\Windows*", "*-Path 'C:\\Windows*", "*-Path %windir%*", "*-Path $env:windir*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*FullControl*", "*Allow*")))

name: SIGMA - Change PowerShell Policies to an Insecure Level
author: frack113
description: Detects changing the PowerShell script execution policy to a potentially insecure level using the "-ExecutionPolicy" flag.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((OriginalFileName like~ ("powershell_ise.exe", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or (Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-executionpolicy *", "* -ep *", "* -exec *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Bypass*", "*Unrestricted*"))) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\msiexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\*", "*-NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\PowerShell\\7\\*")))) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*C:\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\Avast\\*", "*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\Avast\\*", "*\\instup.exe*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-ExecutionPolicy ByPass -File \"C:\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\Avast*", "*-ExecutionPolicy ByPass -File \"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\Avast\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Service StartupType Change Via PowerShell Set-Service
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the PowerShell "Set-Service" cmdlet to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "manual"
false_positives: False positives may occur with troubleshooting scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\powershell.exe" or OriginalFileName:"PowerShell.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*Set-Service*" and CommandLine:"*-StartupType*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Disabled*", "*Manual*"))))

name: SIGMA - Deletion of Volume Shadow Copies via WMI with PowerShell
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects deletion of Windows Volume Shadow Copies with PowerShell code and Get-WMIObject. This technique is used by numerous ransomware families such as Sodinokibi/REvil
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*Get-WmiObject*", "*gwmi*", "*Get-CimInstance*", "*gcim*")) and CommandLine:"*Win32_ShadowCopy*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*.Delete()*", "*Remove-WmiObject*", "*rwmi*", "*Remove-CimInstance*", "*rcim*")))

name: SIGMA - Exchange PowerShell Snap-Ins Usage
author: FPT.EagleEye, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects adding and using Exchange PowerShell snap-ins to export mailbox data. As seen used by HAFNIUM and APT27
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and CommandLine:"*Add-PSSnapin*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*Microsoft.Exchange.Powershell.Snapin*", "*Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn*"))) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe" and CommandLine:"*$exserver=Get-ExchangeServer ([Environment]::MachineName) -ErrorVariable exerr 2> $null*")))

name: SIGMA - Stop Windows Service Via PowerShell Stop-Service
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the stopping of a Windows service via the PowerShell Cmdlet "Stop-Service"
false_positives: There are many legitimate reasons to stop a service. This rule isn't looking for any suspicious behaviour in particular. Filter legitimate activity accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) and CommandLine:"*Stop-Service *")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Download and Execute Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download patterns that are often used in malicious scripts, stagers or downloaders (make sure that your backend applies the strings case-insensitive)
false_positives: Software installers that pull packages from remote systems and execute them
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*IEX ((New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString*", "*IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString*", "*IEX((New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString*", "*IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString*", "* -command (New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadFile(*", "* -c (New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadFile(*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Parameter Substring
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Daniel Bohannon (idea), Roberto Rodriguez (Fix)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation with a parameter substring
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -windowstyle h *", "* -windowstyl h*", "* -windowsty h*", "* -windowst h*", "* -windows name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Parent Process
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Harish Segar
description: Detects a suspicious or uncommon parent processes of PowerShell
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*tomcat*" or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\amigo.exe", "*\\browser.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\firefox.exe", "*\\httpd.exe", "*\\iexplore.exe", "*\\jbosssvc.exe", "*\\microsoftedge.exe", "*\\microsoftedgecp.exe", "*\\MicrosoftEdgeSH.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\nginx.exe", "*\\outlook.exe", "*\\php-cgi.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\safari.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\sqlagent.exe", "*\\sqlserver.exe", "*\\sqlservr.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe", "*\\w3wp.exe"))) and ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*/c powershell*", "*/c pwsh*")) or Description:"Windows PowerShell" or Product:"PowerShell Core 6" or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Script Run in AppData
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that invokes PowerShell with reference to an AppData folder
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*powershell.exe*", "*\\powershell*", "*\\pwsh*", "*pwsh.exe*")) and ((CommandLine:"*/c *" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Local\\*", "*Roaming\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Powershell Token Obfuscation - Process Creation
author: frack113
description: Detects TOKEN OBFUSCATION technique from Invoke-Obfuscation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine regex~ "\w+`(\w+|-|.)`[\w+|\s]" or CommandLine regex~ ""(\{\d\})+"\s*-f" or CommandLine regex~ "(?i)\$\{`?e`?n`?v`?:`?p`?a`?t`?h`?\}") and (not CommandLine:"*${env:path}*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Uninstall of Windows Defender Feature via PowerShell
author: yxinmiracle
description: Detects the use of PowerShell with Uninstall-WindowsFeature or Remove-WindowsFeature cmdlets to disable or remove the Windows Defender GUI feature, a common technique used by adversaries to evade defenses.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell_ISE.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Uninstall-WindowsFeature*", "*Remove-WindowsFeature*")) and CommandLine:"*Windows-Defender*")

name: SIGMA - User Discovery And Export Via Get-ADUser Cmdlet
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Get-ADUser cmdlet to collect user information and output it to a file
false_positives: Legitimate admin scripts may use the same technique, it's better to exclude specific computers or users who execute these commands or scripts often
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and ((CommandLine:"*Get-ADUser *" and CommandLine:"* -Filter \**") and (CommandLine like~ ("* > *", "* | Select *", "*Out-File*", "*Set-Content*", "*Add-Content*"))))

name: SIGMA - Net WebClient Casing Anomalies
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell command line contents that include a suspicious abnormal casing in the Net.Webclient (e.g. nEt.WEbCliEnT) string as used in obfuscation techniques
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*TgBlAFQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AdwBFAEIA*", "*OAGUAVAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*bgBFAHQALgB3AGUAYg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AdwBlAGIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAHcAZQBiA*", "*TgBFAHQALgB3AGUAYg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAHcAZQBiA*", "*bgBlAFQALgB3AGUAYg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AdwBlAGIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAHcAZQBiA*", "*TgBlAFQALgB3AGUAYg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAHcAZQBiA*", "*bgBFAFQALgB3AGUAYg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AdwBlAGIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAHcAZQBiA*", "*bgBlAHQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AVwBlAGIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*bgBFAHQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AVwBlAGIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*TgBFAHQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*bgBlAFQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AVwBlAGIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*TgBlAFQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*bgBFAFQALgBXAGUAYg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AVwBlAGIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAFcAZQBiA*", "*bgBlAHQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AdwBFAGIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*TgBlAHQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*OAGUAdAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*bgBFAHQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AdwBFAGIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*TgBFAHQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*bgBlAFQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AdwBFAGIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*TgBlAFQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*bgBFAFQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AdwBFAGIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*TgBFAFQALgB3AEUAYg*", "*OAEUAVAAuAHcARQBiA*", "*bgBlAHQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AVwBFAGIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*TgBlAHQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*OAGUAdAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*bgBFAHQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AVwBFAGIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*TgBFAHQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*bgBlAFQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AVwBFAGIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*TgBlAFQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*bgBFAFQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AVwBFAGIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*TgBFAFQALgBXAEUAYg*", "*OAEUAVAAuAFcARQBiA*", "*bgBlAHQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AdwBlAEIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*TgBlAHQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*OAGUAdAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*bgBFAHQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AdwBlAEIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*TgBFAHQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*bgBlAFQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AdwBlAEIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*TgBlAFQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*bgBFAFQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AdwBlAEIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*TgBFAFQALgB3AGUAQg*", "*OAEUAVAAuAHcAZQBCA*", "*bgBlAHQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AVwBlAEIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*TgBlAHQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*OAGUAdAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*bgBFAHQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AVwBlAEIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*TgBFAHQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*bgBlAFQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AVwBlAEIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*TgBlAFQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*bgBFAFQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AVwBlAEIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*TgBFAFQALgBXAGUAQg*", "*OAEUAVAAuAFcAZQBCA*", "*bgBlAHQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AdwBFAEIA*", "*uAGUAdAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*TgBlAHQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*OAGUAdAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*bgBFAHQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AdwBFAEIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*TgBFAHQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*bgBlAFQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*uAGUAVAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*bgBFAFQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AdwBFAEIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*TgBFAFQALgB3AEUAQg*", "*OAEUAVAAuAHcARQBCA*", "*TgBlAHQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*4AZQB0AC4AVwBFAEIA*", "*OAGUAdAAuAFcARQBCA*", "*bgBFAHQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*4ARQB0AC4AVwBFAEIA*", "*uAEUAdAAuAFcARQBCA*", "*TgBFAHQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*OAEUAdAAuAFcARQBCA*", "*bgBlAFQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*4AZQBUAC4AVwBFAEIA*", "*uAGUAVAAuAFcARQBCA*", "*TgBlAFQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*OAGUAVAAuAFcARQBCA*", "*bgBFAFQALgBXAEUAQg*", "*4ARQBUAC4AVwBFAEIA*", "*uAEUAVAAuAFcARQBCA*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious X509Enrollment - Process Creation
author: frack113
description: Detect use of X509Enrollment
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*X509Enrollment.CBinaryConverter*", "*884e2002-217d-11da-b2a4-000e7bbb2b09*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious XOR Encoded PowerShell Command
author: Sami Ruohonen, Harish Segar, Tim Shelton, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Vasiliy Burov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects presence of a potentially xor encoded powershell command
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll")) or Description:"Windows PowerShell" or Product:"PowerShell Core 6") and CommandLine:"*bxor*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*ForEach*", "*for(*", "*for *", "*-join *", "*-join'*", "*-join\"*", "*-join`*", "*::Join*", "*[char]*")))

name: SIGMA - Folder Compress To Potentially Suspicious Output Via Compress-Archive Cmdlet
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath $env:TEMP*", "*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via PresentationHost.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "PresentationHost" which is a utility that runs ".xbap" (Browser Applications) files to download arbitrary files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\presentationhost.exe" or OriginalFileName:"PresentationHost.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*http://*", "*https://*", "*ftp://*")))

name: SIGMA - XBAP Execution From Uncommon Locations Via PresentationHost.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of ".xbap" (Browser Applications) files via PresentationHost.EXE from an uncommon location. These files can be abused to run malicious ".xbap" files any bypass AWL
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\presentationhost.exe" or OriginalFileName:"PresentationHost.exe") and CommandLine:"*.xbap*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* C:\\Windows\\*", "* C:\\Program Files*"))))

name: SIGMA - Visual Studio NodejsTools PressAnyKey Arbitrary Binary Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects child processes of Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe that can be used to execute any other binary
false_positives: Legitimate use by developers as part of NodeJS development with Visual Studio Tools
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe"

name: SIGMA - Abusing Print Executable
author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative
description: Attackers can use print.exe for remote file copy
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\print.exe" and CommandLine:"print*" and (CommandLine:"*/D*" and CommandLine:"*.exe*")) and (not CommandLine:"*print.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - File Download Using ProtocolHandler.exe
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of "ProtocolHandler" to download files. Downloaded files will be located in the cache folder (for example - %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\IE)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\protocolhandler.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ProtocolHandler.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp://*", "*http://*", "*https://*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Provlaunch.EXE Binary Proxy Execution Abuse
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects child processes of "provlaunch.exe" which might indicate potential abuse to proxy execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\provlaunch.exe" and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Temp\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Provlaunch.EXE Child Process
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of "provlaunch.exe" which might indicate potential abuse to proxy execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\provlaunch.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Temp\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Screen Capture Activity Via Psr.EXE
author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description: Detects execution of Windows Problem Steps Recorder (psr.exe), a utility used to record the user screen and clicks.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Psr.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/start*", "*-start*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - 3Proxy Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of 3proxy, a tiny free proxy server
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\3proxy.exe" or Description:"3proxy - tiny proxy server" or CommandLine:"*.exe -i127.0.0.1 -p*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Suspicious ActiveDirectory Enumeration Via AdFind.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects active directory enumeration activity using known AdFind CLI flags
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*lockoutduration*", "*lockoutthreshold*", "*lockoutobservationwindow*", "*maxpwdage*", "*minpwdage*", "*minpwdlength*", "*pwdhistorylength*", "*pwdproperties*")) or CommandLine:"*-sc admincountdmp*" or CommandLine:"*-sc exchaddresses*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - AdFind.EXE Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of Adfind.exe utility, which can be used for reconnaissance in an Active Directory environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\AdFind.exe" or OriginalFileName:"AdFind.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=d144de8117df2beceaba2201ad304764*", "*IMPHASH=12ce1c0f3f5837ecc18a3782408fa975*", "*IMPHASH=bca5675746d13a1f246e2da3c2217492*", "*IMPHASH=4fbf3f084fbbb2470b80b2013134df35*", "*IMPHASH=49b639b4acbecc49d72a01f357aa4930*", "*IMPHASH=53e117a96057eaf19c41380d0e87f1c2*", "*IMPHASH=680dad9e300346e05a85023965867201*", "*IMPHASH=21aa085d54992511b9f115355e468782*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - AdFind Suspicious Execution
author: Janantha Marasinghe (https://github.com/blueteam0ps), FPT.EagleEye Team, omkar72, oscd.community
description: Detects AdFind execution with common flags seen used during attacks
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*domainlist*", "*trustdmp*", "*dcmodes*", "*adinfo*", "* dclist *", "*computer_pwdnotreqd*", "*objectcategory=*", "*-subnets -f*", "*name=\"Domain Admins\"*", "*-sc u:*", "*domainncs*", "*dompol*", "* oudmp *", "*subnetdmp*", "*gpodmp*", "*fspdmp*", "*users_noexpire*", "*computers_active*", "*computers_pwdnotreqd*"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - AdvancedRun Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of AdvancedRun utility
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"AdvancedRun.exe" or (CommandLine:"* /EXEFilename *" and CommandLine:"* /Run*") or (CommandLine:"* /WindowState 0*" and CommandLine:"* /RunAs *" and CommandLine:"* /CommandLine *"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - AdvancedRun Suspicious Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of AdvancedRun utility in the context of the TrustedInstaller, SYSTEM, Local Service or Network Service accounts
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*/EXEFilename*", "*/CommandLine*")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("* /RunAs 8 *", "* /RunAs 4 *", "* /RunAs 10 *", "* /RunAs 11 *")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*/RunAs 8", "*/RunAs 4", "*/RunAs 10", "*/RunAs 11"))))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Advanced IP Scanner Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @ROxPinTeddy
description: Detects the use of Advanced IP Scanner. Seems to be a popular tool for ransomware groups.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\advanced_ip_scanner*" or OriginalFileName:"*advanced_ip_scanner*" or Description:"*Advanced IP Scanner*") or (CommandLine:"*/portable*" and CommandLine:"*/lng*"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Advanced Port Scanner Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of Advanced Port Scanner.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\advanced_port_scanner*" or OriginalFileName:"*advanced_port_scanner*" or Description:"*Advanced Port Scanner*") or (CommandLine:"*/portable*" and CommandLine:"*/lng*"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Chisel Tunneling Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Chisel tunneling tool via the commandline arguments
false_positives: Some false positives may occur with other tools with similar commandlines
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\chisel.exe" or ((CommandLine like~ ("*exe client *", "*exe server *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-socks5*", "*-reverse*", "* r:*", "*:127.0.0.1:*", "*-tls-skip-verify *", "*:socks*"))))

name: SIGMA - PUA - CleanWipe Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of CleanWipe a tool usually used to delete Symantec antivirus.
false_positives: Legitimate administrative use (Should be investigated either way)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SepRemovalToolNative_x64.exe" or (Image:"*\\CATClean.exe" and CommandLine:"*--uninstall*") or (Image:"*\\NetInstaller.exe" and CommandLine:"*-r*") or (Image:"*\\WFPUnins.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/uninstall*" and CommandLine:"*/enterprise*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Crassus Execution
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Crassus, a Windows privilege escalation discovery tool, based on PE metadata characteristics.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\Crassus.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Crassus.exe" or Description:"*Crassus*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - CsExec Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the lesser known remote execution tool named CsExec a PsExec alternative
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\csexec.exe" or Description:"csexec")

name: SIGMA - PUA - DefenderCheck Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of DefenderCheck, a tool to evaluate the signatures used in Microsoft Defender. It can be used to figure out the strings / byte chains used in Microsoft Defender to detect a tool and thus used for AV evasion.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\DefenderCheck.exe" or Description:"DefenderCheck")

name: SIGMA - PUA - DIT Snapshot Viewer
author: Furkan Caliskan (@caliskanfurkan_)
description: Detects the use of Ditsnap tool, an inspection tool for Active Directory database, ntds.dit.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\ditsnap.exe" or CommandLine:"*ditsnap.exe*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Fast Reverse Proxy (FRP) Execution
author: frack113, Florian Roth
description: Detects the use of Fast Reverse Proxy. frp is a fast reverse proxy to help you expose a local server behind a NAT or firewall to the Internet.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\frpc.exe", "*\\frps.exe")) or CommandLine:"*\\frpc.ini*" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=7D9C233B8C9E3F0EA290D2B84593C842*", "*SHA1=06DDC9280E1F1810677935A2477012960905942F*", "*SHA256=57B0936B8D336D8E981C169466A15A5FD21A7D5A2C7DAF62D5E142EE860E387C*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA- IOX Tunneling Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of IOX - a tool for port forwarding and intranet proxy purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\iox.exe" or (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe fwd -l *", "*.exe fwd -r *", "*.exe proxy -l *", "*.exe proxy -r *")) or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=9DB2D314DD3F704A02051EF5EA210993*", "*SHA1=039130337E28A6623ECF9A0A3DA7D92C5964D8DD*", "*SHA256=C6CF82919B809967D9D90EA73772A8AA1C1EB3BC59252D977500F64F1A0D6731*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Mouse Lock Execution
author: Cian Heasley
description: In Kaspersky's 2020 Incident Response Analyst Report they listed legitimate tool "Mouse Lock" as being used for both credential access and collection in security incidents.
false_positives: Legitimate uses of Mouse Lock software
query: any where EventID:1 and (Product:"*Mouse Lock*" or Company:"*Misc314*" or CommandLine:"*Mouse Lock_*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Netcat Suspicious Execution
author: frack113, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of Netcat. Adversaries may use a non-application layer protocol for communication between host and C2 server or among infected hosts within a network
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\nc.exe", "*\\ncat.exe", "*\\netcat.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("* -lvp *", "* -lvnp*", "* -l -v -p *", "* -lv -p *", "* -l --proxy-type http *", "* -vnl --exec *", "* -vnl -e *", "* --lua-exec *", "* --sh-exec *")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - SoftPerfect Netscan Execution
author: @d4ns4n_ (Wuerth-Phoenix)
description:
Detects usage of SoftPerfect's "netscan.exe". An application for scanning networks.
It is actively used in-the-wild by threat actors to inspect and understand the network architecture of a victim.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\netscan.exe" or Product:"Network Scanner" or Description:"Application for scanning networks")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Ngrok Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of Ngrok, a utility used for port forwarding and tunneling, often used by threat actors to make local protected services publicly available.
Involved domains are bin.equinox.io for download and *.ngrok.io for connections.
false_positives: Another tool that uses the command line switches of Ngrok
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* tcp 139*", "* tcp 445*", "* tcp 3389*", "* tcp 5985*", "* tcp 5986*")) or (CommandLine:"* start *" and CommandLine:"*--all*" and CommandLine:"*--config*" and CommandLine:"*.yml*") or (Image:"*ngrok.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("* tcp *", "* http *", "* authtoken *"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe authtoken *", "*.exe start --all*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Nimgrab Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the usage of nimgrab, a tool bundled with the Nim programming framework and used for downloading files.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Nim on a developer systems
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\nimgrab.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=2DD44C3C29D667F5C0EF5F9D7C7FFB8B*", "*SHA256=F266609E91985F0FE3E31C5E8FAEEEC4FFA5E0322D8B6F15FE69F4C5165B9559*", "*IMPHASH=C07FDDD21D123EA9B3A08EEF44AAAC45*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - NimScan Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of NimScan, a portscanner utility.
In early 2025, adversaries were observed using this utility to scan for open ports on remote hosts in a compromised environment.
This rule identifies the execution of NimScan based on the process image name and specific hash values associated with different versions of the tool.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\NimScan.exe" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=41BB1C7571B3A724EB83A1D2B96DBB8C*", "*IMPHASH=B1B6ADACB172795480179EFD18A29549*", "*IMPHASH=0D1F896DC7642AD8384F9042F30279C2*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - NirCmd Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of NirCmd tool for command execution, which could be the result of legitimate administrative activity
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\NirCmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"NirCmd.exe") or (CommandLine like~ ("* execmd *", "*.exe script *", "*.exe shexec *", "* runinteractive *"))) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* exec *", "* exec2 *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* show *", "* hide *"))))

name: SIGMA - PUA - NirCmd Execution As LOCAL SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of NirCmd tool for command execution as SYSTEM user
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"* runassystem *"

name: SIGMA - PUA - Nmap/Zenmap Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of namp/zenmap. Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\nmap.exe", "*\\zennmap.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("nmap.exe", "zennmap.exe")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - NPS Tunneling Tool Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of NPS, a port forwarding and intranet penetration proxy server
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\npc.exe" or (CommandLine:"* -server=*" and CommandLine:"* -vkey=*" and CommandLine:"* -password=*") or CommandLine:"* -config=npc*" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=AE8ACF66BFE3A44148964048B826D005*", "*SHA1=CEA49E9B9B67F3A13AD0BE1C2655293EA3C18181*", "*SHA256=5A456283392FFCEEEACA3D3426C306EB470304637520D72FED1CC1FEBBBD6856*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - NSudo Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects the use of NSudo tool for command execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\NSudo.exe", "*\\NSudoLC.exe", "*\\NSudoLG.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("NSudo.exe", "NSudoLC.exe", "NSudoLG.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-U:S *", "*-U:T *", "*-U:E *", "*-P:E *", "*-M:S *", "*-M:H *", "*-U=S *", "*-U=T *", "*-U=E *", "*-P=E *", "*-M=S *", "*-M=H *", "*-ShowWindowMode:Hide*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - PingCastle Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects the execution of PingCastle, a tool designed to quickly assess the Active Directory security level.

name: SIGMA - PUA - PingCastle Execution From Potentially Suspicious Parent
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of PingCastle, a tool designed to quickly assess the Active Directory security level via a script located in a potentially suspicious or uncommon location.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentCommandLine like~ ("*.bat*", "*.chm*", "*.cmd*", "*.hta*", "*.htm*", "*.html*", "*.js*", "*.lnk*", "*.ps1*", "*.vbe*", "*.vbs*", "*.wsf*")) or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*")) or ((ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\Contacts\\*"))) and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*.bat*", "*.chm*", "*.cmd*", "*.hta*", "*.htm*", "*.html*", "*.js*", "*.lnk*", "*.ps1*", "*.vbe*", "*.vbs*", "*.wsf*")) and (Image:"*\\PingCastle.exe" or OriginalFileName:"PingCastle.exe" or Product:"Ping Castle" or (CommandLine like~ ("*--scanner aclcheck*", "*--scanner antivirus*", "*--scanner computerversion*", "*--scanner foreignusers*", "*--scanner laps_bitlocker*", "*--scanner localadmin*", "*--scanner nullsession*", "*--scanner nullsession-trust*", "*--scanner oxidbindings*", "*--scanner remote*", "*--scanner share*", "*--scanner smb*", "*--scanner smb3querynetwork*", "*--scanner spooler*", "*--scanner startup*", "*--scanner zerologon*")) or CommandLine:"*--no-enum-limit*" or (CommandLine:"*--healthcheck*" and CommandLine:"*--level Full*") or (CommandLine:"*--healthcheck*" and CommandLine:"*--server *")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Process Hacker Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of Process Hacker based on binary metadata information (Image, Hash, Imphash, etc).
Process Hacker is a tool to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level options.
Threat actors abused older vulnerable versions to manipulate system processes.
false_positives: While sometimes 'Process Hacker is used by legitimate administrators, the execution of Process Hacker must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\ProcessHacker_*" or Image:"*\\ProcessHacker.exe" or (OriginalFileName like~ ("ProcessHacker.exe", "Process Hacker")) or Description:"Process Hacker" or Product:"Process Hacker" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=68F9B52895F4D34E74112F3129B3B00D*", "*MD5=B365AF317AE730A67C936F21432B9C71*", "*SHA1=A0BDFAC3CE1880B32FF9B696458327CE352E3B1D*", "*SHA1=C5E2018BF7C0F314FED4FD7FE7E69FA2E648359E*", "*SHA256=D4A0FE56316A2C45B9BA9AC1005363309A3EDC7ACF9E4DF64D326A0FF273E80F*", "*SHA256=BD2C2CF0631D881ED382817AFCCE2B093F4E412FFB170A719E2762F250ABFEA4*", "*IMPHASH=3695333C60DEDECDCAFF1590409AA462*", "*IMPHASH=04DE0AD9C37EB7BD52043D2ECAC958DF*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Radmin Viewer Utility Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of Radmin which can be abused by an adversary to remotely control Windows machines
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"Radmin Viewer" or Product:"Radmin Viewer" or OriginalFileName:"Radmin.exe")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Potential PE Metadata Tamper Using Rcedit
author: Micah Babinski
description: Detects the use of rcedit to potentially alter executable PE metadata properties, which could conceal efforts to rename system utilities for defense evasion.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the tool by administrators or users to update metadata of a binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\rcedit-x64.exe", "*\\rcedit-x86.exe")) or Description:"Edit resources of exe" or Product:"rcedit") and CommandLine:"*--set-*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*OriginalFileName*", "*CompanyName*", "*FileDescription*", "*ProductName*", "*ProductVersion*", "*LegalCopyright*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Rclone Execution
author: Bhabesh Raj, Sittikorn S, Aaron Greetham (@beardofbinary) - NCC Group
description: Detects execution of RClone utility for exfiltration as used by various ransomwares strains like REvil, Conti, FiveHands, etc
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*--config *" and CommandLine:"*--no-check-certificate *" and CommandLine:"* copy *") or ((Image:"*\\rclone.exe" or Description:"Rsync for cloud storage") and (CommandLine like~ ("*pass*", "*user*", "*copy*", "*sync*", "*config*", "*lsd*", "*remote*", "*ls*", "*mega*", "*pcloud*", "*ftp*", "*ignore-existing*", "*auto-confirm*", "*transfers*", "*multi-thread-streams*", "*no-check-certificate *"))))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Restic Backup Tool Execution
author: Nounou Mbeiri, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of the Restic backup tool, which can be used for data exfiltration.
Threat actors may leverage Restic to back up and exfiltrate sensitive data to remote storage locations, including cloud services.
If not legitimately used in the enterprise environment, its presence may indicate malicious activity.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Restic for backup purposes within the organization.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*--password-file*" and CommandLine:"*init*" and CommandLine:"* -r *") or (CommandLine:"*--use-fs-snapshot*" and CommandLine:"*backup*" and CommandLine:"* -r *")) or ((CommandLine like~ ("*sftp:*", "*rest:http*", "*s3:s3.*", "*s3.http*", "*azure:*", "* gs:*", "*rclone:*", "*swift:*", "* b2:*")) and (CommandLine:"* init *" and CommandLine:"* -r *")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - RunXCmd Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the RunXCmd tool to execute commands with System or TrustedInstaller accounts
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* /account=system *", "* /account=ti *")) and CommandLine:"*/exec=*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Seatbelt Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the PUA/Recon tool Seatbelt via PE information of command line parameters
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\Seatbelt.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Seatbelt.exe" or Description:"Seatbelt" or (CommandLine like~ ("* DpapiMasterKeys*", "* InterestingProcesses*", "* InterestingFiles*", "* CertificateThumbprints*", "* ChromiumBookmarks*", "* ChromiumHistory*", "* ChromiumPresence*", "* CloudCredentials*", "* CredEnum*", "* CredGuard*", "* FirefoxHistory*", "* ProcessCreationEvents*"))) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* -group=misc*", "* -group=remote*", "* -group=chromium*", "* -group=slack*", "* -group=system*", "* -group=user*", "* -group=all*")) and CommandLine:"* -outputfile=*"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - System Informer Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of System Informer, a task manager tool to view and manipulate processes, kernel options and other low level operations
false_positives: System Informer is regularly used legitimately by system administrators or developers. Apply additional filters accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SystemInformer.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SystemInformer.exe" or Description:"System Informer" or Product:"System Informer" or (Hashes like~ ("*MD5=19426363A37C03C3ED6FEDF57B6696EC*", "*SHA1=8B12C6DA8FAC0D5E8AB999C31E5EA04AF32D53DC*", "*SHA256=8EE9D84DE50803545937A63C686822388A3338497CDDB660D5D69CF68B68F287*", "*IMPHASH=B68908ADAEB5D662F87F2528AF318F12*")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - TruffleHog Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of TruffleHog, a tool used to search for secrets in different platforms like Git, Jira, Slack, SharePoint, etc. that could be used maliciously.
While it is a legitimate tool, intended for use in CI pipelines and security assessments,
It was observed in the Shai-Hulud malware campaign targeting npm packages to steal sensitive information.
false_positives: Legitimate use of TruffleHog by security teams or developers.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\trufflehog.exe" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* docker --image *", "* Git *", "* GitHub *", "* Jira *", "* Slack *", "* Confluence *", "* SharePoint *", "* s3 *", "* gcs *")) and CommandLine:"* --results=verified*"))

name: SIGMA - PUA - WebBrowserPassView Execution
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of WebBrowserPassView.exe. A password recovery tool that reveals the passwords stored by the following Web browsers, Internet Explorer (Version 4.0 - 11.0), Mozilla Firefox (All Versions), Google Chrome, Safari, and Opera
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"Web Browser Password Viewer" or Image:"*\\WebBrowserPassView.exe")

name: SIGMA - PUA - Wsudo Suspicious Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of wsudo (Windows Sudo Utility). Which is a tool that let the user execute programs with different permissions (System, Trusted Installer, Administrator...etc)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\wsudo.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wsudo.exe" or Description:"Windows sudo utility" or ParentImage:"*\\wsudo-bridge.exe") or (CommandLine like~ ("*-u System*", "*-uSystem*", "*-u TrustedInstaller*", "*-uTrustedInstaller*", "* --ti *")))

name: SIGMA - PUA - Adidnsdump Execution
author: frack113
description:
This tool enables enumeration and exporting of all DNS records in the zone for recon purposes of internal networks Python 3 and python.exe must be installed,
Usee to Query/modify DNS records for Active Directory integrated DNS via LDAP
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\python.exe" and CommandLine:"*adidnsdump*")

name: SIGMA - Python Inline Command Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of python using the "-c" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live python code.
false_positives: Python libraries that use a flag starting with "-c". Filter according to your environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName:"python.exe" or (Image like~ ("*python.exe", "*python3.exe", "*python2.exe"))) and CommandLine:"* -c*") and (not (((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Python*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Python*")) and ParentImage:"*\\python.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*-E -s -m ensurepip -U --default-pip*") or ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Python*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Python*")) and (CommandLine:"*-W ignore::DeprecationWarning*" and CommandLine:"*['install', '--no-cache-dir', '--no-index', '--find-links',*" and CommandLine:"*'--upgrade', 'pip'*")))) and (not ((ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Microsoft VS Code\\Code.exe" or (ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft VS Code\\Code.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft VS Code\\Code.exe"))) or (CommandLine:"*<pip-setuptools-caller>*" and CommandLine:"*exec(compile(*"))))

name: SIGMA - Python Spawning Pretty TTY on Windows
author: Nextron Systems
description: Detects python spawning a pretty tty
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*python.exe", "*python3.exe", "*python2.exe")) and ((CommandLine:"*import pty*" and CommandLine:"*.spawn(*") or CommandLine:"*from pty import spawn*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Usage Of Qemu
author: Muhammad Faisal (@faisalusuf), Hunter Juhan (@threatHNTR)
description:
Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Qemu utility in a Windows environment.
Threat actors have leveraged this utility and this technique for achieving network access as reported by Kaspersky.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("*-m 1M*", "*-m 2M*", "*-m 3M*")) and (CommandLine:"*restrict=off*" and CommandLine:"*-netdev *" and CommandLine:"*connect=*" and CommandLine:"*-nographic*")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* -cdrom *", "* type=virt *", "* -blockdev *"))))

name: SIGMA - Query Usage To Exfil Data
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "query.exe" a system binary to exfil information such as "sessions" and "processes" for later use
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\query.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*session >*", "*process >*")))

name: SIGMA - QuickAssist Execution
author: Muhammad Faisal (@faisalusuf)
description: Detects the execution of Microsoft Quick Assist tool "QuickAssist.exe". This utility can be used by attackers to gain remote access.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Quick Assist in the environment.
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\QuickAssist.exe"

name: SIGMA - Rar Usage with Password and Compression Level
author: @ROxPinTeddy
description: Detects the use of rar.exe, on the command line, to create an archive with password protection or with a specific compression level. This is pretty indicative of malicious actions.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Winrar command line version
Other command line tools, that use these flags
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"* -hp*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -m*", "* a *")))

name: SIGMA - Files Added To An Archive Using Rar.EXE
author: Timur Zinniatullin, E.M. Anhaus, oscd.community
description: Detects usage of "rar" to add files to an archive for potential compression. An adversary may compress data (e.g. sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.
false_positives: Highly likely if rar is a default archiver in the monitored environment.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\rar.exe" and CommandLine:"* a *")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Greedy Compression Using Rar.EXE
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects RAR usage that creates an archive from a suspicious folder, either a system folder or one of the folders often used by attackers for staging purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\rar.exe" or Description:"Command line RAR") or (CommandLine like~ ("*.exe a *", "* a -m*"))) and ((CommandLine:"* -hp*" and CommandLine:"* -r *") and (CommandLine like~ ("* ?:\\\*.*", "* ?:\\\\\*.*", "* ?:\\$Recycle.bin\\*", "* ?:\\PerfLogs\\*", "* ?:\\Temp*", "* ?:\\Users\\Public\\*", "* ?:\\Windows\\*", "* %public%*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious RASdial Activity
author: juju4
description: Detects suspicious process related to rasdial.exe
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*rasdial.exe"

name: SIGMA - Process Memory Dump via RdrLeakDiag.EXE
author: Cedric MAURUGEON, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the Microsoft Windows Resource Leak Diagnostic tool "rdrleakdiag.exe" to dump process memory
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rdrleakdiag.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RdrLeakDiag.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-memdmp*", "*/memdmp*", "*–memdmp*", "*—memdmp*", "*―memdmp*") or CommandLine like~ ("*fullmemdmp*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -o *", "* /o *", "* –o *", "* —o *", "* ―o *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -p *", "* /p *", "* –p *", "* —p *", "* ―p *")))

name: SIGMA - Windows Recovery Environment Disabled Via Reagentc
author: Daniel Koifman (KoifSec), Michael Vilshin
description:
Detects attempts to disable windows recovery environment using Reagentc.
ReAgentc.exe is a command-line tool in Windows used to manage the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE).
It allows users to enable, disable, and configure WinRE, which is used for troubleshooting and repairing common boot issues.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reagentc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reagentc.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-disable*", "*/disable*", "*–disable*", "*—disable*", "*―disable*"))

name: SIGMA - RegAsm.EXE Execution Without CommandLine Flags or Files
author: frack113
description:
Detects the execution of "RegAsm.exe" without a commandline flag or file, which might indicate potential process injection activity.
Usually "RegAsm.exe" should point to a dedicated DLL file or call the help with the "/?" flag.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Regasm by developers.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\RegAsm.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RegAsm.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*RegAsm", "*RegAsm.exe", "*RegAsm.exe\"", "*RegAsm.exe'")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs With Uncommon Extension
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities with an uncommon extension.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\Regsvcs.exe", "*\\Regasm.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("RegSvcs.exe", "RegAsm.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.dat*", "*.gif*", "*.jpeg*", "*.jpg*", "*.png*", "*.txt*")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs From Uncommon Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities from a potentially suspicious location
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\Regsvcs.exe", "*\\Regasm.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("RegSvcs.exe", "RegAsm.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Exports Critical Registry Keys To a File
author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the export of a crital Registry key to a file.
false_positives: Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGEDIT.EXE") and CommandLine like~ ("* -E *", "* /E *", "* –E *", "* —E *", "* ―E *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*hklm*", "*hkey_local_machine*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\system", "*\\sam", "*\\security")))

name: SIGMA - Exports Registry Key To a File
author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the export of the target Registry key to a file.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\regedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGEDIT.EXE") and CommandLine like~ ("* -E *", "* /E *", "* –E *", "* —E *", "* ―E *")) and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*hklm*", "*hkey_local_machine*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\system", "*\\sam", "*\\security")))))

name: SIGMA - Imports Registry Key From a File
author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the import of the specified file to the registry with regedit.exe.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\regedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGEDIT.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /i *", "* /s *", "*.reg*"))) and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("* -e *", "* /e *", "* –e *", "* —e *", "* ―e *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -a *", "* /a *", "* –a *", "* —a *", "* ―a *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *")) and CommandLine regex~ ":[^ \\]")))

name: SIGMA - Imports Registry Key From an ADS
author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the import of a alternate datastream to the registry with regedit.exe.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\regedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGEDIT.EXE") and ((CommandLine like~ ("* /i *", "*.reg*")) and CommandLine regex~ ":[^ \\]")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* -e *", "* /e *", "* –e *", "* —e *", "* ―e *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -a *", "* /a *", "* –a *", "* —a *", "* ―a *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *"))))

name: SIGMA - Regedit as Trusted Installer
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or by ProcessHacker.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\regedit.exe" and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\TrustedInstaller.exe", "*\\ProcessHacker.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Registry Modification From ADS Via Regini.EXE
author: Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the import of an alternate data stream with regini.exe, regini.exe can be used to modify registry keys.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regini.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGINI.EXE") and CommandLine regex~ ":[^ \\]")

name: SIGMA - Registry Modification Via Regini.EXE
author: Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
description: Detects the execution of regini.exe which can be used to modify registry keys, the changes are imported from one or more text files.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regini.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGINI.EXE") and (not CommandLine regex~ ":[^ \\]"))

name: SIGMA - DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe
author: Ivan Dyachkov, Yulia Fomina, oscd.community
description: Detects using register-cimprovider.exe to execute arbitrary dll file.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\register-cimprovider.exe" and (CommandLine:"*-path*" and CommandLine:"*dll*"))

name: SIGMA - Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects processes that query known 3rd party registry keys that holds credentials via commandline
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\Software\\Aerofox\\Foxmail\\V3.1*", "*\\Software\\Aerofox\\FoxmailPreview*", "*\\Software\\DownloadManager\\Passwords*", "*\\Software\\FTPWare\\COREFTP\\Sites*", "*\\Software\\IncrediMail\\Identities*", "*\\Software\\Martin Prikryl\\WinSCP 2\\Sessions*", "*\\Software\\Mobatek\\MobaXterm\\*", "*\\Software\\OpenSSH\\Agent\\Keys*", "*\\Software\\OpenVPN-GUI\\configs*", "*\\Software\\ORL\\WinVNC3\\Password*", "*\\Software\\Qualcomm\\Eudora\\CommandLine*", "*\\Software\\RealVNC\\WinVNC4*", "*\\Software\\RimArts\\B2\\Settings*", "*\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions*", "*\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\SshHostKeys\\*", "*\\Software\\Sota\\FFFTP*", "*\\Software\\TightVNC\\Server*", "*\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Radmin\\v3.0\\Server\\Parameters\\Radmin*")) and (not (Image:"*reg.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*export*", "*save*")))))

name: SIGMA - Registry Export of Third-Party Credentials
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of reg.exe to export registry paths associated with third-party credentials.
Credential stealers have been known to use this technique to extract sensitive information from the registry.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*save*", "*export*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Software\\Aerofox\\Foxmail\\V3.1*", "*\\Software\\Aerofox\\FoxmailPreview*", "*\\Software\\DownloadManager\\Passwords*", "*\\Software\\FTPWare\\COREFTP\\Sites*", "*\\Software\\IncrediMail\\Identities*", "*\\Software\\Martin Prikryl\\WinSCP 2\\Sessions*", "*\\Software\\Mobatek\\MobaXterm*", "*\\Software\\OpenSSH\\Agent\\Keys*", "*\\Software\\OpenVPN-GUI\\configs*", "*\\Software\\ORL\\WinVNC3\\Password*", "*\\Software\\Qualcomm\\Eudora\\CommandLine*", "*\\Software\\RealVNC\\WinVNC4*", "*\\Software\\RimArts\\B2\\Settings*", "*\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions*", "*\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\SshHostKeys*", "*\\Software\\Sota\\FFFTP*", "*\\Software\\TightVNC\\Server*", "*\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Radmin\\v3.0\\Server\\Parameters\\Radmin*")))

name: SIGMA - IE ZoneMap Setting Downgraded To MyComputer Zone For HTTP Protocols Via CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to Internet Explorer's (IE / Windows Internet properties) ZoneMap configuration of the "HTTP" and "HTTPS" protocols to point to the "My Computer" zone. This allows downloaded files from the Internet to be granted the same level of trust as files stored locally.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Internet Settings\\ZoneMap\\ProtocolDefaults*" and CommandLine:"*http*" and CommandLine:"* 0*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Debugger Registration Cmdline
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro
description: Detects the registration of a debugger for a program that is available in the logon screen (sticky key backdoor).
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*sethc.exe*", "*utilman.exe*", "*osk.exe*", "*magnify.exe*", "*narrator.exe*", "*displayswitch.exe*", "*atbroker.exe*", "*HelpPane.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Logon Scripts - CommandLine
author: Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure)
description: Detects the addition of a new LogonScript to the registry value "UserInitMprLogonScript" for potential persistence
false_positives: Legitimate addition of Logon Scripts via the command line by administrators or third party tools
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*UserInitMprLogonScript*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
false_positives: Other legitimate network providers used and not filtred in this rule
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\NetworkProvider*")

name: SIGMA - Python Function Execution Security Warning Disabled In Excel
author: @Kostastsale
description:
Detects changes to the registry value "PythonFunctionWarnings" that would prevent any warnings or alerts from showing when Python functions are about to be executed.
Threat actors could run malicious code through the new Microsoft Excel feature that allows Python to run within the spreadsheet.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*\\Microsoft\\Office\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Excel\\Security*" and CommandLine:"*PythonFunctionWarnings*") and CommandLine:"* 0*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Privilege Escalation via Service Permissions Weakness
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov
description: Detect modification of services configuration (ImagePath, FailureCommand and ServiceDLL) in registry by processes with Medium integrity level
query: any where EventID:1 and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("Medium", "S-1-16-8192")) and (CommandLine:"*ControlSet*" and CommandLine:"*services*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\ImagePath*", "*\\FailureCommand*", "*\\ServiceDll*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Provisioning Registry Key Abuse For Binary Proxy Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects potential abuse of the provisioning registry key for indirect command execution through "Provlaunch.exe".
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Provisioning\\Commands\\*"

name: SIGMA - Potential PowerShell Execution Policy Tampering - ProcCreation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects changes to the PowerShell execution policy registry key in order to bypass signing requirements for script execution from the CommandLine
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\ShellIds\\Microsoft.PowerShell\\ExecutionPolicy*", "*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\PowerShell\\ExecutionPolicy*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Bypass*", "*RemoteSigned*", "*Unrestricted*")))

name: SIGMA - Hiding User Account Via SpecialAccounts Registry Key - CommandLine
author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects changes to the registry key "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\SpecialAccounts\Userlist" where the value is set to "0" in order to hide user account from being listed on the logon screen.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\SpecialAccounts\\UserList*" and CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*/v*" and CommandLine:"*/d 0*"))

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via TypedPaths - CommandLine
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects modification addition to the 'TypedPaths' key in the user or admin registry via the commandline. Which might indicate persistence attempt
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\TypedPaths*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Regsvr32 Commandline Flag Anomaly
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a potential command line flag anomaly related to "regsvr32" in which the "/i" flag is used without the "/n" which should be uncommon.
false_positives: Administrator typo might cause some false positives
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("* -i:*", "* /i:*", "* –i:*", "* —i:*", "* ―i:*")) and (not CommandLine like~ ("* -n *", "* /n *", "* –n *", "* —n *", "* ―n *")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP IP Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects regsvr32 execution to download and install DLLs located remotely where the address is an IP address.
false_positives: FQDNs that start with a number such as "7-Zip"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP/FTP Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects regsvr32 execution to download/install/register new DLLs that are hosted on Web or FTP servers.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGSVR32.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("* /i*", "* -i*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*ftp*", "*http*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution From Remote Share
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects REGSVR32.exe to execute DLL hosted on remote shares
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\REGSVR32.EXE") and CommandLine:"* \\\\*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of Regsvr32
author: elhoim, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "regsvr32.exe".
false_positives: Unlikely, but can rarely occur. Apply additional filters accordingly.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\regsvr32.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\explorer.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\werfault.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))) and (not (Image:"*\\werfault.exe" and CommandLine:"* -u -p *")))

name: SIGMA - Regsvr32 Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a potentially suspicious location.
false_positives: Some installers might execute "regsvr32" with DLLs located in %TEMP% or in %PROGRAMDATA%. Apply additional filters if necessary.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGSVR32.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Regsvr32 Execution From Highly Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a highly suspicious locations
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGSVR32.EXE") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*\\Windows\\Registration\\CRMLog*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\com\\dmp\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\FxsTmp\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\Microsoft\\Crypto\\RSA\\MachineKeys\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\drivers\\color\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\PRINTERS\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\SERVERS\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks_Migrated\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\*", "*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\com\\dmp\\*", "*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\FxsTmp\\*", "*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\PLA\\System\\*", "*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\*", "*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*\\Windows\\Tracing\\*")) or ((CommandLine like~ ("* \"C:\\*", "* C:\\*", "* 'C:\\*", "*D:\\*")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "*C:\\Program Files\\*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Users\\*", "* C:\\Windows\\*", "* \"C:\\Windows\\*", "* 'C:\\Windows\\*"))))) and (not (CommandLine=="" or (?CommandLine == null))))

name: SIGMA - Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Suspicious File Extension
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects the execution of REGSVR32.exe with DLL files masquerading as other files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGSVR32.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.bin", "*.bmp", "*.cr2", "*.dat", "*.eps", "*.gif", "*.ico", "*.jpeg", "*.jpg", "*.log", "*.nef", "*.orf", "*.png", "*.raw", "*.rtf", "*.sr2", "*.temp", "*.tif", "*.tiff", "*.tmp", "*.txt")))

name: SIGMA - Scripting/CommandLine Process Spawned Regsvr32
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects various command line and scripting engines/processes such as "PowerShell", "Wscript", "Cmd", etc. spawning a "regsvr32" instance.
false_positives: Legitimate ".bat", ".hta", ".ps1" or ".vbs" scripts leverage legitimately often. Apply additional filter and exclusions as necessary
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe") and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"* /s C:\\Windows\\System32\\RpcProxy\\RpcProxy.dll")))

name: SIGMA - Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a "regsvr32" execution where the DLL doesn't contain a common file extension.
false_positives: Other legitimate extensions currently not in the list either from third party or specific Windows components.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"REGSVR32.EXE") and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*.ax*", "*.cpl*", "*.dll*", "*.ocx*")) or (?CommandLine == null) or CommandLine=="")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*.ppl*", "*.bav*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Attempt Via Run Keys Using Reg.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious command line reg.exe tool adding key to RUN key in Registry
false_positives: Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.
Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"* add *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run*")))

name: SIGMA - Add SafeBoot Keys Via Reg Utility
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "add" or "copy" flags on safe boot registry keys. Often used by attacker to allow the ransomware to work in safe mode as some security products do not
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* copy *", "* add *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Reg Add BitLocker
author: frack113
description: Detects suspicious addition to BitLocker related registry keys via the reg.exe utility
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*REG*" and CommandLine:"*ADD*" and CommandLine:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\FVE*" and CommandLine:"*/v*" and CommandLine:"*/f*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*EnableBDEWithNoTPM*", "*UseAdvancedStartup*", "*UseTPM*", "*UseTPMKey*", "*UseTPMKeyPIN*", "*RecoveryKeyMessageSource*", "*UseTPMPIN*", "*RecoveryKeyMessage*")))

name: SIGMA - Dropping Of Password Filter DLL
author: Sreeman
description: Detects dropping of dll files in system32 that may be used to retrieve user credentials from LSASS
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*HKLM\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa*" and CommandLine:"*scecli\\0*" and CommandLine:"*reg add*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Windows Defender Folder Exclusion Added Via Reg.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to add Defender folder exclusions. Qbot has been seen using this technique to add exclusions for folders within AppData and ProgramData.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions\\Paths*", "*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Microsoft Antimalware\\Exclusions\\Paths*")) and (CommandLine:"*ADD *" and CommandLine:"*/t *" and CommandLine:"*REG_DWORD *" and CommandLine:"*/v *" and CommandLine:"*/d *" and CommandLine:"*0*"))

name: SIGMA - RunMRU Registry Key Deletion
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects deletion of the RunMRU registry key, which stores the history of commands executed via the Run dialog.
In the clickfix techniques, the phishing lures instruct users to open a run dialog through (Win + R) and execute malicious commands.
Adversaries may delete this key to cover their tracks after executing commands.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"* del*" and CommandLine:"*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\RunMRU*"))

name: SIGMA - SafeBoot Registry Key Deleted Via Reg.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "delete" flag on safe boot registry keys. Often used by attacker to prevent safeboot execution of security products
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"* delete *" and CommandLine:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot*"))

name: SIGMA - Service Registry Key Deleted Via Reg.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" commands with the "delete" flag on services registry key. Often used by attacker to remove AV software services
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"* delete *" and CommandLine:"*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\services\\*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Desktop Background Change Using Reg.EXE
author: Stephen Lincoln @slincoln-aiq (AttackIQ)
description:
Detects the execution of "reg.exe" to alter registry keys that would replace the user's desktop background.
