This is a common technique used by malware to change the desktop background to a ransom note or other image.
false_positives: Administrative scripts that change the desktop background to a company logo or other image.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"*add*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*Control Panel\\Desktop*", "*CurrentVersion\\Policies\\ActiveDesktop*", "*CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System*")) and ((CommandLine:"*/v NoChangingWallpaper*" and CommandLine:"*/d 1*") or (CommandLine:"*/v Wallpaper*" and CommandLine:"*/t REG_SZ*") or (CommandLine:"*/v WallpaperStyle*" and CommandLine:"*/d 2*")))

name: SIGMA - Direct Autorun Keys Modification
author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects direct modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry using reg.exe.
false_positives: Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.
Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"*add*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run*", "*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Userinit*", "*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Shell*", "*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows*", "*\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\User Shell Folders*", "*\\system\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot\\AlternateShell*")))

name: SIGMA - Disabling Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session via Reg.exe
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description:
Detects the use of reg.exe to disable the Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) Autologger session for Windows Defender API and Audit events.
By setting the 'Start' value to '0' for the 'DefenderApiLogger' or 'DefenderAuditLogger' session, an attacker can prevent these critical security events
from being logged, effectively blinding monitoring tools that rely on this data. This is a powerful defense evasion technique.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Control\\WMI\\Autologger\\DefenderApiLogger\\Start*", "*\\Control\\WMI\\Autologger\\DefenderAuditLogger\\Start*")) and (CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*0*")) and (not CommandLine:"*0x00000001*"))

name: SIGMA - Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), John Lambert (idea), elhoim
description: Detects execution of "reg.exe" to disable security services such as Windows Defender.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"*add*") and ((CommandLine:"*d 4*" and CommandLine:"*v Start*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppIDSvc*", "*\\MsMpSvc*", "*\\NisSrv*", "*\\SecurityHealthService*", "*\\Sense*", "*\\UsoSvc*", "*\\WdBoot*", "*\\WdFilter*", "*\\WdNisDrv*", "*\\WdNisSvc*", "*\\WinDefend*", "*\\wscsvc*", "*\\wuauserv*"))))

name: SIGMA - Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Endgame, JHasenbusch, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, frack113
description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to dump sensitive registry hives. This includes SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY hives.
false_positives: Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* save *", "* export *", "* ˢave *", "* eˣport *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*hklm*", "*hk˪m*", "*hkey_local_machine*", "*hkey_˪ocal_machine*", "*hkey_loca˪_machine*", "*hkey_˪oca˪_machine*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\system*", "*\\sam*", "*\\security*", "*\\ˢystem*", "*\\syˢtem*", "*\\ˢyˢtem*", "*\\ˢam*", "*\\ˢecurity*")))

name: SIGMA - Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE
author: Sajid Nawaz Khan
description:
Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation.
Windows Recall can be enabled by deleting the existing "DisableAIDataAnalysis" value, or setting it to 0.
Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities.
This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"*Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI*" and CommandLine:"*DisableAIDataAnalysis*") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*add*", "*0*")) or CommandLine:"*delete*"))

name: SIGMA - Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials.
The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs.
Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"* query *" and CommandLine:"*/t *" and CommandLine:"*REG_SZ*" and CommandLine:"*/s*")) and ((CommandLine:"*/f *" and CommandLine:"*HKLM*") or (CommandLine:"*/f *" and CommandLine:"*HKCU*") or CommandLine:"*HKCU\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects the import of '.reg' files from suspicious paths using the 'reg.exe' utility
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"* import *" and (CommandLine like~ ("*C:\\Users\\*", "*%temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*%appdata%*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*")))

name: SIGMA - RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
author: frack113
description:
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode.
RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop.
This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa*" and CommandLine:"*DisableRestrictedAdmin*")

name: SIGMA - LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of the "reg.exe" utility to disable PPL protection on the LSA process
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"*SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa*" and (CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"* /d 0*" and CommandLine:"* /v RunAsPPL *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Query of MachineGUID
author: frack113
description: Use of reg to get MachineGuid information
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography*" and CommandLine:"*/v *" and CommandLine:"*MachineGuid*"))

name: SIGMA - Modify Group Policy Settings
author: frack113
description: Detect malicious GPO modifications can be used to implement many other malicious behaviors.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\System*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*GroupPolicyRefreshTimeDC*", "*GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffsetDC*", "*GroupPolicyRefreshTime*", "*GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffset*", "*EnableSmartScreen*", "*ShellSmartScreenLevel*")))

name: SIGMA - Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects changes to the "NoLMHash" registry value in order to allow Windows to store LM Hashes.
By setting this registry value to "0" (DWORD), Windows will be allowed to store a LAN manager hash of your password in Active Directory and local SAM databases.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa*" and CommandLine:"*NoLMHash*" and CommandLine:"* 0*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Reg Add Open Command
author: frack113
description: Threat actors performed dumping of SAM, SECURITY and SYSTEM registry hives using DelegateExecute key
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*hkcu\\software\\classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command*" and CommandLine:"*/ve *" and CommandLine:"*/d*") or (CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"*add*" and CommandLine:"*hkcu\\software\\classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command*" and CommandLine:"*/v*" and CommandLine:"*DelegateExecute*") or (CommandLine:"*reg*" and CommandLine:"*delete*" and CommandLine:"*hkcu\\software\\classes\\ms-settings*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and CommandLine:"*query*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*currentVersion\\windows*", "*winlogon\\*", "*currentVersion\\shellServiceObjectDelayLoad*", "*currentVersion\\run*", "*currentVersion\\policies\\explorer\\run*", "*currentcontrolset\\services*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Tampering With RDP Related Registry Keys Via Reg.EXE
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects the execution of "reg.exe" for enabling/disabling the RDP service on the host by tampering with the 'CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' values
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"*\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Terminal Server*" and CommandLine:"*REG_DWORD*" and CommandLine:"* /f*")) and ((CommandLine:"*Licensing Core*" and CommandLine:"*EnableConcurrentSessions*") or (CommandLine like~ ("*WinStations\\RDP-Tcp*", "*MaxInstanceCount*", "*fEnableWinStation*", "*TSUserEnabled*", "*TSEnabled*", "*TSAppCompat*", "*IdleWinStationPoolCount*", "*TSAdvertise*", "*AllowTSConnections*", "*fSingleSessionPerUser*", "*fDenyTSConnections*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*HKEY_CURRENT_USER\\Control Panel\\Desktop*", "*HKCU\\Control Panel\\Desktop*"))) and ((CommandLine:"*/v ScreenSaveActive*" and CommandLine:"*/t REG_SZ*" and CommandLine:"*/d 1*" and CommandLine:"*/f*") or (CommandLine:"*/v ScreenSaveTimeout*" and CommandLine:"*/t REG_SZ*" and CommandLine:"*/d *" and CommandLine:"*/f*") or (CommandLine:"*/v ScreenSaverIsSecure*" and CommandLine:"*/t REG_SZ*" and CommandLine:"*/d 0*" and CommandLine:"*/f*") or (CommandLine:"*/v SCRNSAVE.EXE*" and CommandLine:"*/t REG_SZ*" and CommandLine:"*/d *" and CommandLine:"*.scr*" and CommandLine:"*/f*")))

name: SIGMA - Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"*add *" and CommandLine:"*SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*" and CommandLine:"* ImagePath *")) and CommandLine like~ ("* -d *", "* /d *", "* –d *", "* —d *", "* ―d *"))

name: SIGMA - Detected Windows Software Discovery
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Adversaries may attempt to enumerate software for a variety of reasons, such as figuring out what security measures are present or if the compromised system has a version of software that is vulnerable.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\reg.exe" and (CommandLine:"*query*" and CommandLine:"*\\software\\*" and CommandLine:"*/v*" and CommandLine:"*svcversion*"))

name: SIGMA - Reg Add Suspicious Paths
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an adversary uses the reg.exe utility to add or modify new keys or subkeys
false_positives: Rare legitimate add to registry via cli (to these locations)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppDataLow\\Software\\Microsoft\\*", "*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\OOBE*", "*\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\Currentversion\\Winlogon*", "*\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SecurityProviders\\WDigest*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Disabled Volume Snapshots
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects commands that temporarily turn off Volume Snapshots
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\Services\\VSS\\Diag*" and CommandLine:"*/d Disabled*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to tamper with different Windows Defender registry keys in order to disable some important features related to protection and detection
false_positives: Rare legitimate use by administrators to test software (should always be investigated)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\reg.exe" or OriginalFileName:"reg.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\*", "*SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender Security Center*", "*SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\*"))) and (((CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"*d 0*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*DisallowExploitProtectionOverride*", "*EnableControlledFolderAccess*", "*MpEnablePus*", "*PUAProtection*", "*SpynetReporting*", "*SubmitSamplesConsent*", "*TamperProtection*"))) or ((CommandLine:"* add *" and CommandLine:"*d 1*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*DisableAccess*", "*DisableAntiSpyware*", "*DisableAntiSpywareRealtimeProtection*", "*DisableAntiVirus*", "*DisableAntiVirusSignatures*", "*DisableArchiveScanning*", "*DisableBehaviorMonitoring*", "*DisableBlockAtFirstSeen*", "*DisableCloudProtection*", "*DisableConfig*", "*DisableEnhancedNotifications*", "*DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem*", "*DisableIOAVProtection*", "*DisableNetworkProtection*", "*DisableOnAccessProtection*", "*DisablePrivacyMode*", "*DisableRealtimeMonitoring*", "*DisableRoutinelyTakingAction*", "*DisableScanOnRealtimeEnable*", "*DisableScriptScanning*", "*DisableSecurityCenter*", "*Notification_Suppress*", "*SignatureDisableUpdateOnStartupWithoutEngine*")))))

name: SIGMA - Write Protect For Storage Disabled
author: Sreeman
description:
Detects applications trying to modify the registry in order to disable any write-protect property for storage devices.
This could be a precursor to a ransomware attack and has been an observed technique used by cypherpunk group.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control*" and CommandLine:"*Write Protection*" and CommandLine:"*0*" and CommandLine:"*storage*")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\AnyDesk.exe", "*\\AnyDeskMSI.exe")) or Description:"AnyDesk" or Product:"AnyDesk" or Company:"AnyDesk Software GmbH")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Piped Password Via CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects piping the password to an anydesk instance via CMD and the '--set-password' flag.
false_positives: Legitimate piping of the password to anydesk
Some FP could occur with similar tools that uses the same command line '--set-password'
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*/c *" and CommandLine:"*echo *" and CommandLine:"*.exe --set-password*")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution With Known Revoked Signing Certificate
author: Sai Prashanth Pulisetti, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of an AnyDesk binary with a version prior to 8.0.8.
Prior to version 8.0.8, the Anydesk application used a signing certificate that got compromised by threat actors.
Use this rule to detect instances of older versions of Anydesk using the compromised certificate
This is recommended in order to avoid attackers leveraging the certificate and signing their binaries to bypass detections.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\AnyDesk.exe" or Description:"AnyDesk" or Product:"AnyDesk" or Company:"AnyDesk Software GmbH") and (FileVersion like~ ("7.0.*", "7.1.*", "8.0.1*", "8.0.2*", "8.0.3*", "8.0.4*", "8.0.5*", "8.0.6*", "8.0.7*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* --remove*", "* --uninstall*"))))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Silent Installation
author: Ján Trenčanský
description: Detects AnyDesk Remote Desktop silent installation. Which can be used by attackers to gain remote access.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*--install*" and CommandLine:"*--start-with-win*" and CommandLine:"*--silent*")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Anydesk Execution From Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
false_positives: Legitimate use of AnyDesk from a non-standard folder
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\AnyDesk.exe", "*\\AnyDeskMSI.exe")) or Description:"AnyDesk" or Product:"AnyDesk" or Company:"AnyDesk Software GmbH") and (not (Image like~ ("*\\AppData\\*", "*Program Files (x86)\\AnyDesk*", "*Program Files\\AnyDesk*"))))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - GoToAssist Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"GoTo Opener" or Product:"GoTo Opener" or Company:"LogMeIn, Inc.")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - LogMeIn Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"LMIGuardianSvc" or Product:"LMIGuardianSvc" or Company:"LogMeIn, Inc.")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Potential MeshAgent Execution - Windows
author: Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC)
description:
Detects potential execution of MeshAgent which is a tool used for remote access.
Historical data shows that threat actors rename MeshAgent binary to evade detection.
Matching command lines with the '--meshServiceName' argument can indicate that the MeshAgent is being used for remote access.
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*--meshServiceName*"

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - MeshAgent Command Execution via MeshCentral
author: @Kostastsale
description:
Detects the use of MeshAgent to execute commands on the target host, particularly when threat actors might abuse it to execute commands directly.
MeshAgent can execute commands on the target host by leveraging win-console to obscure their activities and win-dispatcher to run malicious code through IPC with child processes.
false_positives: False positives can be found in environments using MeshAgent for remote management, analysis should prioritize the grandparent process, MeshAgent.exe, and scrutinize the resulting child processes triggered by any suspicious interactive commands directed at the target host.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\meshagent.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"NetSupport Client Configurator" or Product:"NetSupport Remote Control" or Company:"NetSupport Ltd" or OriginalFileName:"PCICFGUI.EXE")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution From Unusual Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of client32.exe (NetSupport RAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\client32.exe" or Product:"*NetSupport Remote Control*" or OriginalFileName:"*client32.exe*" or Hashes:"*IMPHASH=a9d50692e95b79723f3e76fcf70d023e*") and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Renamed MeshAgent Execution - Windows
author: Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC)
description:
Detects the execution of a renamed instance of the Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tool, MeshAgent.
RMM tools such as MeshAgent are commonly utilized by IT administrators for legitimate remote support and system management.
However, malicious actors may exploit these tools by renaming them to bypass detection mechanisms, enabling unauthorized access and control over compromised systems.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*--meshServiceName*" or OriginalFileName:"*meshagent*") and (not Image:"*\\meshagent.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - RURAT Execution From Unusual Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of Remote Utilities RAT (RURAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\rutserv.exe", "*\\rfusclient.exe")) or Product:"Remote Utilities") and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Remote Utilities*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Remote Utilities*"))))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"ScreenConnect Service" or Product:"ScreenConnect" or Company:"ScreenConnect Software")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Installation Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects ScreenConnect program starts that establish a remote access to a system.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*e=Access&*" and CommandLine:"*y=Guest&*" and CommandLine:"*&p=*" and CommandLine:"*&c=*" and CommandLine:"*&k=*")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Remote Command Execution
author: Ali Alwashali
description: Detects the execution of a system command via the ScreenConnect RMM service.
false_positives: Legitimate use of ScreenConnect. Disable this rule if ScreenConnect is heavily used.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe" and (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine:"*\\TEMP\\ScreenConnect\\*")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Potential Suspicious Remote Command Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @Kostastsale
description: Detects potentially suspicious child processes launched via the ScreenConnect client service.
false_positives: If the script being executed make use of any of the utilities mentioned in the detection then they should filtered out or allowed.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Windows\\TEMP\\ScreenConnect\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*run.cmd*") and (Image like~ ("*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\curl.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wevtutil.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Server Web Shell Execution
author: Jason Rathbun (Blackpoint Cyber)
description: Detects potential web shell execution from the ScreenConnect server process.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\ScreenConnect.Service.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\csc.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Simple Help Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\JWrapper-Remote Access\\*", "*\\JWrapper-Remote Support\\*")) and Image:"*\\SimpleService.exe")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - TacticalRMM Agent Registration to Potentially Attacker-Controlled Server
author: Ahmed Nosir (@egycondor)
description:
Detects TacticalRMM agent installations where the --api, --auth, and related flags are used on the command line.
These parameters configure the agent to connect to a specific RMM server with authentication, client ID, and site ID.
This technique could indicate a threat actor attempting to register the agent with an attacker-controlled RMM infrastructure silently.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\TacticalAgent\\tacticalrmm.exe*" and (CommandLine:"*--api*" and CommandLine:"*--auth*" and CommandLine:"*--client-id*" and CommandLine:"*--site-id*" and CommandLine:"*--agent-type*"))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Team Viewer Session Started On Windows Host
author: Josh Nickels, Qi Nan
description:
Detects the command line executed when TeamViewer starts a session started by a remote host.
Once a connection has been started, an investigator can verify the connection details by viewing the "incoming_connections.txt" log file in the TeamViewer folder.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"TeamViewer_Desktop.exe" and ParentImage:"TeamViewer_Service.exe" and CommandLine:"*TeamViewer_Desktop.exe --IPCport 5939 --Module 1")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - UltraViewer Execution
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks.
These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment.
Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Product:"UltraViewer" or Company:"DucFabulous Co,ltd" or OriginalFileName:"UltraViewer_Desktop.exe")

name: SIGMA - Discovery of a System Time
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Identifies use of various commands to query a systems time. This technique may be used before executing a scheduled task or to discover the time zone of a target system.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the system utilities to discover system time for legitimate reason
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe")) and CommandLine:"*time*") or (Image:"*\\w32tm.exe" and CommandLine:"*tz*"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed AdFind Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of a renamed Adfind.exe. AdFind continues to be seen across majority of breaches. It is used to domain trust discovery to plan out subsequent steps in the attack chain.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("*domainlist*", "*trustdmp*", "*dcmodes*", "*adinfo*", "* dclist *", "*computer_pwdnotreqd*", "*objectcategory=*", "*-subnets -f*", "*name=\"Domain Admins\"*", "*-sc u:*", "*domainncs*", "*dompol*", "* oudmp *", "*subnetdmp*", "*gpodmp*", "*fspdmp*", "*users_noexpire*", "*computers_active*", "*computers_pwdnotreqd*")) or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=BCA5675746D13A1F246E2DA3C2217492*", "*IMPHASH=53E117A96057EAF19C41380D0E87F1C2*", "*IMPHASH=d144de8117df2beceaba2201ad304764*", "*IMPHASH=12ce1c0f3f5837ecc18a3782408fa975*", "*IMPHASH=4fbf3f084fbbb2470b80b2013134df35*", "*IMPHASH=49b639b4acbecc49d72a01f357aa4930*", "*IMPHASH=680dad9e300346e05a85023965867201*", "*IMPHASH=21aa085d54992511b9f115355e468782*")) or OriginalFileName:"AdFind.exe") and (not Image:"*\\AdFind.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed AutoHotkey.EXE Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects execution of a renamed autohotkey.exe binary based on PE metadata fields
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"*AutoHotkey*" or Description:"*AutoHotkey*" or (OriginalFileName like~ ("AutoHotkey.exe", "AutoHotkey.rc"))) and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\AutoHotkey.exe", "*\\AutoHotkey32.exe", "*\\AutoHotkey32_UIA.exe", "*\\AutoHotkey64.exe", "*\\AutoHotkey64_UIA.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyA32.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyA32_UIA.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyU32.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyU32_UIA.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyU64.exe", "*\\AutoHotkeyU64_UIA.exe")) or Image:"*\\AutoHotkey*")))

name: SIGMA - Renamed AutoIt Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of a renamed AutoIt2.exe or AutoIt3.exe.
AutoIt is a scripting language and automation tool for Windows systems. While primarily used for legitimate automation tasks, it can be misused in cyber attacks.
Attackers can leverage AutoIt to create and distribute malware, including keyloggers, spyware, and botnets. A renamed AutoIt executable is particularly suspicious.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("* /AutoIt3ExecuteScript*", "* /ErrorStdOut*")) or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=FDC554B3A8683918D731685855683DDF*", "*IMPHASH=CD30A61B60B3D60CECDB034C8C83C290*", "*IMPHASH=F8A00C72F2D667D2EDBB234D0C0AE000*")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("AutoIt3.exe", "AutoIt2.exe", "AutoIt.exe"))) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\AutoIt.exe", "*\\AutoIt2.exe", "*\\AutoIt3_x64.exe", "*\\AutoIt3.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Via Binary Rename
author: Matthew Green @mgreen27, Ecco, James Pemberton @4A616D6573, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.
false_positives: Custom applications use renamed binaries adding slight change to binary name. Typically this is easy to spot and add to whitelist
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "CONHOST.EXE", "7z.exe", "7za.exe", "WinRAR.exe", "wevtutil.exe", "net.exe", "net1.exe", "netsh.exe", "InstallUtil.exe")) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\conhost.exe", "*\\7z.exe", "*\\7za.exe", "*\\WinRAR.exe", "*\\wevtutil.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\netsh.exe", "*\\InstallUtil.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Via Rename Of Highly Relevant Binaries
author: Matthew Green - @mgreen27, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.
false_positives: Custom applications use renamed binaries adding slight change to binary name. Typically this is easy to spot and add to whitelist
PsExec installed via Windows Store doesn't contain original filename field (False negative)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Description:"Execute processes remotely" or Product:"Sysinternals PsExec" or (Description like~ ("Windows PowerShell*", "pwsh*")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("certutil.exe", "cmstp.exe", "cscript.exe", "IE4UINIT.EXE", "mshta.exe", "msiexec.exe", "msxsl.exe", "powershell_ise.exe", "powershell.exe", "psexec.c", "psexec.exe", "psexesvc.exe", "pwsh.dll", "reg.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "rundll32.exe", "WerMgr", "wmic.exe", "wscript.exe"))) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cmstp.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\ie4uinit.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\msxsl.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\psexec.exe", "*\\psexec64.exe", "*\\PSEXESVC.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wermgr.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed BOINC Client Execution
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed BOINC binary.
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"BOINC.exe" and (not Image:"*\\BOINC.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed BrowserCore.EXE Execution
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects process creation with a renamed BrowserCore.exe (used to extract Azure tokens)
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"BrowserCore.exe" and (not Image:"*\\BrowserCore.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Cloudflared.EXE Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "cloudflared" binary.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((CommandLine:"* tunnel *" and CommandLine:"*cleanup *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-config *", "*-connector-id *"))) or ((CommandLine:"* tunnel *" and CommandLine:"* run *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-config *", "*-credentials-contents *", "*-credentials-file *", "*-token *"))) or (CommandLine:"*-url*" and CommandLine:"*tunnel*") or (Hashes like~ ("*SHA256=2fb6c04c4f95fb8d158af94c137f90ac820716deaf88d8ebec956254e046cb29*", "*SHA256=b3d21940a10fdef5e415ad70331ce257c24fe3bcf7722262302e0421791f87e8*", "*SHA256=1fbd8362b2d2d2e6a5750ae3db69cd1815e6c1d31da48a98b796450971a8e039*", "*SHA256=0409c9b12f9d0eda86e461ed9bdabeefb00172b26322079681a0bdf48e68dc28*", "*SHA256=7cfb411d04bac42ef93d1f0c93c0a481e38c6f4612b97ae89d4702595988edc7*", "*SHA256=5b3c2d846ab162dc6bc595cce3a49de5731afde5d6060be7066d21b013a28373*", "*\\cloudflared-windows-386.exe", "*\\cloudflared-windows-amd64.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed CreateDump Utility Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uses of a renamed legitimate createdump.exe LOLOBIN utility to dump process memory
false_positives: Command lines that use the same flags
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"FX_VER_INTERNALNAME_STR" or ((CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -f *" and CommandLine:"*.dmp*") or (CommandLine:"* --full *" and CommandLine:"* --name *" and CommandLine:"*.dmp*"))) and (not Image:"*\\createdump.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed CURL.EXE Execution
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "CURL.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"curl.exe" or Description:"The curl executable") and (not Image:"*\\curl*"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed ZOHO Dctask64 Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a renamed "dctask64.exe" execution, a signed binary by ZOHO Corporation part of ManageEngine Endpoint Central.
This binary can be abused for DLL injection, arbitrary command and process execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=6834B1B94E49701D77CCB3C0895E1AFD*", "*IMPHASH=1BB6F93B129F398C7C4A76BB97450BBA*", "*IMPHASH=FAA2AC19875FADE461C8D89DCF2710A3*", "*IMPHASH=F1039CED4B91572AB7847D26032E6BBF*")) and (not Image:"*\\dctask64.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed FTP.EXE Execution
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "ftp.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"ftp.exe" and (not Image:"*\\ftp.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Gpg.EXE Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "gpg.exe". Often used by ransomware and loaders to decrypt/encrypt data.
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"gpg.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\gpg.exe", "*\\gpg2.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Jusched.EXE Execution
author: Markus Neis, Swisscom
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "jusched.exe" as seen used by the cobalt group
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Description like~ ("Java Update Scheduler", "Java(TM) Update Scheduler")) and (not Image:"*\\jusched.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Mavinject.EXE Execution
author: frack113, Florian Roth
description: Detects the execution of a renamed version of the "Mavinject" process. Which can be abused to perform process injection using the "/INJECTRUNNING" flag
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName like~ ("mavinject32.exe", "mavinject64.exe")) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\mavinject32.exe", "*\\mavinject64.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed MegaSync Execution
author: Sittikorn S
description: Detects the execution of a renamed MegaSync.exe as seen used by ransomware families like Nefilim, Sodinokibi, Pysa, and Conti.
false_positives: Software that illegally integrates MegaSync in a renamed form
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"megasync.exe" and (not Image:"*\\megasync.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Msdt.EXE Execution
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "Msdt.exe" binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"msdt.exe" and (not Image:"*\\msdt.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Microsoft Teams Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed Microsoft Teams binary.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName like~ ("msteams.exe", "teams.exe")) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\msteams.exe", "*\\teams.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed NetSupport RAT Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "client32.exe" (NetSupport RAT) via Imphash, Product and OriginalFileName strings
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"*NetSupport Remote Control*" or OriginalFileName:"*client32.exe*" or Hashes:"*IMPHASH=A9D50692E95B79723F3E76FCF70D023E*") and (not Image:"*\\client32.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed NirCmd.EXE Execution
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "NirCmd.exe" binary based on the PE metadata fields.
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"NirCmd.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\nircmd.exe", "*\\nircmdc.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Office Binary Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed office binary
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("Excel.exe", "MSACCESS.EXE", "MSPUB.EXE", "OneNote.exe", "OneNoteM.exe", "OUTLOOK.EXE", "POWERPNT.EXE", "WinWord.exe")) or (Description like~ ("Microsoft Access", "Microsoft Excel", "Microsoft OneNote", "Microsoft Outlook", "Microsoft PowerPoint", "Microsoft Publisher", "Microsoft Word", "Sent to OneNote Tool"))) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\EXCEL.exe", "*\\excelcnv.exe", "*\\MSACCESS.exe", "*\\MSPUB.EXE", "*\\ONENOTE.EXE", "*\\ONENOTEM.EXE", "*\\OUTLOOK.EXE", "*\\POWERPNT.EXE", "*\\WINWORD.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed PAExec Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jason Lynch
description: Detects execution of renamed version of PAExec. Often used by attackers
false_positives: Weird admins that rename their tools
Software companies that bundle PAExec with their software and rename it, so that it is less embarrassing
When executed with the "-s" flag. PAExec will copy itself to the "C:\Windows\" directory with a different name. Usually like this "PAExec-[XXXXX]-[ComputerName]"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Description:"PAExec Application" or OriginalFileName:"PAExec.exe" or Product:"*PAExec*" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=11D40A7B7876288F919AB819CC2D9802*", "*IMPHASH=6444f8a34e99b8f7d9647de66aabe516*", "*IMPHASH=dfd6aa3f7b2b1035b76b718f1ddc689f*", "*IMPHASH=1a6cca4d5460b1710a12dea39e4a592c*"))) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\paexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\PAExec-*"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed PingCastle Binary Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed "PingCastle" binary based on the PE metadata fields.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("PingCastleReporting.exe", "PingCastleCloud.exe", "PingCastle.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*--scanner aclcheck*", "*--scanner antivirus*", "*--scanner computerversion*", "*--scanner foreignusers*", "*--scanner laps_bitlocker*", "*--scanner localadmin*", "*--scanner nullsession*", "*--scanner nullsession-trust*", "*--scanner oxidbindings*", "*--scanner remote*", "*--scanner share*", "*--scanner smb*", "*--scanner smb3querynetwork*", "*--scanner spooler*", "*--scanner startup*", "*--scanner zerologon*")) or CommandLine:"*--no-enum-limit*" or (CommandLine:"*--healthcheck*" and CommandLine:"*--level Full*") or (CommandLine:"*--healthcheck*" and CommandLine:"*--server *")) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\PingCastleReporting.exe", "*\\PingCastleCloud.exe", "*\\PingCastle.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Plink Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a renamed version of the Plink binary
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"Plink" or (CommandLine:"* -l forward*" and CommandLine:"* -P *" and CommandLine:"* -R *")) and (not Image:"*\\plink.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Visual Studio NodejsTools PressAnyKey Renamed Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects renamed execution of "Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe", which can be abused as a LOLBIN to execute arbitrary binaries
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe" and (not Image:"*\\Microsoft.NodejsTools.PressAnyKey.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Renamed Rundll32 Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when 'DllRegisterServer' is called in the commandline and the image is not rundll32. This could mean that the 'rundll32' utility has been renamed in order to avoid detection
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*DllRegisterServer*" and (not Image:"*\\rundll32.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Remote Utilities RAT (RURAT) Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of renamed Remote Utilities (RURAT) via Product PE header field
query: any where EventID:1 and (Product:"Remote Utilities" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\rutserv.exe", "*\\rfusclient.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed SysInternals DebugView Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious renamed SysInternals DebugView execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (Product:"Sysinternals DebugView" and (not (OriginalFileName:"Dbgview.exe" and Image:"*\\Dbgview.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Renamed ProcDump Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of a renamed ProcDump executable.
This often done by attackers or malware in order to evade defensive mechanisms.
false_positives: Procdump illegally bundled with legitimate software.
Administrators who rename binaries (should be investigated).
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"procdump" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* -ma *", "* /ma *", "* –ma *", "* —ma *", "* ―ma *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -mp *", "* /mp *", "* –mp *", "* —mp *", "* ―mp *")) and CommandLine like~ ("* -accepteula*", "* /accepteula*", "* –accepteula*", "* —accepteula*", "* ―accepteula*"))) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\procdump.exe", "*\\procdump64.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed PsExec Service Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious launch of a renamed version of the PSEXESVC service with, which is not often used by legitimate administrators
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"psexesvc.exe" and (not Image:"C:\\Windows\\PSEXESVC.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Sysinternals Sdelete Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of a renamed SysInternals Sdelete, which is something an administrator shouldn't do (the renaming)
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"sdelete.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\sdelete.exe", "*\\sdelete64.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Vmnat.exe Execution
author: elhoim
description: Detects renamed vmnat.exe or portable version that can be used for DLL side-loading
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"vmnat.exe" and (not Image:"*vmnat.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Whoami Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of whoami that has been renamed to a different name to avoid detection
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"whoami.exe" and (not Image:"*\\whoami.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Capture Credentials with Rpcping.exe
author: Julia Fomina, oscd.community
description: Detects using Rpcping.exe to send a RPC test connection to the target server (-s) and force the NTLM hash to be sent in the process.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\rpcping.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("*-s*", "*/s*", "*–s*", "*—s*", "*―s*") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*-u*", "*/u*", "*–u*", "*—u*", "*―u*") and CommandLine like~ ("*NTLM*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*-t*", "*/t*", "*–t*", "*—t*", "*―t*") and CommandLine like~ ("*ncacn_np*"))))

name: SIGMA - Ruby Inline Command Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of ruby using the "-e" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live ruby code.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\ruby.exe" or OriginalFileName:"ruby.exe") and CommandLine:"* -e*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Rundll32 Execution With DLL Stored In ADS
author: Harjot Singh, '@cyb3rjy0t'
description: Detects execution of rundll32 where the DLL being called is stored in an Alternate Data Stream (ADS).
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and CommandLine regex~ "[Rr][Uu][Nn][Dd][Ll][Ll]32(\.[Ee][Xx][Ee])? \S+?\w:\S+?:")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Advpack Call Via Rundll32.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "rundll32" calling "advpack.dll" with potential obfuscated ordinal calls in order to leverage the "RegisterOCX" function
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE" or CommandLine:"*rundll32*") and CommandLine:"*advpack*" and ((CommandLine:"*#+*" and CommandLine:"*12*") or CommandLine:"*#-*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Rundll32 Invoking Inline VBScript
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process related to rundll32 based on command line that invokes inline VBScript as seen being used by UNC2452
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe*" and CommandLine:"*Execute*" and CommandLine:"*RegRead*" and CommandLine:"*window.close*")

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 InstallScreenSaver Execution
author: Christopher Peacock @securepeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io, TactiKoolSec
description: An attacker may execute an application as a SCR File using rundll32.exe desk.cpl,InstallScreenSaver
false_positives: Legitimate installation of a new screensaver
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and CommandLine:"*InstallScreenSaver*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Key Manager Access
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the invocation of the Stored User Names and Passwords dialogue (Key Manager)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*keymgr*" and CommandLine:"*KRShowKeyMgr*"))

name: SIGMA - Mshtml.DLL RunHTMLApplication Suspicious Usage
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems),  Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Josh Nickels, frack113, Zaw Min Htun (ZETA)
description: Detects execution of commands that leverage the "mshtml.dll" RunHTMLApplication export to run arbitrary code via different protocol handlers (vbscript, javascript, file, http...)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*\\..\\*" and CommandLine:"*mshtml*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*#135*", "*RunHTMLApplication*")))

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Execution Without CommandLine Parameters
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious start of rundll32.exe without any parameters as found in CobaltStrike beacon activity
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe\"", "*\\rundll32")) and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious NTLM Authentication on the Printer Spooler Service
author: Elastic (idea), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a privilege elevation attempt by coercing NTLM authentication on the Printer Spooler service
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*C:\\windows\\system32\\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie*" and CommandLine:"*http*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*spoolss*", "*srvsvc*", "*/print/pipe/*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Obfuscated Ordinal Call Via Rundll32
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "rundll32" with potential obfuscated ordinal calls
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE" or CommandLine:"*rundll32*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*#+*", "*#-*", "*#0*", "*#655*", "*#656*")))

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Spawned Via Explorer.EXE
author: CD_ROM_
description: Detects execution of "rundll32.exe" with a parent process of Explorer.exe. This has been observed by variants of Raspberry Robin, as first reported by Red Canary.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\explorer.exe" and (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "* -localserver 22d8c27b-47a1-48d1-ad08-7da7abd79617"))))

name: SIGMA - Process Memory Dump Via Comsvcs.DLL
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Modexp, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a process memory dump via "comsvcs.dll" using rundll32, covering multiple different techniques (ordinal, minidump function, etc.)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE" or CommandLine:"*rundll32*") and ((CommandLine:"*comsvcs*" and CommandLine:"*full*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*#-*", "*#+*", "*#24*", "*24 *", "*MiniDump*", "*#65560*")))) or ((CommandLine:"*24*" and CommandLine:"*comsvcs*" and CommandLine:"*full*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* #*", "*,#*", "*, #*", "*\"#*"))))

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Registered COM Objects
author: frack113
description: load malicious registered COM objects
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*-sta *", "*-localserver *")) and (CommandLine:"*{*" and CommandLine:"*}*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Start Locations
author: juju4, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects suspicious process run from unusual locations
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*:\\RECYCLER\\*", "*:\\SystemVolumeInformation\\*")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "C:\\Windows\\debug\\*", "C:\\Windows\\fonts\\*", "C:\\Windows\\help\\*", "C:\\Windows\\drivers\\*", "C:\\Windows\\addins\\*", "C:\\Windows\\cursors\\*", "C:\\Windows\\system32\\tasks\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Rundll32 Setupapi.dll Activity
author: Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community
description: setupapi.dll library provide InstallHinfSection function for processing INF files. INF file may contain instructions allowing to create values in the registry, modify files and install drivers. This technique could be used to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys, run process or use other DLLs chain calls (see references) InstallHinfSection function in setupapi.dll calls runonce.exe executable regardless of actual content of INF file.
false_positives: Scripts and administrative tools that use INF files for driver installation with setupapi.dll
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\runonce.exe" and ParentImage:"*\\rundll32.exe" and (ParentCommandLine:"*setupapi.dll*" and ParentCommandLine:"*InstallHinfSection*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell32 DLL Execution in Suspicious Directory
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects shell32.dll executing a DLL in a suspicious directory
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*shell32.dll*" and CommandLine:"*Control_RunDLL*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*%AppData%*", "*%LocalAppData%*", "*%Temp%*", "*%tmp%*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential ShellDispatch.DLL Functionality Abuse
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential "ShellDispatch.dll" functionality abuse to execute arbitrary binaries via "ShellExecute"
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and CommandLine:"*RunDll_ShellExecuteW*")

name: SIGMA - RunDLL32 Spawning Explorer
author: elhoim, CD_ROM_
description: Detects RunDLL32.exe spawning explorer.exe as child, which is very uncommon, often observes Gamarue spawning the explorer.exe process in an unusual way
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\rundll32.exe" and Image:"*\\explorer.exe") and (not ParentCommandLine:"*\\shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Rundll32 Activity
author: juju4, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious execution of rundll32, with specific calls to some DLLs with known LOLBIN functionalities
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*javascript:*" and CommandLine:"*.RegisterXLL*") or (CommandLine:"*url.dll*" and CommandLine:"*OpenURL*") or (CommandLine:"*url.dll*" and CommandLine:"*OpenURLA*") or (CommandLine:"*url.dll*" and CommandLine:"*FileProtocolHandler*") or (CommandLine:"*zipfldr.dll*" and CommandLine:"*RouteTheCall*") or (CommandLine:"*shell32.dll*" and CommandLine:"*Control_RunDLL*") or (CommandLine:"*shell32.dll*" and CommandLine:"*ShellExec_RunDLL*") or (CommandLine:"*mshtml.dll*" and CommandLine:"*PrintHTML*") or (CommandLine:"*advpack.dll*" and CommandLine:"*LaunchINFSection*") or (CommandLine:"*advpack.dll*" and CommandLine:"*RegisterOCX*") or (CommandLine:"*ieadvpack.dll*" and CommandLine:"*LaunchINFSection*") or (CommandLine:"*ieadvpack.dll*" and CommandLine:"*RegisterOCX*") or (CommandLine:"*ieframe.dll*" and CommandLine:"*OpenURL*") or (CommandLine:"*shdocvw.dll*" and CommandLine:"*OpenURL*") or (CommandLine:"*syssetup.dll*" and CommandLine:"*SetupInfObjectInstallAction*") or (CommandLine:"*setupapi.dll*" and CommandLine:"*InstallHinfSection*") or (CommandLine:"*pcwutl.dll*" and CommandLine:"*LaunchApplication*") or (CommandLine:"*dfshim.dll*" and CommandLine:"*ShOpenVerbApplication*") or (CommandLine:"*dfshim.dll*" and CommandLine:"*ShOpenVerbShortcut*") or (CommandLine:"*scrobj.dll*" and CommandLine:"*GenerateTypeLib*" and CommandLine:"*http*") or (CommandLine:"*shimgvw.dll*" and CommandLine:"*ImageView_Fullscreen*" and CommandLine:"*http*") or (CommandLine:"*comsvcs.dll*" and CommandLine:"*MiniDump*")) and (not (CommandLine:"*shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL desk.cpl,screensaver,@screensaver*" or (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\control.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*.cpl*" and (CommandLine:"*Shell32.dll*" and CommandLine:"*Control_RunDLL*" and CommandLine:"*.cpl*")) or (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\control.exe" and CommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\rundll32.exe\" Shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL \"C:\\Windows\\System32\\*" and CommandLine:"*.cpl\","))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Control Panel DLL Load
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious Rundll32 execution from control.exe as used by Equation Group and Exploit Kits
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\System32\\control.exe" and (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE")) and (not CommandLine:"*Shell32.dll*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Rundll32 Execution With Image Extension
author: Hieu Tran
description: Detects the execution of Rundll32.exe with DLL files masquerading as image files
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.bmp*", "*.cr2*", "*.eps*", "*.gif*", "*.ico*", "*.jpeg*", "*.jpg*", "*.nef*", "*.orf*", "*.png*", "*.raw*", "*.sr2*", "*.tif*", "*.tiff*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Usage Of ShellExec_RunDLL
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious usage of the ShellExec_RunDLL function to launch other commands as seen in the the raspberry-robin attack
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*ShellExec_RunDLL*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*comspec*", "*iex*", "*Invoke-*", "*msiexec*", "*odbcconf*", "*regsvr32*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious ShellExec_RunDLL Call Via Ordinal
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description:
Detects suspicious call to the "ShellExec_RunDLL" exported function of SHELL32.DLL through the ordinal number to launch other commands.
Adversary might only use the ordinal number in order to bypass existing detection that alert on usage of ShellExec_RunDLL on CommandLine.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentCommandLine:"*SHELL32.DLL*" and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*#568*", "*#570*", "*#572*", "*#576*"))) and (((ParentCommandLine like~ ("*comspec*", "*iex*", "*Invoke-*", "*msiexec*", "*odbcconf*", "*regsvr32*")) or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\ProgramData\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*"))) or (Image like~ ("*\\bash.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\curl.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\msxsl.exe", "*\\odbcconf.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - ShimCache Flush
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects actions that clear the local ShimCache and remove forensic evidence
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*rundll32*" and CommandLine:"*apphelp.dll*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ShimFlushCache*", "*#250*"))) or ((CommandLine:"*rundll32*" and CommandLine:"*kernel32.dll*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*BaseFlushAppcompatCache*", "*#46*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Rundll32 Activity Invoking Sys File
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process related to rundll32 based on command line that includes a *.sys file as seen being used by UNC2452
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*.sys,*", "*.sys *")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Rundll32.EXE Execution of UDL File
author: @kostastsale
description:
Detects the execution of rundll32.exe with the oledb32.dll library to open a UDL file.
Threat actors can abuse this technique as a phishing vector to capture authentication credentials or other sensitive data.
false_positives: UDL files serve as a convenient and flexible tool for managing and testing database connections in various development and administrative scenarios.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\explorer.exe" and (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*oledb32.dll*" and CommandLine:"*,OpenDSLFile *" and CommandLine:"*\\Users\\*\\Downloads\\*") and CommandLine:"*.udl"))

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Execution With Uncommon DLL Extension
author: Tim Shelton, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Yassine Oukessou
description: Detects the execution of rundll32 with a command line that doesn't contain a common extension
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and (not ((?CommandLine == null) or CommandLine=="" or ((CommandLine like~ ("*.cpl *", "*.cpl,*", "*.cpl\"*", "*.cpl'*", "*.dll *", "*.dll,*", "*.dll\"*", "*.dll'*", "*.inf *", "*.inf,*", "*.inf\"*", "*.inf'*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*.cpl", "*.dll", "*.inf"))) or CommandLine:"* -localserver *" or (ParentImage:"*\\msiexec.exe" and (CommandLine:"*:\\Windows\\Installer\\*" and CommandLine:"*.tmp*" and CommandLine:"*zzzzInvokeManagedCustomActionOutOfProc*")))) and (not (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\EdgeUpdate\\Install\\{*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\EDGEMITMP_*" and ParentCommandLine:"*.tmp\\setup.exe*" and ParentCommandLine:"*--install-archive=*" and ParentCommandLine:"*--previous-version=*" and ParentCommandLine:"*--msedgewebview --verbose-logging --do-not-launch-msedge --user-level*")))

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 UNC Path Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects rundll32 execution where the DLL is located on a remote location (share)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE" or CommandLine:"*rundll32*") and CommandLine:"* \\\\*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Workstation Locking via Rundll32
author: frack113
description: Detects a suspicious call to the user32.dll function that locks the user workstation
false_positives: Scripts or links on the user desktop used to lock the workstation instead of Windows+L or the menu option
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and ParentImage:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*user32.dll,*" and CommandLine:"*LockWorkStation*")

name: SIGMA - WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description:
Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like "C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie".
This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on a WebDav server).
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\svchost.exe" and (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and CommandLine:"*C:\\windows\\system32\\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie. This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on WebDav Server) or potentially a sign of exploitation of CVE-2023-23397

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Execution Without Parameters
author: Bartlomiej Czyz, Relativity
description: Detects rundll32 execution without parameters as observed when running Metasploit windows/smb/psexec exploit module
false_positives: False positives may occur if a user called rundll32 from CLI with no options
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("rundll32.exe", "rundll32"))

name: SIGMA - Run Once Task Execution as Configured in Registry
author: Avneet Singh @v3t0_, oscd.community, Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock (updated)
description: This rule detects the execution of Run Once task as configured in the registry
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\runonce.exe" or Description:"Run Once Wrapper") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/AlternateShellStartup*", "*/r")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Schtasks Execution AppData Folder
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a schtask that executes a file from C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Local
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/Create*" and CommandLine:"*/RU*" and CommandLine:"*/TR*" and CommandLine:"*C:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*NT AUT*", "* SYSTEM *"))) and (not ((ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" and ParentImage:"*TeamViewer_.exe*") and Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine:"*/TN TVInstallRestore*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Modification Of Scheduled Tasks
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects when an attacker tries to modify an already existing scheduled tasks to run from a suspicious location
Attackers can create a simple looking task in order to avoid detection on creation as it's often the most focused on
Instead they modify the task after creation to include their malicious payload
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"* /Change *" and CommandLine:"* /TN *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\WINDOWS\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Perflogs\\*", "*%ProgramData%*", "*%appdata%*", "*%comspec%*", "*%localappdata%*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*cmd /c *", "*cmd /k *", "*cmd /r *", "*cmd.exe /c *", "*cmd.exe /k *", "*cmd.exe /r *", "*powershell*", "*mshta*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*", "*certutil*", "*bitsadmin*", "*bash.exe*", "*bash *", "*scrcons*", "*wmic *", "*wmic.exe*", "*forfiles*", "*scriptrunner*", "*hh.exe*", "*hh *")))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Creation Via Schtasks.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine:"* /create *") and (not (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation Involving Temp Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks that involves a temporary folder and runs only once
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"* /create *" and CommandLine:"* /sc once *" and CommandLine:"*\\Temp\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Creation with Curl and PowerShell Execution Combo
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the creation of a scheduled task using schtasks.exe, potentially in combination with curl for downloading payloads and PowerShell for executing them.
This facilitates executing malicious payloads or connecting with C&C server persistently without dropping the malware sample on the host.
false_positives: Legitimate use of schtasks for administrative purposes.
Automation scripts combining curl and PowerShell in controlled environments.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("* -create *", "* /create *", "* –create *", "* —create *", "* ―create *")) and (CommandLine:"*curl *" and CommandLine:"*http*" and CommandLine:"*-o*") and CommandLine:"*powershell*")

name: SIGMA - Delete Important Scheduled Task
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by deleting their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/delete*" and CommandLine:"*/tn*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Windows\\BitLocker*", "*\\Windows\\ExploitGuard*", "*\\Windows\\SystemRestore\\SR*", "*\\Windows\\UpdateOrchestrator\\*", "*\\Windows\\Windows Defender\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsBackup\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsUpdate\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Delete All Scheduled Tasks
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of schtasks with the delete flag and the asterisk symbol to delete all tasks from the schedule of the local computer, including tasks scheduled by other users.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"* /delete *" and CommandLine:"*/tn \**" and CommandLine:"* /f*"))

name: SIGMA - Disable Important Scheduled Task
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior
description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by disabling their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/Change*" and CommandLine:"*/TN*" and CommandLine:"*/disable*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Windows\\BitLocker*", "*\\Windows\\ExploitGuard*", "*\\Windows\\ExploitGuard\\ExploitGuard MDM policy Refresh*", "*\\Windows\\SystemRestore\\SR*", "*\\Windows\\UpdateOrchestrator\\*", "*\\Windows\\Windows Defender\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsBackup\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsUpdate\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Schedule Task Creation From Env Variable Or Potentially Suspicious Path Via Schtasks.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Schtask creations that point to a suspicious folder or an environment variable often used by malware
false_positives: Benign scheduled tasks creations or executions that happen often during software installations
Software that uses the AppData folder and scheduled tasks to update the software in the AppData folders
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("* -create *", "* /create *", "* –create *", "* —create *", "* ―create *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs*", "*:\\Users\\All Users\\*", "*:\\Users\\Default\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*%AppData%*", "*%Public%*"))) or (ParentCommandLine:"*\\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p -s Schedule" and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp*", "*\\Users\\Public*", "*%Public%*")))) and (not ((ParentCommandLine:"*unattended.ini*" or CommandLine:"*update_task.xml*") or CommandLine:"*/Create /TN TVInstallRestore /TR*" or (CommandLine:"*/Create /Xml *" and CommandLine:"*\\Temp\\.CR.*" and CommandLine:"*\\Avira_Security_Installation.xml*") or ((CommandLine:"*/Create /F /TN*" and CommandLine:"*/Xml *" and CommandLine:"*\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"*Avira_*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*.tmp\\UpdateFallbackTask.xml*", "*.tmp\\WatchdogServiceControlManagerTimeout.xml*", "*.tmp\\SystrayAutostart.xml*", "*.tmp\\MaintenanceTask.xml*"))) or (CommandLine:"*\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"*/Create /TN \"klcp_update\" /XML *" and CommandLine:"*\\klcp_update_task.xml*"))))

name: SIGMA - Schtasks From Suspicious Folders
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scheduled task creations that have suspicious action command and folder combinations
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and CommandLine:"* /create *" and (CommandLine like~ ("*powershell*", "*pwsh*", "*cmd /c *", "*cmd /k *", "*cmd /r *", "*cmd.exe /c *", "*cmd.exe /k *", "*cmd.exe /r *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*%ProgramData%*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scheduled Task Name As GUID
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects creation of a scheduled task with a GUID like name
false_positives: Legitimate software naming their tasks as GUIDs
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine:"*/Create *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/TN \"{*", "*/TN '{*", "*/TN {*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*}\"*", "*}'*", "*} *")))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon One Time Only Scheduled Task At 00:00
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scheduled task creation events that include suspicious actions, and is run once at 00:00
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe*" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*wscript*", "*vbscript*", "*cscript*", "*wmic *", "*wmic.exe*", "*regsvr32.exe*", "*powershell*", "*\\AppData\\*")) and (CommandLine:"*once*" and CommandLine:"*00:00*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential SSH Tunnel Persistence Install Using A Scheduled Task
author: Rory Duncan
description: Detects the creation of new scheduled tasks via commandline, using Schtasks.exe. This rule detects tasks creating that call OpenSSH, which may indicate the creation of reverse SSH tunnel to the attacker's server.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and ((CommandLine:"* /create *" and CommandLine:"*sshd.exe*" and CommandLine:"*-f*") or (CommandLine:"* /create *" and CommandLine:"*ssh.exe*" and CommandLine:"*-i*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser
author: Sreeman
description:
Detects manual execution of the "Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser" task via schtasks.
In order to trigger persistence stored in the "\AppCompatFlags\TelemetryController" registry key.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine:"*run *" and CommandLine:"*\\Application Experience\\Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Powershell Search Order Hijacking - Task
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious powershell execution via a schedule task where the command ends with an suspicious flags to hide the powershell instance instead of executeing scripts or commands. This could be a sign of persistence via PowerShell "Get-Variable" technique as seen being used in Colibri Loader
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\svchost.exe" and (ParentCommandLine:"*-k netsvcs*" and ParentCommandLine:"*-s Schedule*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -windowstyle hidden", "* -w hidden", "* -ep bypass", "* -noni")))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Executing Payload from Registry
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a schtasks that potentially executes a payload stored in the Windows Registry using PowerShell.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and CommandLine:"*/Create*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*Get-ItemProperty*", "* gp *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*HKCU:*", "*HKLM:*", "*registry::*", "*HKEY_*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*FromBase64String*", "*encodedcommand*"))))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Executing Encoded Payload from Registry
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport, X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a schtask that potentially executes a base64 encoded payload stored in the Windows Registry using PowerShell.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and CommandLine:"*/Create*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*FromBase64String*", "*encodedcommand*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Get-ItemProperty*", "* gp *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*HKCU:*", "*HKLM:*", "*registry::*", "*HKEY_*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Types
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification on a suspicious schedule type
false_positives: Legitimate processes that run at logon. Filter according to your environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* ONLOGON *", "* ONSTART *", "* ONCE *", "* ONIDLE *"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*NT AUT*", "* SYSTEM*", "*HIGHEST*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Type With High Privileges
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification to be run with high privileges on a suspicious schedule type
