false_positives: Some installers were seen using this method of creation unfortunately. Filter them in your environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* ONLOGON *", "* ONSTART *", "* ONCE *", "* ONIDLE *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*NT AUT*", "* SYSTEM*", "*HIGHEST*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation via Masqueraded XML File
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task using the "-XML" flag with a file without the '.xml' extension. This behavior could be indicative of potential defense evasion attempt during persistence

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/create*", "*-create*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/xml*", "*-xml*"))) and (not (CommandLine:"*.xml*" or (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384")) or (ParentImage:"*\\rundll32.exe" and (ParentCommandLine:"*:\\WINDOWS\\Installer\\MSI*" and ParentCommandLine:"*.tmp,zzzzInvokeManagedCustomActionOutOfProc*")))) and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*:\\ProgramData\\OEM\\UpgradeTool\\CareCenter_*\\BUnzip\\Setup_msi.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Axis Communications\\AXIS Camera Station\\SetupActions.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Axis Communications\\AXIS Device Manager\\AdmSetupActions.exe", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Zemana\\AntiMalware\\AntiMalware.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Dell\\SupportAssist\\pcdrcui.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Command Patterns In Scheduled Task Creation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scheduled task creation using "schtasks" that contain potentially suspicious or uncommon commands
false_positives: Software installers that run from temporary folders and also install scheduled tasks are expected to generate some false positives
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine:"*/Create *") and (((CommandLine like~ ("*/sc minute *", "*/ru system *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd /c*", "*cmd /k*", "*cmd /r*", "*cmd.exe /c *", "*cmd.exe /k *", "*cmd.exe /r *"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("* -decode *", "* -enc *", "* -w hidden *", "* bypass *", "* IEX*", "*.DownloadData*", "*.DownloadFile*", "*.DownloadString*", "*/c start /min *", "*FromBase64String*", "*mshta http*", "*mshta.exe http*")) or ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Tmp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*%AppData%*", "*%Temp%*", "*%tmp%*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*cscript*", "*curl*", "*wscript*")))))

name: SIGMA - Schtasks Creation Or Modification With SYSTEM Privileges
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation or update of a scheduled task to run with "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" privileges
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("* /change *", "* /create *"))) and CommandLine:"*/ru *" and (CommandLine like~ ("*NT AUT*", "* SYSTEM *"))) and (not ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/TN TVInstallRestore*" and CommandLine:"*\\TeamViewer_.exe*")) or (CommandLine:"*Subscription Heartbeat*" and CommandLine:"*\\HeartbeatConfig.xml*" and CommandLine:"*\\Microsoft Shared\\OFFICE*") or (CommandLine like~ ("*/Create /F /RU System /SC WEEKLY /TN AviraSystemSpeedupVerify /TR *", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avira\\System Speedup\\setup\\avira_speedup_setup.exe*", "*/VERIFY /VERYSILENT /NOSTART /NODOTNET /NORESTART\" /RL HIGHEST*")))))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Creation Masquerading as System Processes
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of scheduled tasks that involve system processes, which may indicate malicious actors masquerading as or abusing these processes to execute payloads or maintain persistence.
false_positives: Legitimate system administration tasks scheduling trusted system processes.

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\schtasks.exe" or OriginalFileName:"schtasks.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -create *", "* /create *", "* –create *", "* —create *", "* ―create *") and (CommandLine like~ ("* audiodg*", "* conhost*", "* dwm.exe*", "* explorer*", "* lsass*", "* lsm*", "* mmc*", "* msiexec*", "* regsvr32*", "* rundll32*", "* services*", "* spoolsv*", "* svchost*", "* taskeng*", "* taskhost*", "* wininit*", "* winlogon*"))))

name: SIGMA - Script Event Consumer Spawning Process
author: Sittikorn S
description: Detects a suspicious child process of Script Event Consumer (scrcons.exe).
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\scrcons.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\svchost.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\msbuild.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Possible Privilege Escalation via Weak Service Permissions
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov
description: Detection of sc.exe utility spawning by user with Medium integrity level to change service ImagePath or FailureCommand
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("Medium", "S-1-16-8192"))) and ((CommandLine:"*config*" and CommandLine:"*binPath*") or (CommandLine:"*failure*" and CommandLine:"*command*")))

name: SIGMA - New Service Creation Using Sc.EXE
author: Timur Zinniatullin, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects the creation of a new service using the "sc.exe" utility.
false_positives: Legitimate administrator or user creates a service for legitimate reasons.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" and (CommandLine:"*create*" and CommandLine:"*binPath*")) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dropbox\\Client\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Dropbox\\Client\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\Dropbox.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Service StartupType Change Via Sc.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect the use of "sc.exe" to change the startup type of a service to "disabled" or "demand"
false_positives: False positives may occur with troubleshooting scripts
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and ((CommandLine:"* config *" and CommandLine:"*start*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*disabled*", "*demand*"))))

name: SIGMA - New Kernel Driver Via SC.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects creation of a new service (kernel driver) with the type "kernel"
false_positives: Rare legitimate installation of kernel drivers via sc.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*create*", "*config*")) and (CommandLine:"*binPath*" and CommandLine:"*type*" and CommandLine:"*kernel*")) and (not ((CommandLine:"*create netprotection_network_filter*" and CommandLine:"*type= kernel start= *" and CommandLine:"*binPath= System32\\drivers\\netprotection_network_filter*" and CommandLine:"*DisplayName= netprotection_network_filter*" and CommandLine:"*group= PNP_TDI tag= yes*") or (CommandLine:"*create avelam binpath=C:\\Windows\\system32\\drivers\\avelam.sys*" and CommandLine:"*type=kernel start=boot error=critical group=Early-Launch*"))))

name: SIGMA - Interesting Service Enumeration Via Sc.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description:
Detects the enumeration and query of interesting and in some cases sensitive services on the system via "sc.exe".
Attackers often try to enumerate the services currently running on a system in order to find different attack vectors.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and CommandLine:"*query*" and CommandLine:"*termservice*")

name: SIGMA - Allow Service Access Using Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications to allow access to a service from a suspicious trustee. This can be used to override access restrictions set by previous ACLs.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and (CommandLine:"*sdset*" and CommandLine:"*A;*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*;IU*", "*;SU*", "*;BA*", "*;SY*", "*;WD*"))) and (not ParentImage:"C:\\Hexnode\\Hexnode Agent\\Current\\HexnodeAgent.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Deny Service Access Using Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
author: Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects suspicious DACL modifications to deny access to a service that affects critical trustees. This can be used to hide services or make them unstoppable.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and (CommandLine:"*sdset*" and CommandLine:"*D;*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*;IU*", "*;SU*", "*;BA*", "*;SY*", "*;WD*")))

name: SIGMA - Service DACL Abuse To Hide Services Via Sc.EXE
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects usage of the "sc.exe" utility adding a new service with special permission seen used by threat actors which makes the service hidden and unremovable.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and (CommandLine:"*sdset*" and CommandLine:"*DCLCWPDTSD*"))

name: SIGMA - Service Security Descriptor Tampering Via Sc.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detection of sc.exe utility adding a new service with special permission which hides that service.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sc.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sc.exe") and CommandLine:"*sdset*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service Path Modification
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects service path modification via the "sc" binary to a suspicious command or path
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\sc.exe" and (CommandLine:"*config*" and CommandLine:"*binPath*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*powershell*", "*cmd *", "*mshta*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*", "*rundll32*", "*svchost*", "*dllhost*", "*cmd.exe /c*", "*cmd.exe /k*", "*cmd.exe /r*", "*cmd /c*", "*cmd /k*", "*cmd /r*", "*C:\\Users\\Public*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Attempt Via Existing Service Tampering
author: Sreeman
description: Detects the modification of an existing service in order to execute an arbitrary payload when the service is started or killed as a potential method for persistence.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*sc *" and CommandLine:"*config *" and CommandLine:"*binpath=*") or (CommandLine:"*sc *" and CommandLine:"*failure*" and CommandLine:"*command=*")) or (((CommandLine:"*reg *" and CommandLine:"*add *" and CommandLine:"*FailureCommand*") or (CommandLine:"*reg *" and CommandLine:"*add *" and CommandLine:"*ImagePath*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*.sh*", "*.exe*", "*.dll*", "*.bin$*", "*.bat*", "*.cmd*", "*.js*", "*.msh$*", "*.reg$*", "*.scr*", "*.ps*", "*.vb*", "*.jar*", "*.pl*"))))

name: SIGMA - Stop Windows Service Via Sc.EXE
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the stopping of a Windows service via the "sc.exe" utility
false_positives: There are many legitimate reasons to stop a service. This rule isn't looking for any suspicious behavior in particular. Filter legitimate activity accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"sc.exe" or Image:"*\\sc.exe") and CommandLine:"* stop *")

name: SIGMA - Potential Shim Database Persistence via Sdbinst.EXE
author: Markus Neis
description:
Detects installation of a new shim using sdbinst.exe.
Adversaries may establish persistence and/or elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by application shims
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\sdbinst.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sdbinst.exe") and CommandLine:"*.sdb*") and (not (ParentImage:"*\\msiexec.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\IIS Express\\iisexpressshim.sdb*", "*:\\Program Files\\IIS Express\\iisexpressshim.sdb*")))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Extension Shim Database Installation Via Sdbinst.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects installation of a potentially suspicious new shim with an uncommon extension using sdbinst.exe.
Adversaries may establish persistence and/or elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by application shims
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sdbinst.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sdbinst.exe") and (not (CommandLine:"*.sdb*" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* -c", "* -f", "* -mm", "* -t")) or CommandLine:"* -m -bg*") or (?CommandLine == null) or CommandLine=="")))

name: SIGMA - Sdclt Child Processes
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: A General detection for sdclt spawning new processes. This could be an indicator of sdclt being used for bypass UAC techniques.
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\sdclt.exe"

name: SIGMA - Sdiagnhost Calling Suspicious Child Process
author: Nextron Systems, @Kostastsale
description: Detects sdiagnhost.exe calling a suspicious child process (e.g. used in exploits for Follina / CVE-2022-30190)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\sdiagnhost.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\taskkill.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\calc.exe"))) and (not ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*bits*") or (Image:"*\\powershell.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-noprofile -", "*-noprofile"))))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Activity Using SeCEdit
author: Janantha Marasinghe
description: Detects potential suspicious behaviour using secedit.exe. Such as exporting or modifying the security policy

subtechnique.name: Path Interception by PATH Environment Variable

query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\secedit.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SeCEdit") and ((CommandLine:"*/export*" and CommandLine:"*/cfg*") or (CommandLine:"*/configure*" and CommandLine:"*/db*")))

name: SIGMA - NodeJS Execution of JavaScript File
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of JavaScript or JSC files using NodeJs binary node.exe, that could be potentially suspicious.
Node.js is a popular open-source JavaScript runtime that runs code outside browsers and is widely used for both frontend and backend development.
Adversaries have been observed abusing Node.js to disguise malware as legitimate processes, evade security defenses, and maintain persistence within target systems.
Because Node.js is commonly used, this rule may generate false positives in some environments. However, if such activity is unusual in your environment, it is highly suspicious and warrants immediate investigation.
false_positives: Legitimate use of node.exe to execute JavaScript or JSC files on your environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\node.exe" or OriginalFileName:"node.exe" or Product:"Node.js") and CommandLine:"*.js*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Serv-U Process Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious process pattern which could be a sign of an exploited Serv-U service
false_positives: Legitimate uses in which users or programs use the SSH service of Serv-U for remote command execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\Serv-U.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\sh.exe", "*\\bash.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\forfiles.exe", "*\\scriptrunner.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Process Of Setres.EXE
author: @gott_cyber, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects uncommon child process of Setres.EXE.
Setres.EXE is a Windows server only process and tool that can be used to set the screen resolution.
It can potentially be abused in order to launch any arbitrary file with a name containing the word "choice" from the current execution path.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\setres.exe" and Image:"*\\choice*") and (not (Image like~ ("*C:\\Windows\\System32\\choice.exe", "*C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\choice.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential SPN Enumeration Via Setspn.EXE
author: Markus Neis, keepwatch
description: Detects service principal name (SPN) enumeration used for Kerberoasting
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\setspn.exe" or OriginalFileName:"setspn.exe" or (Description:"*Query or reset the computer*" and Description:"*SPN attribute*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -q *", "* /q *")))

name: SIGMA - Setup16.EXE Execution With Custom .Lst File
author: frack113
description:
Detects the execution of "Setup16.EXE" and old installation utility with a custom ".lst" file.
These ".lst" file can contain references to external program that "Setup16.EXE" will execute.
Attackers and adversaries might leverage this as a living of the land utility.
false_positives: On modern Windows system, the "Setup16" utility is practically never used, hence false positive should be very rare.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\setup16.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"* -m *") and (not Image:"C:\\~MSSETUP.T\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution of Shutdown
author: frack113
description: Use of the commandline to shutdown or reboot windows
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\shutdown.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/r *", "*/s *")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution of Shutdown to Log Out
author: frack113
description: Detects the rare use of the command line tool shutdown to logoff a user
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\shutdown.exe" and CommandLine:"*/l*")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Sigverif.EXE Child Process
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uncommon child processes spawning from "sigverif.exe", which could indicate potential abuse of the latter as a living of the land binary in order to proxy execution.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\sigverif.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\WerFault.exe", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WerFault.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Child Processes Of SndVol.exe
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially uncommon child processes of SndVol.exe (the Windows volume mixer)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\SndVol.exe" and (not (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine:"* shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL *")))

name: SIGMA - Audio Capture via SoundRecorder
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detect attacker collecting audio via SoundRecorder application.
false_positives: Legitimate audio capture by legitimate user.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\SoundRecorder.exe" and CommandLine:"*/FILE*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Splwow64 Without Params
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious Splwow64.exe process without any command line parameters
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\splwow64.exe" and CommandLine:"*splwow64.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Spool Service Child Process
author: Justin C. (@endisphotic), @dreadphones (detection), Thomas Patzke (Sigma rule)
description: Detects suspicious print spool service (spoolsv.exe) child processes.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\spoolsv.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384"))) and ((Image like~ ("*\\gpupdate.exe", "*\\whoami.exe", "*\\nltest.exe", "*\\taskkill.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\taskmgr.exe", "*\\sc.exe", "*\\findstr.exe", "*\\curl.exe", "*\\wget.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\accesschk.exe", "*\\wevtutil.exe", "*\\bcdedit.exe", "*\\fsutil.exe", "*\\cipher.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\write.exe", "*\\wuauclt.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\reg.exe", "*\\query.exe")) or ((Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe")) and (not CommandLine:"*start*")) or (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*.spl*", "*route add*", "*program files*")))) or (Image:"*\\netsh.exe" and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*add portopening*", "*rule name*")))) or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (not CommandLine:"*.spl*")) or ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" or OriginalFileName:"RUNDLL32.EXE") and CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Veeam Backup Database Suspicious Query
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious SQL queries using SQLCmd targeting the Veeam backup databases in order to steal information.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\sqlcmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*VeeamBackup*" and CommandLine:"*From *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*BackupRepositories*", "*Backups*", "*Credentials*", "*HostCreds*", "*SmbFileShares*", "*Ssh_creds*", "*VSphereInfo*")))

name: SIGMA - VeeamBackup Database Credentials Dump Via Sqlcmd.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects dump of credentials in VeeamBackup dbo
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\sqlcmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*SELECT*" and CommandLine:"*TOP*" and CommandLine:"*[VeeamBackup].[dbo].[Credentials]*"))

name: SIGMA - SQLite Chromium Profile Data DB Access
author: TropChaud
description: Detect usage of the "sqlite" binary to query databases in Chromium-based browsers for potential data stealing.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"SQLite" or (Image like~ ("*\\sqlite.exe", "*\\sqlite3.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\User Data\\*", "*\\Opera Software\\*", "*\\ChromiumViewer\\*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Login Data*", "*Cookies*", "*Web Data*", "*History*", "*Bookmarks*")))

name: SIGMA - SQLite Firefox Profile Data DB Access
author: frack113
description: Detect usage of the "sqlite" binary to query databases in Firefox and other Gecko-based browsers for potential data stealing.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"SQLite" or (Image like~ ("*\\sqlite.exe", "*\\sqlite3.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*cookies.sqlite*", "*places.sqlite*")))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary File Download Via Squirrel.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Karneades / Markus Neis, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects the usage of the "Squirrel.exe" to download arbitrary files. This binary is part of multiple Electron based software installations (Slack, Teams, Discord, etc.)
false_positives: Expected FP with some Electron based applications such as (1Clipboard, Beaker Browser, Caret, Discord, GitHub Desktop, etc.)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\squirrel.exe", "*\\update.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* --download *", "* --update *", "* --updateRollback=*")) and CommandLine:"*http*")

name: SIGMA - Process Proxy Execution Via Squirrel.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Karneades / Markus Neis, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects the usage of the "Squirrel.exe" binary to execute arbitrary processes. This binary is part of multiple Electron based software installations (Slack, Teams, Discord, etc.)
false_positives: Expected FP with some Electron based applications such as (1Clipboard, Beaker Browser, Caret, Discord, GitHub Desktop, etc.)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\squirrel.exe", "*\\update.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*--processStart*", "*--processStartAndWait*", "*--createShortcut*"))) and (not (((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Discord\\Update.exe*" and CommandLine:"*Discord.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*--createShortcut*", "*--processStart*"))) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\GitHubDesktop\\Update.exe*" and CommandLine:"*GitHubDesktop.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*--createShortcut*", "*--processStartAndWait*"))) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Update.exe*" and CommandLine:"*Teams.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*--processStart*", "*--createShortcut*"))) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\yammerdesktop\\Update.exe*" and CommandLine:"*Yammer.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*--processStart*", "*--createShortcut*"))))))

name: SIGMA - Port Forwarding Activity Via SSH.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects port forwarding activity via SSH.exe
false_positives: Administrative activity using a remote port forwarding to a local port
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\ssh.exe" and CommandLine like~ ("* -R *", "* /R *", "* –R *", "* —R *", "* ―R *"))

name: SIGMA - Program Executed Using Proxy/Local Command Via SSH.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detect usage of the "ssh.exe" binary as a proxy to launch other programs.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe" or ((Image:"*\\ssh.exe" or Product:"OpenSSH for Windows" or (Hashes like~ ("*IMPHASH=55b4964d29aad5438b9e950052dbbbc0*", "*IMPHASH=334d66c33503ccbf647c15b47c27eef4*", "*IMPHASH=27b0da080ef92afb37983d30d839141e*", "*IMPHASH=977eb4c263d384e47daa0712d34713ab*", "*IMPHASH=3eaadce9ae43d5a918bb082065815c3b*", "*IMPHASH=980fe6cf0d996ab1eedf877222e722aa*", "*IMPHASH=5f959422308ac3d721010d66647e100e*", "*IMPHASH=a49aaa3d03d1cd9c8dc7fca60f7f480b*", "*IMPHASH=dd335f759b6d5d6a8382b71dd9d65791*"))) and (CommandLine:"*ProxyCommand=*" or (CommandLine:"*PermitLocalCommand=yes*" and CommandLine:"* LocalCommand*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential RDP Tunneling Via SSH
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Execution of ssh.exe to perform data exfiltration and tunneling through RDP
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\ssh.exe" and CommandLine:"*:3389*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Amazon SSM Agent Hijacking
author: Muhammad Faisal
description: Detects potential Amazon SSM agent hijack attempts as outlined in the Mitiga research report.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\amazon-ssm-agent.exe" and (CommandLine:"*-register *" and CommandLine:"*-code *" and CommandLine:"*-id *" and CommandLine:"*-region *"))

name: SIGMA - Execution via stordiag.exe
author: Austin Songer (@austinsonger)
description: Detects the use of stordiag.exe to execute schtasks.exe systeminfo.exe and fltmc.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\stordiag.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\systeminfo.exe", "*\\fltmc.exe"))) and (not (ParentImage like~ ("c:\\windows\\system32\\*", "c:\\windows\\syswow64\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Start of NT Virtual DOS Machine
author: frack113
description: Ntvdm.exe allows the execution of 16-bit Windows applications on 32-bit Windows operating systems, as well as the execution of both 16-bit and 32-bit DOS applications
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*\\ntvdm.exe", "*\\csrstub.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Abused Debug Privilege by Arbitrary Parent Processes
author: Semanur Guneysu @semanurtg, oscd.community
description: Detection of unusual child processes by different system processes
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((ParentImage like~ ("*\\winlogon.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\csrss.exe", "*\\smss.exe", "*\\wininit.exe", "*\\spoolsv.exe", "*\\searchindexer.exe")) and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*"))) and ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "Cmd.Exe")))) and (not (CommandLine:"* route *" and CommandLine:"* ADD *")))

name: SIGMA - User Added to Local Administrators Group
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects addition of users to the local administrator group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*localgroup *" and CommandLine:"* /add*") or (CommandLine:"*Add-LocalGroupMember *" and CommandLine:"* -Group *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* administrators *", "* administrateur*")))

name: SIGMA - User Added To Highly Privileged Group
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects addition of users to highly privileged groups via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
false_positives: Administrative activity that must be investigated
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*localgroup *" and CommandLine:"* /add*") or (CommandLine:"*Add-LocalGroupMember *" and CommandLine:"* -Group *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Group Policy Creator Owners*", "*Schema Admins*")))

name: SIGMA - User Added to Remote Desktop Users Group
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects addition of users to the local Remote Desktop Users group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*localgroup *" and CommandLine:"* /add*") or (CommandLine:"*Add-LocalGroupMember *" and CommandLine:"* -Group *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Remote Desktop Users*", "*Utilisateurs du Bureau à distance*", "*Usuarios de escritorio remoto*")))

name: SIGMA - Execute From Alternate Data Streams
author: frack113
description: Detects execution from an Alternate Data Stream (ADS). Adversaries may use NTFS file attributes to hide their malicious data in order to evade detection
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*txt:*" and ((CommandLine:"*type *" and CommandLine:"* > *") or (CommandLine:"*makecab *" and CommandLine:"*.cab*") or (CommandLine:"*reg *" and CommandLine:"* export *") or (CommandLine:"*regedit *" and CommandLine:"* /E *") or (CommandLine:"*esentutl *" and CommandLine:"* /y *" and CommandLine:"* /d *" and CommandLine:"* /o *")))

name: SIGMA - Always Install Elevated Windows Installer
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang (rule), oscd.community
description: Detects Windows Installer service (msiexec.exe) trying to install MSI packages with SYSTEM privilege
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image:"*\\Windows\\Installer\\*" and Image:"*msi*") and Image:"*tmp") or (Image:"*\\msiexec.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384")))) and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and (not (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\services.exe" or (CommandLine:"*\\system32\\msiexec.exe /V" or ParentCommandLine:"*\\system32\\msiexec.exe /V") or ParentImage:"C:\\ProgramData\\Sophos\\*" or ParentImage:"C:\\ProgramData\\Avira\\*" or (ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\*")) or (ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Update\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Update\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Windows App Activity
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious child process of applications launched from inside the WindowsApps directory. This could be a sign of a rogue ".appx" package installation/execution
false_positives: Legitimate packages that make use of external binaries such as Windows Terminal
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\*" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*cmd /c*", "*Invoke-*", "*Base64*"))) and (not ((ParentImage:"*:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.WindowsTerminal*" and ParentImage:"*\\WindowsTerminal.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))) or (ParentImage:"C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.SysinternalsSuite*" and Image:"*\\cmd.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Arbitrary Shell Command Execution Via Settingcontent-Ms
author: Sreeman
description: The .SettingContent-ms file type was introduced in Windows 10 and allows a user to create "shortcuts" to various Windows 10 setting pages. These files are simply XML and contain paths to various Windows 10 settings binaries.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*.SettingContent-ms*" and (not CommandLine:"*immersivecontrolpanel*"))

name: SIGMA - Phishing Pattern ISO in Archive
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects cases in which an ISO files is opend within an archiver like 7Zip or Winrar, which is a sign of phishing as threat actors put small ISO files in archives as email attachments to bypass certain filters and protective measures (mark of web)
false_positives: Legitimate cases in which archives contain ISO or IMG files and the user opens the archive and the image via clicking and not extraction
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\Winrar.exe", "*\\7zFM.exe", "*\\peazip.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\isoburn.exe", "*\\PowerISO.exe", "*\\ImgBurn.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Automated Collection Command Prompt
author: frack113
description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*.doc*", "*.docx*", "*.xls*", "*.xlsx*", "*.ppt*", "*.pptx*", "*.rtf*", "*.pdf*", "*.txt*")) and ((CommandLine:"*dir *" and CommandLine:"* /b *" and CommandLine:"* /s *") or (OriginalFileName:"FINDSTR.EXE" and (CommandLine like~ ("* /e *", "* /si *")))))

name: SIGMA - Bad Opsec Defaults Sacrificial Processes With Improper Arguments
author: Oleg Kolesnikov @securonix invrep_de, oscd.community, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects attackers using tooling with bad opsec defaults.
E.g. spawning a sacrificial process to inject a capability into the process without taking into account how the process is normally run.
One trivial example of this is using rundll32.exe without arguments as a sacrificial process (default in CS, now highlighted by c2lint), running WerFault without arguments (Kraken - credit am0nsec), and other examples.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\WerFault.exe" and CommandLine:"*WerFault.exe") or (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe") or (Image:"*\\regsvcs.exe" and CommandLine:"*regsvcs.exe") or (Image:"*\\regasm.exe" and CommandLine:"*regasm.exe") or (Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe" and CommandLine:"*regsvr32.exe")) and (not ((ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\EdgeUpdate\\Install\\{*" and Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe") or ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\BraveSoftware\\Brave-Browser\\Application\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\Installer\\setup.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*--uninstall *" and Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine:"*rundll32.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Browser Launch From Document Reader Process
author: Joseph Kamau
description: Detects when a browser process or browser tab is launched from an application that handles document files such as Adobe, Microsoft Office, etc. And connects to a web application over http(s), this could indicate a possible phishing attempt.
false_positives: Unlikely in most cases, further investigation should be done in the commandline of the browser process to determine the context of the URL accessed.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*Acrobat Reader*", "*Microsoft Office*", "*PDF Reader*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\brave.exe", "*\\chrome.exe", "*\\firefox.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\opera.exe", "*\\maxthon.exe", "*\\seamonkey.exe", "*\\vivaldi.exe")) and CommandLine:"*http*") and (not CommandLine:"*https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*http://ad.foxitsoftware.com/adlog.php?*", "*https://globe-map.foxitservice.com/go.php?do=redirect*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Child Process Created as System
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Roberto Rodriguez (@Cyb3rWard0g), Open Threat Research (OTR)
description: Detection of child processes spawned with SYSTEM privileges by parents with LOCAL SERVICE or NETWORK SERVICE accounts
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentUser like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and (ParentUser like~ ("*\\NETWORK SERVICE", "*\\LOCAL SERVICE")) and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and (User like~ ("*\\SYSTEM", "*\\Système", "*\\СИСТЕМА")) and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384"))) and (not (Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine:"*DavSetCookie*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Commandline Obfuscation Using Escape Characters
author: juju4
description: Detects potential commandline obfuscation using known escape characters
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*h^t^t^p*", "*h\"t\"t\"p*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential CommandLine Obfuscation Using Unicode Characters From Suspicious Image
author: frack113, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Josh Nickels
description:
Detects potential commandline obfuscation using unicode characters.
Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents on the system or in transit.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.EXE", "cscript.exe", "PowerShell.EXE", "PowerShell_ISE.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "wscript.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*ˣ*", "*˪*", "*ˢ*", "*∕*", "*⁄*", "*―*", "*—*", "* *", "*¯*", "*®*", "*¶*", "*⠀*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Command Line Path Traversal Evasion Attempt
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential evasion or obfuscation attempts using bogus path traversal via the commandline
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\Windows\\*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\..\\Windows\\*", "*\\..\\System32\\*", "*\\..\\..\\*"))) or CommandLine:"*.exe\\..\\*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Google\\Drive\\googledrivesync.exe\\..\\*", "*\\Citrix\\Virtual Smart Card\\Citrix.Authentication.VirtualSmartcard.Launcher.exe\\..\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Browser Data Stealing
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("*copy-item*", "*copy *", "*cpi *", "* cp *", "*move *", "*move-item*", "* mi *", "* mv *")) or (Image like~ ("*\\esentutl.exe", "*\\xcopy.exe", "*\\robocopy.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("esentutl.exe", "XCOPY.EXE", "robocopy.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Amigo\\User Data*", "*\\BraveSoftware\\Brave-Browser\\User Data*", "*\\CentBrowser\\User Data*", "*\\Chromium\\User Data*", "*\\CocCoc\\Browser\\User Data*", "*\\Comodo\\Dragon\\User Data*", "*\\Elements Browser\\User Data*", "*\\Epic Privacy Browser\\User Data*", "*\\Google\\Chrome Beta\\User Data*", "*\\Google\\Chrome SxS\\User Data*", "*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*", "*\\Kometa\\User Data*", "*\\Maxthon5\\Users*", "*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data*", "*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles*", "*\\Nichrome\\User Data*", "*\\Opera Software\\Opera GX Stable\\*", "*\\Opera Software\\Opera Neon\\User Data*", "*\\Opera Software\\Opera Stable\\*", "*\\Orbitum\\User Data*", "*\\QIP Surf\\User Data*", "*\\Sputnik\\User Data*", "*\\Torch\\User Data*", "*\\uCozMedia\\Uran\\User Data*", "*\\Vivaldi\\User Data*")))

name: SIGMA - Copy From Or To Admin Share Or Sysvol Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, Zach Stanford @svch0st, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects a copy command or a copy utility execution to or from an Admin share or remote
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\\\*$*", "*\\Sysvol\\*")) and (((Image like~ ("*\\robocopy.exe", "*\\xcopy.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("robocopy.exe", "XCOPY.EXE"))) or ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine:"*copy*") or (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe*", "*\\pwsh.exe*")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*copy-item*", "*copy *", "*cpi *", "* cp *", "*move *", "*move-item*", "* mi *", "* mv *")))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Copy From or To System Directory
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a program from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk.
Often used to move LOLBINs such as 'certutil' or 'desktopimgdownldr' to a different location with a different name in order to bypass detections based on locations.
false_positives: Depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment (For example an admin scripts like https://www.itexperience.net/sccm-batch-files-and-32-bits-processes-on-64-bits-os/)
When cmd.exe and xcopy.exe are called directly
When the command contains the keywords but not in the correct order
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*copy *") or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*copy-item*", "* copy *", "*cpi *", "* cp *"))) or ((Image like~ ("*\\robocopy.exe", "*\\xcopy.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("robocopy.exe", "XCOPY.EXE")))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\System32*", "*\\SysWOW64*", "*\\WinSxS*")) and (not (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/c copy*" and CommandLine:"*\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\avira_system_speedup.exe*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*C:\\Program Files\\Avira\\*", "*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avira\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - LOL-Binary Copied From System Directory
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious copy operation that tries to copy a known LOLBIN from system (System32, SysWOW64, WinSxS) directories to another on disk in order to bypass detections based on locations.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*copy *") or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*copy-item*", "* copy *", "*cpi *", "* cp *"))) or ((Image like~ ("*\\robocopy.exe", "*\\xcopy.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("robocopy.exe", "XCOPY.EXE")))) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\System32*", "*\\SysWOW64*", "*\\WinSxS*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\bitsadmin.exe*", "*\\calc.exe*", "*\\certutil.exe*", "*\\cmdl32.exe*", "*\\cscript.exe*", "*\\mshta.exe*", "*\\rundll32.exe*", "*\\wscript.exe*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Crypto Mining Activity
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command line parameters or strings often used by crypto miners
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* --cpu-priority=*", "*--donate-level=0*", "* -o pool.*", "* --nicehash*", "* --algo=rx/0 *", "*stratum+tcp://*", "*stratum+udp://*", "*LS1kb25hdGUtbGV2ZWw9*", "*0tZG9uYXRlLWxldmVsP*", "*tLWRvbmF0ZS1sZXZlbD*", "*c3RyYXR1bSt0Y3A6Ly*", "*N0cmF0dW0rdGNwOi8v*", "*zdHJhdHVtK3RjcDovL*", "*c3RyYXR1bSt1ZHA6Ly*", "*N0cmF0dW0rdWRwOi8v*", "*zdHJhdHVtK3VkcDovL*")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* pool.c *", "* pool.o *", "*gcc -*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Data Exfiltration Activity Via CommandLine Tools
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of various CLI utilities exfiltrating data via web requests
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*curl *", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*irm *", "*iwr *", "*wget *")) and (CommandLine:"* -ur*" and CommandLine:"* -me*" and CommandLine:"* -b*" and CommandLine:"* POST *")) or ((Image:"*\\curl.exe" and CommandLine:"*--ur*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* -d *", "* --data *"))) or (Image:"*\\wget.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*--post-data*", "*--post-file*")))) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*Get-Content*", "*GetBytes*", "*hostname*", "*ifconfig*", "*ipconfig*", "*net view*", "*netstat*", "*nltest*", "*qprocess*", "*sc query*", "*systeminfo*", "*tasklist*", "*ToBase64String*", "*whoami*")) or (CommandLine:"*type *" and CommandLine:"* > *" and CommandLine:"* C:\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Raccine Uninstall
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects commands that indicate a Raccine removal from an end system. Raccine is a free ransomware protection tool.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*taskkill *" and CommandLine:"*RaccineSettings.exe*") or (CommandLine:"*reg.exe*" and CommandLine:"*delete*" and CommandLine:"*Raccine Tray*") or (CommandLine:"*schtasks*" and CommandLine:"*/DELETE*" and CommandLine:"*Raccine Rules Updater*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Double Extension File Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), @blu3_team (idea), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious use of an .exe extension after a non-executable file extension like .pdf.exe, a set of spaces or underlines to cloak the executable file in spear phishing campaigns
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*      .exe", "*______.exe", "*.doc.exe", "*.doc.js", "*.docx.exe", "*.docx.js", "*.gif.exe", "*.jpeg.exe", "*.jpg.exe", "*.mkv.exe", "*.mov.exe", "*.mp3.exe", "*.mp4.exe", "*.pdf.exe", "*.pdf.js", "*.png.exe", "*.ppt.exe", "*.ppt.js", "*.pptx.exe", "*.pptx.js", "*.rtf.exe", "*.rtf.js", "*.svg.exe", "*.txt.exe", "*.txt.js", "*.xls.exe", "*.xls.js", "*.xlsx.exe", "*.xlsx.js", "*⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*      .exe*", "*______.exe*", "*.doc.exe*", "*.doc.js*", "*.docx.exe*", "*.docx.js*", "*.gif.exe*", "*.jpeg.exe*", "*.jpg.exe*", "*.mkv.exe*", "*.mov.exe*", "*.mp3.exe*", "*.mp4.exe*", "*.pdf.exe*", "*.pdf.js*", "*.png.exe*", "*.ppt.exe*", "*.ppt.js*", "*.pptx.exe*", "*.pptx.js*", "*.rtf.exe*", "*.rtf.js*", "*.svg.exe*", "*.txt.exe*", "*.txt.js*", "*.xls.exe*", "*.xls.js*", "*.xlsx.exe*", "*.xlsx.js*", "*⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Parent Double Extension File Execution
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect execution of suspicious double extension files in ParentCommandLine
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*.doc.lnk", "*.docx.lnk", "*.xls.lnk", "*.xlsx.lnk", "*.ppt.lnk", "*.pptx.lnk", "*.rtf.lnk", "*.pdf.lnk", "*.txt.lnk", "*.doc.js", "*.docx.js", "*.xls.js", "*.xlsx.js", "*.ppt.js", "*.pptx.js", "*.rtf.js", "*.pdf.js", "*.txt.js")) or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*.doc.lnk*", "*.docx.lnk*", "*.xls.lnk*", "*.xlsx.lnk*", "*.ppt.lnk*", "*.pptx.lnk*", "*.rtf.lnk*", "*.pdf.lnk*", "*.txt.lnk*", "*.doc.js*", "*.docx.js*", "*.xls.js*", "*.xlsx.js*", "*.ppt.js*", "*.pptx.js*", "*.rtf.js*", "*.pdf.js*", "*.txt.js*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Download from Office Domain
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious ways to download files from Microsoft domains that are used to store attachments in Emails or OneNote documents
false_positives: Scripts or tools that download attachments from these domains (OneNote, Outlook 365)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\curl.exe", "*\\wget.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*iwr *", "*curl *", "*wget *", "*Start-BitsTransfer*", "*.DownloadFile(*", "*.DownloadString(*"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*https://attachment.outlook.live.net/owa/*", "*https://onenoteonlinesync.onenote.com/onenoteonlinesync/*")))

name: SIGMA - DumpStack.log Defender Evasion
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of the filename DumpStack.log to evade Microsoft Defender
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\DumpStack.log" or CommandLine:"* -o DumpStack.log*")

name: SIGMA - Always Install Elevated MSI Spawned Cmd And Powershell
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang (rule), oscd.community
description: Detects Windows Installer service (msiexec.exe) spawning "cmd" or "powershell"
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and ((ParentImage:"*\\Windows\\Installer\\*" and ParentImage:"*msi*") and ParentImage:"*tmp"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Electron Application Child Processes
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious child processes of electron apps (teams, discord, slack, etc.). This could be a potential sign of ".asar" file tampering (See reference section for more information) or binary execution proxy through specific CLI arguments (see related rule)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\chrome.exe", "*\\discord.exe", "*\\GitHubDesktop.exe", "*\\keybase.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe", "*\\msteams.exe", "*\\slack.exe", "*\\teams.exe")) and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\whoami.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))) and (not (ParentImage:"*\\Discord.exe" and Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine:"*\\NVSMI\\nvidia-smi.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Electron Application CommandLine
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious CommandLine of electron apps (teams, discord, slack, etc.). This could be a sign of abuse to proxy execution through a signed binary.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\chrome.exe", "*\\code.exe", "*\\discord.exe", "*\\GitHubDesktop.exe", "*\\keybase.exe", "*\\msedge_proxy.exe", "*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe", "*\\msteams.exe", "*\\slack.exe", "*\\Teams.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("chrome.exe", "code.exe", "discord.exe", "GitHubDesktop.exe", "keybase.exe", "msedge_proxy.exe", "msedge.exe", "msedgewebview2.exe", "msteams.exe", "slack.exe", "Teams.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*--browser-subprocess-path*", "*--gpu-launcher*", "*--renderer-cmd-prefix*", "*--utility-cmd-prefix*")))

name: SIGMA - Elevated System Shell Spawned From Uncommon Parent Location
author: frack113, Tim Shelton (update fp)
description: Detects when a shell program such as the Windows command prompt or PowerShell is launched with system privileges from a uncommon parent location.
false_positives: Some legitimate applications may spawn shells from uncommon parent locations. Apply additional filters and perform an initial baseline before deploying.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "powershell_ise.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "Cmd.Exe"))) and ((User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and LogonId:"0x3e7")) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\*", "*:\\ProgramData\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")) or (?ParentImage == null) or (ParentImage like~ ("", "-")))) and (not ((ParentImage:"*:\\ManageEngine\\ADManager Plus\\pgsql\\bin\\postgres.exe" and Image:"*\\cmd.exe") or (CommandLine:"*:\\WINDOWS\\system32\\cmd.exe /c \"*" and CurrentDirectory:"*:\\WINDOWS\\Temp\\asgard2-agent\\*") or (ParentImage:"*:\\IBM\\SpectrumProtect\\webserver\\scripts\\*" and CommandLine:"*:\\IBM\\SpectrumProtect\\webserver\\scripts\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Hidden Powershell in Link File Pattern
author: frack113
description: Detects events that appear when a user click on a link file with a powershell command in it
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe" and Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*powershell*" and CommandLine:"*.lnk*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 1
author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 2
author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 3
author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Activity Via Emoji Usage In CommandLine - 4
author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
description: Detects the usage of emojis in the command line, this could be a sign of potential defense evasion activity.

name: SIGMA - ETW Logging Tamper In .NET Processes Via CommandLine
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description:
Detects changes to environment variables related to ETW logging via the CommandLine.
This could indicate potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*COMPlus_ETWEnabled*", "*COMPlus_ETWFlags*"))

name: SIGMA - ETW Trace Evasion Activity
author: @neu5ron, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects command line activity that tries to clear or disable any ETW trace log which could be a sign of logging evasion.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*cl*" and CommandLine:"*/Trace*") or (CommandLine:"*clear-log*" and CommandLine:"*/Trace*") or (CommandLine:"*sl*" and CommandLine:"*/e:false*") or (CommandLine:"*set-log*" and CommandLine:"*/e:false*") or (CommandLine:"*logman*" and CommandLine:"*update*" and CommandLine:"*trace*" and CommandLine:"*--p*" and CommandLine:"*-ets*") or CommandLine:"*Remove-EtwTraceProvider*" or (CommandLine:"*Set-EtwTraceProvider*" and CommandLine:"*0x11*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Eventlog Clearing or Configuration Change Activity
author: Ecco, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, D3F7A5105, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the clearing or configuration tampering of EventLog using utilities such as "wevtutil", "powershell" and "wmic".
This technique were seen used by threat actors and ransomware strains in order to evade defenses.
false_positives: Scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\wevtutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wevtutil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*clear-log *", "* cl *", "*set-log *", "* sl *", "*lfn:*"))) or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*Clear-EventLog *", "*Remove-EventLog *", "*Limit-EventLog *", "*Clear-WinEvent *")) or (CommandLine:"*Eventing.Reader.EventLogSession*" and CommandLine:"*ClearLog*") or (CommandLine:"*Diagnostics.EventLog*" and CommandLine:"*Clear*"))) or (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wmic.exe")) and CommandLine:"*ClearEventLog*") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\msiexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe")) and CommandLine:"* sl *"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious EventLog Recon Activity Using Log Query Utilities
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of different log query utilities and commands to search and dump the content of specific event logs or look for specific event IDs.
This technique is used by threat actors in order to extract sensitive information from events logs such as usernames, IP addresses, hostnames, etc.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the utility by administrators to query the event log
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine like~ ("*Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell*", "*Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing*", "*Microsoft-Windows-TerminalServices-LocalSessionManager*", "*Microsoft-Windows-TerminalServices-RemoteConnectionManager*", "*Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender*", "*PowerShellCore*", "*Security*", "*Windows PowerShell*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*-InstanceId 462?*", "*.eventid -eq 462?*", "*EventCode=?462?*", "*EventIdentifier=?462?*", "*System[EventID=462?]*", "*-InstanceId 4778*", "*.eventid -eq 4778*", "*System[EventID=4778]*", "*EventCode=?4778?*", "*EventIdentifier=?4778?*", "*-InstanceId 25*", "*.eventid -eq 25*", "*System[EventID=25]*", "*EventCode=?25?*", "*EventIdentifier=?25?*"))) and ((CommandLine:"*Select*" and CommandLine:"*Win32_NTLogEvent*") or ((Image:"*\\wevtutil.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wevtutil.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("* qe *", "* query-events *"))) or ((Image:"*\\wmic.exe" or OriginalFileName:"wmic.exe") and CommandLine:"* ntevent*") or (CommandLine like~ ("*Get-WinEvent *", "*get-eventlog *"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Execution From Parent Process In Public Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a potentially suspicious execution of a parent process located in the "\Users\Public" folder executing a child process containing references to shell or scripting binaries and commandlines.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*:\\Users\\Public\\*" and ((Image like~ ("*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*bitsadmin*", "*certutil*", "*cscript*", "*mshta*", "*powershell*", "*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*wscript*"))))

name: SIGMA - Process Execution From A Potentially Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
description: Detects a potentially suspicious execution from an uncommon folder.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\All Users\\*", "*:\\Users\\Default\\*", "*:\\Users\\NetworkService\\*", "*:\\Windows\\addins\\*", "*:\\Windows\\debug\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Fonts\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Help\\*", "*:\\Windows\\IME\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Media\\*", "*:\\Windows\\repair\\*", "*:\\Windows\\security\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*$Recycle.bin*", "*\\config\\systemprofile\\*", "*\\Intel\\Logs\\*", "*\\RSA\\MachineKeys\\*")) and (not (Image:"C:\\Users\\Public\\IBM\\ClientSolutions\\Start_Programs\\*" or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\config\\systemprofile\\Citrix\\UpdaterBinaries\\*" and Image:"*\\CitrixReceiverUpdater.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious File Characteristics Due to Missing Fields
author: Markus Neis, Sander Wiebing
description: Detects Executables in the Downloads folder without FileVersion,Description,Product,Company likely created with py2exe
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Description:"\?" and FileVersion:"\?") or (Description:"\?" and Product:"\?") or (Description:"\?" and Company:"\?")) and Image:"*\\Downloads\\*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Reconnaissance Activity Via GatherNetworkInfo.VBS
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the built-in script located in "C:\Windows\System32\gatherNetworkInfo.vbs". Which can be used to gather information about the target machine
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*gatherNetworkInfo.vbs*" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Hidden Directory Creation Via NTFS INDEX_ALLOCATION Stream - CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Scoubi (@ScoubiMtl)
description: Detects command line containing reference to the "::$index_allocation" stream, which can be used as a technique to prevent access to folders or files from tooling such as "explorer.exe" or "powershell.exe"
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*::$index_allocation*"

name: SIGMA - Writing Of Malicious Files To The Fonts Folder
author: Sreeman
description: Monitors for the hiding possible malicious files in the C:\Windows\Fonts\ location. This folder doesn't require admin privillege to be written and executed from.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*echo*", "*copy*", "*type*", "*file createnew*", "*cacls*")) and CommandLine:"*C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*.sh*", "*.exe*", "*.dll*", "*.bin*", "*.bat*", "*.cmd*", "*.js*", "*.msh*", "*.reg*", "*.scr*", "*.ps*", "*.vb*", "*.jar*", "*.pl*", "*.inf*", "*.cpl*", "*.hta*", "*.msi*", "*.vbs*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Homoglyph Attack Using Lookalike Characters
author: Micah Babinski, @micahbabinski
description:
Detects the presence of unicode characters which are homoglyphs, or identical in appearance, to ASCII letter characters.
This is used as an obfuscation and masquerading techniques. Only "perfect" homoglyphs are included; these are characters that
are indistinguishable from ASCII characters and thus may make excellent candidates for homoglyph attack characters.
false_positives: Commandlines with legitimate Cyrillic text; will likely require tuning (or not be usable) in countries where these alphabets are in use.

name: SIGMA - Execution Of Non-Existing File
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Checks whether the image specified in a process creation event is not a full, absolute path (caused by process ghosting or other unorthodox methods to start a process)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((not Image:"*\\*") and (not ((?Image == null) or (Image like~ ("-", "")) or ((Image like~ ("System", "Registry", "MemCompression", "vmmem")) or (CommandLine like~ ("Registry", "MemCompression", "vmmem"))))))

name: SIGMA - Base64 MZ Header In CommandLine
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects encoded base64 MZ header in the commandline
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*TVqQAAMAAAAEAAAA*", "*TVpQAAIAAAAEAA8A*", "*TVqAAAEAAAAEABAA*", "*TVoAAAAAAAAAAAAA*", "*TVpTAQEAAAAEAAAA*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Inline JavaScript Execution via NodeJS Binary
author: Microsoft (idea), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potentially suspicious inline JavaScript execution using Node.js with specific keywords in the command line.
false_positives: Legitimate scripts using Node.js with these modules
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\node.exe" or OriginalFileName:"node.exe" or Product:"Node.js") and (CommandLine:"*http*" and CommandLine:"*execSync*" and CommandLine:"*spawn*" and CommandLine:"*fs*" and CommandLine:"*path*" and CommandLine:"*zlib*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential WinAPI Calls Via CommandLine
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of WinAPI Functions via the commandline. As seen used by threat actors via the tool winapiexec
false_positives: Some legitimate action or applications may use these functions. Investigate further to determine the legitimacy of the activity.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*AddSecurityPackage*", "*AdjustTokenPrivileges*", "*Advapi32*", "*CloseHandle*", "*CreateProcessWithToken*", "*CreatePseudoConsole*", "*CreateRemoteThread*", "*CreateThread*", "*CreateUserThread*", "*DangerousGetHandle*", "*DuplicateTokenEx*", "*EnumerateSecurityPackages*", "*FreeHGlobal*", "*FreeLibrary*", "*GetDelegateForFunctionPointer*", "*GetLogonSessionData*", "*GetModuleHandle*", "*GetProcAddress*", "*GetProcessHandle*", "*GetTokenInformation*", "*ImpersonateLoggedOnUser*", "*kernel32*", "*LoadLibrary*", "*memcpy*", "*MiniDumpWriteDump*", "*ntdll*", "*OpenDesktop*", "*OpenProcess*", "*OpenProcessToken*", "*OpenThreadToken*", "*OpenWindowStation*", "*PtrToString*", "*QueueUserApc*", "*ReadProcessMemory*", "*RevertToSelf*", "*RtlCreateUserThread*", "*secur32*", "*SetThreadToken*", "*VirtualAlloc*", "*VirtualFree*", "*VirtualProtect*", "*WaitForSingleObject*", "*WriteInt32*", "*WriteProcessMemory*", "*ZeroFreeGlobalAllocUnicode*")) and (not ((Image:"*\\MpCmdRun.exe" and CommandLine:"*GetLoadLibraryWAddress32*") or (ParentImage:"*\\CompatTelRunner.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*FreeHGlobal*", "*PtrToString*", "*kernel32*", "*CloseHandle*"))))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious JWT Token Search Via CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), kagebunsher
description:
Detects potentially suspicious search for JWT tokens via CLI by looking for the string "eyJ0eX" or "eyJhbG".
JWT tokens are often used for access-tokens across various applications and services like Microsoft 365, Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, and others.
Threat actors may search for these tokens to steal them for lateral movement or privilege escalation.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*find *", "*find.exe*", "*findstr*", "*select-string *", "*strings*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*eyJ0eXAiOi*", "*eyJhbGciOi*", "* eyJ0eX*", "* \"eyJ0eX\"*", "* 'eyJ0eX'*", "* eyJhbG*", "* \"eyJhbG\"*", "* 'eyJhbG'*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious LNK Command-Line Padding with Whitespace Characters
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects exploitation of LNK file command-line length discrepancy, where attackers hide malicious commands beyond the 260-character UI limit while the actual command-line argument field supports 4096 characters using whitespace padding (e.g., 0x20, 0x09-0x0D).
Adversaries insert non-printable whitespace characters (e.g., Line Feed \x0A, Carriage Return \x0D) to pad the visible section of the LNK file, pushing malicious commands past the UI-visible boundary.
The hidden payload, executed at runtime but invisible in Windows Explorer properties, enables stealthy execution and evasion—commonly used for social engineering attacks.
This rule flags suspicious use of such padding observed in real-world attacks.
query:
any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\explorer.exe" or ParentCommandLine:"*.lnk*") and ((CommandLine like~ ("*                 *", "*\u0009*", "*\u000A*", "*\u0011*", "*\u0012*", "*\u0013*", "*\u000B*", "*\u000C*", "*\u000D*")) or CommandLine regex~ "





"))

name: SIGMA - Local Accounts Discovery
author: Timur Zinniatullin, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Local accounts, System Owner/User discovery using operating systems utilities
false_positives: Legitimate administrator or user enumerates local users for legitimate reason
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"* /c*" and CommandLine:"*dir *" and CommandLine:"*\\Users\\*")) and (not CommandLine:"* rmdir *")) or (((Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe")) and CommandLine:"*user*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*/domain*", "*/add*", "*/delete*", "*/active*", "*/expires*", "*/passwordreq*", "*/scriptpath*", "*/times*", "*/workstations*")))) or (((Image like~ ("*\\whoami.exe", "*\\quser.exe", "*\\qwinsta.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("whoami.exe", "quser.exe", "qwinsta.exe"))) or (Image:"*\\wmic.exe" and (CommandLine:"*useraccount*" and CommandLine:"*get*")) or (Image:"*\\cmdkey.exe" and CommandLine:"* /l*")))

name: SIGMA - LOLBIN Execution From Abnormal Drive
author: Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io', Angelo Violetti - SEC Consult '@angelo_violetti', Aaron Herman
description: Detects LOLBINs executing from an abnormal or uncommon drive such as a mounted ISO.
false_positives: Rare false positives could occur on servers with multiple drives.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cmstp.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\installutil.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("CALC.EXE", "CertUtil.exe", "CMSTP.EXE", "cscript.exe", "installutil.exe", "MSHTA.EXE", "REGSVR32.EXE", "RUNDLL32.EXE", "wscript.exe"))) and (not (CurrentDirectory:"*C:\\*" or CurrentDirectory=="" or (?CurrentDirectory == null))))

name: SIGMA - LSASS Dump Keyword In CommandLine
author: E.M. Anhaus, Tony Lambert, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the presence of the keywords "lsass" and ".dmp" in the commandline, which could indicate a potential attempt to dump or create a dump of the lsass process.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*lsass.dmp*", "*lsass.zip*", "*lsass.rar*", "*Andrew.dmp*", "*Coredump.dmp*", "*NotLSASS.zip*", "*lsass_2*", "*lsassdump*", "*lsassdmp*")) or (CommandLine:"*lsass*" and CommandLine:"*.dmp*") or (CommandLine:"*SQLDmpr*" and CommandLine:"*.mdmp*") or (CommandLine:"*nanodump*" and CommandLine:"*.dmp*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential File Download Via MS-AppInstaller Protocol Handler
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description:
Detects usage of the "ms-appinstaller" protocol handler via command line to potentially download arbitrary files via AppInstaller.EXE
The downloaded files are temporarly stored in ":\Users\%username%\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.DesktopAppInstaller_8wekyb3d8bbwe\AC\INetCache\<RANDOM-8-CHAR-DIRECTORY>"
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*ms-appinstaller://?source=*" and CommandLine:"*http*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Network Command
author: frack113, Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io'
description: Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*ipconfig /all*", "*netsh interface show interface*", "*arp -a*", "*nbtstat -n*", "*net config*", "*route print*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scan Loop Network
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of other systems by IP address, hostname, or other logical identifier on a network that may be used for Lateral Movement from the current system
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*for *", "*foreach *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*nslookup*", "*ping*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Network Sniffing Activity Using Network Tools
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects potential network sniffing via use of network tools such as "tshark", "windump".
Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection.
An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.
false_positives: Legitimate administration activity to troubleshoot network issues
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tshark.exe" and CommandLine:"*-i*") or Image:"*\\windump.exe")

name: SIGMA - Execution of Suspicious File Type Extension
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects whether the image specified in a process creation event doesn't refer to an ".exe" (or other known executable extension) file. This can be caused by process ghosting or other unorthodox methods to start a process.
This rule might require some initial baselining to align with some third party tooling in the user environment.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((not (Image like~ ("*.bin", "*.cgi", "*.com", "*.exe", "*.scr", "*.tmp"))) and (not ((Image like~ ("System", "Registry", "MemCompression", "vmmem")) or Image:"*:\\Windows\\Installer\\MSI*" or Image:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\DriverStore\\FileRepository\\*" or (Image:"*:\\Config.Msi\\*" and (Image like~ ("*.rbf", "*.rbs"))) or (ParentImage:"*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*" or Image:"*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*") or Image:"*:\\$Extend\\$Deleted\\*" or (Image like~ ("-", "")) or (?Image == null))) and (not (ParentImage:"*:\\ProgramData\\Avira\\*" or (Image:"*NVIDIA\\NvBackend\\*" and Image:"*.dat") or ((Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\WINPAKPRO\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\WINPAKPRO\\*")) and Image:"*.ngn") or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\MyQ\\Server\\pcltool.dll", "*:\\Program Files\\MyQ\\Server\\pcltool.dll")) or (Image:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Packages\\*" and Image:"*\\LocalState\\rootfs\\*") or Image:"*\\LZMA_EXE" or Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\*" or (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\services.exe" and Image:"*com.docker.service"))))

name: SIGMA - Non-privileged Usage of Reg or Powershell
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Ryan Plas (rule), oscd.community
description: Search for usage of reg or Powershell by non-privileged users to modify service configuration in registry
query: any where EventID:1 and (((CommandLine:"*reg *" and CommandLine:"*add*") or (CommandLine like~ ("*powershell*", "*set-itemproperty*", "* sp *", "*new-itemproperty*"))) and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("Medium", "S-1-16-8192")) and (CommandLine:"*ControlSet*" and CommandLine:"*Services*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ImagePath*", "*FailureCommand*", "*ServiceDLL*"))))

name: SIGMA - Process Launched Without Image Name
author: Matt Anderson (Huntress)
description: Detect the use of processes with no name (".exe"), which can be used to evade Image-based detections.
query: any where EventID:1 and Image:"*\\.exe"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Patterns NTDS.DIT Exfil
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process patterns used in NTDS.DIT exfiltration
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\NTDSDump.exe", "*\\NTDSDumpEx.exe")) or (CommandLine:"*ntds.dit*" and CommandLine:"*system.hiv*") or CommandLine:"*NTDSgrab.ps1*") or (CommandLine:"*ac i ntds*" and CommandLine:"*create full*") or (CommandLine:"*/c copy *" and CommandLine:"*\\windows\\ntds\\ntds.dit*") or (CommandLine:"*activate instance ntds*" and CommandLine:"*create full*") or (CommandLine:"*powershell*" and CommandLine:"*ntds.dit*")) or (CommandLine:"*ntds.dit*" and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\apache*", "*\\tomcat*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Public\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*")) or (Image like~ ("*\\apache*", "*\\tomcat*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Public\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Call To Win32_NTEventlogFile Class
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the WMI class "Win32_NTEventlogFile" in a potentially suspicious way (delete, backup, change permissions, etc.) from a PowerShell script
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*Win32_NTEventlogFile*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*.BackupEventlog(*", "*.ChangeSecurityPermissions(*", "*.ChangeSecurityPermissionsEx(*", "*.ClearEventLog(*", "*.Delete(*", "*.DeleteEx(*", "*.Rename(*", "*.TakeOwnerShip(*", "*.TakeOwnerShipEx(*")))

name: SIGMA - Use Short Name Path in Image
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image detection
false_positives: Applications could use this notation occasionally which might generate some false positives. In that case Investigate the parent and child process.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*~1\\*", "*~2\\*")) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\Dism.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\cleanmgr.exe")) or ((Image:"*\\AppData\\*" and Image:"*\\Temp\\*") or (Image like~ ("*~1\\unzip.exe", "*~1\\7zG.exe"))))) and (not (ParentImage:"*\\WebEx\\WebexHost.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\thor\\thor64.exe" or (Product:"InstallShield (R)" or Description:"InstallShield (R) Setup Engine" or Company:"InstallShield Software Corporation"))))

name: SIGMA - Use NTFS Short Name in Command Line
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid command-line detection
false_positives: Applications could use this notation occasionally which might generate some false positives. In that case Investigate the parent and child process.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*~1.exe*", "*~1.bat*", "*~1.msi*", "*~1.vbe*", "*~1.vbs*", "*~1.dll*", "*~1.ps1*", "*~1.js*", "*~1.hta*", "*~2.exe*", "*~2.bat*", "*~2.msi*", "*~2.vbe*", "*~2.vbs*", "*~2.dll*", "*~2.ps1*", "*~2.js*", "*~2.hta*")) and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\WebEx\\WebexHost.exe", "*\\thor\\thor64.exe")) or CommandLine:"*C:\\xampp\\vcredist\\VCREDI~1.EXE*")))

name: SIGMA - Use NTFS Short Name in Image
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid Image based detection
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*~1.bat*", "*~1.dll*", "*~1.exe*", "*~1.hta*", "*~1.js*", "*~1.msi*", "*~1.ps1*", "*~1.tmp*", "*~1.vbe*", "*~1.vbs*", "*~2.bat*", "*~2.dll*", "*~2.exe*", "*~2.hta*", "*~2.js*", "*~2.msi*", "*~2.ps1*", "*~2.tmp*", "*~2.vbe*", "*~2.vbs*")) and (not ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe") and (not (ParentImage:"*\\WebEx\\WebexHost.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\thor\\thor64.exe" or Image:"C:\\PROGRA~1\\WinZip\\WZPREL~1.EXE" or Image:"*\\VCREDI~1.EXE")))

name: SIGMA - Obfuscated IP Download Activity
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects use of an encoded/obfuscated version of an IP address (hex, octal...) in an URL combined with a download command
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*iwr *", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*irm *", "*wget *", "*curl *", "*DownloadFile*", "*DownloadString*")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("* 0x*", "*//0x*", "*.0x*", "*.00x*")) or (CommandLine:"*http://%*" and CommandLine:"*%2e*") or (CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.0[0-9]{3,4}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.0[0-9]{3,7}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/0[0-9]{3,11}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/(0[0-9]{1,11}\.){3}0[0-9]{1,11}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/0[0-9]{1,11}" or CommandLine regex~ " [0-7]{7,13}")) and (not CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/((25[0-5]|(2[0-4]|1\d|[1-9])?\d)(\.|\b)){4}"))

name: SIGMA - Obfuscated IP Via CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of an encoded/obfuscated version of an IP address (hex, octal, etc.) via command line
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\ping.exe", "*\\arp.exe")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("* 0x*", "*//0x*", "*.0x*", "*.00x*")) or (CommandLine:"*http://%*" and CommandLine:"*%2e*") or (CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.0[0-9]{3,4}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/[0-9]{1,3}\.0[0-9]{3,7}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/0[0-9]{3,11}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/(0[0-9]{1,11}\.){3}0[0-9]{1,11}" or CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/0[0-9]{1,11}" or CommandLine regex~ " [0-7]{7,13}")) and (not CommandLine regex~ "https?:\/\/((25[0-5]|(2[0-4]|1\d|[1-9])?\d)(\.|\b)){4}"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Parents
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious parent processes that should not have any children or should only have a single possible child program
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\minesweeper.exe", "*\\winver.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe")) or ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\csrss.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\eventvwr.exe", "*\\calc.exe", "*\\notepad.exe")) and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\WerFault.exe", "*\\wermgr.exe", "*\\conhost.exe", "*\\mmc.exe", "*\\win32calc.exe", "*\\notepad.exe")) or (?Image == null)))))

name: SIGMA - Potential PowerShell Execution Via DLL
