author: Markus Neis, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects potential PowerShell execution from a DLL instead of the usual PowerShell process as seen used in PowerShdll.
This detection assumes that PowerShell commands are passed via the CommandLine.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\InstallUtil.exe", "*\\RegAsm.exe", "*\\RegSvcs.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("InstallUtil.exe", "RegAsm.exe", "RegSvcs.exe", "REGSVR32.EXE", "RUNDLL32.EXE"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Default.GetString*", "*DownloadString*", "*FromBase64String*", "*ICM *", "*IEX *", "*Invoke-Command*", "*Invoke-Expression*")))

name: SIGMA - Private Keys Reconnaissance Via CommandLine Tools
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Adversaries may search for private key certificate files on compromised systems for insecurely stored credential
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*.key*", "*.pgp*", "*.gpg*", "*.ppk*", "*.p12*", "*.pem*", "*.pfx*", "*.cer*", "*.p7b*", "*.asc*")) and (((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"Cmd.Exe") and CommandLine:"*dir *") or (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll"))) and CommandLine:"*Get-ChildItem *") or (Image:"*\\findstr.exe" or OriginalFileName:"FINDSTR.EXE")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious RunAs-Like Flag Combination
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious command line flags that let the user set a target user and command as e.g. seen in PsExec-like tools
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* -u system *", "* --user system *", "* -u NT*", "* -u \"NT*", "* -u 'NT*", "* --system *", "* -u administrator *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -c cmd*", "* -c \"cmd*", "* -c powershell*", "* -c \"powershell*", "* --command cmd*", "* --command powershell*", "* -c whoami*", "* -c wscript*", "* -c cscript*")))

name: SIGMA - Privilege Escalation via Named Pipe Impersonation
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects a remote file copy attempt to a hidden network share. This may indicate lateral movement or data staging activity.
false_positives: Other programs that cause these patterns (please report)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "PowerShell.EXE"))) and (CommandLine:"*echo*" and CommandLine:"*>*" and CommandLine:"*\\\\.\\pipe\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Processes Suspicious Parent Directory
author: vburov
description: Detect suspicious parent processes of well-known Windows processes
false_positives: Some security products seem to spawn these
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\svchost.exe", "*\\taskhost.exe", "*\\lsm.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\lsaiso.exe", "*\\csrss.exe", "*\\wininit.exe", "*\\winlogon.exe")) and (not (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\SavService.exe", "*\\ngen.exe")) or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\System32\\*", "*\\SysWOW64\\*"))) or ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\Windows Defender\\*", "*\\Microsoft Security Client\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\MsMpEng.exe") or ((?ParentImage == null) or (ParentImage like~ ("", "-"))))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Program Names
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious patterns in program names or folders that are often found in malicious samples or hacktools
false_positives: Legitimate tools that accidentally match on the searched patterns
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\CVE-202*", "*\\CVE202*")) or (Image like~ ("*\\poc.exe", "*\\artifact.exe", "*\\artifact64.exe", "*\\artifact_protected.exe", "*\\artifact32.exe", "*\\artifact32big.exe", "*obfuscated.exe", "*obfusc.exe", "*\\meterpreter"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("*inject.ps1*", "*Invoke-CVE*", "*pupy.ps1*", "*payload.ps1*", "*beacon.ps1*", "*PowerView.ps1*", "*bypass.ps1*", "*obfuscated.ps1*", "*obfusc.ps1*", "*obfus.ps1*", "*obfs.ps1*", "*evil.ps1*", "*MiniDogz.ps1*", "*_enc.ps1*", "*\\shell.ps1*", "*\\rshell.ps1*", "*revshell.ps1*", "*\\av.ps1*", "*\\av_test.ps1*", "*adrecon.ps1*", "*mimikatz.ps1*", "*\\PowerUp_*", "*powerup.ps1*", "*\\Temp\\a.ps1*", "*\\Temp\\p.ps1*", "*\\Temp\\1.ps1*", "*Hound.ps1*", "*encode.ps1*", "*powercat.ps1*")))

name: SIGMA - Recon Information for Export with Command Prompt
author: frack113
description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\tree.com", "*\\WMIC.exe", "*\\doskey.exe", "*\\sc.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("wmic.exe", "DOSKEY.EXE", "sc.exe"))) and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("* > %TEMP%\\*", "* > %TMP%\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Execution From Fake Recycle.Bin Folder
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects process execution from a fake recycle bin folder, often used to avoid security solution.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*RECYCLERS.BIN\\*", "*RECYCLER.BIN\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Redirection to Local Admin Share
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious output redirection to the local admins share, this technique is often found in malicious scripts or hacktool stagers
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*>*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\\\127.0.0.1\\admin$\\*", "*\\\\localhost\\admin$\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Remote Desktop Tunneling
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects potential use of an SSH utility to establish RDP over a reverse SSH Tunnel. This can be used by attackers to enable routing of network packets that would otherwise not reach their intended destination.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*:3389*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -L *", "* -P *", "* -R *", "* -pw *", "* -ssh *")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Defense Evasion Via Right-to-Left Override
author: Micah Babinski, @micahbabinski, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the presence of the "u202+E" character, which causes a terminal, browser, or operating system to render text in a right-to-left sequence.
This is used as an obfuscation and masquerading techniques.
false_positives: Commandlines that contains scriptures such as arabic or hebrew might make use of this character
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\u202e*", "*[U+202E]*"))

name: SIGMA - Script Interpreter Execution From Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious script execution in temporary folders or folders accessible by environment variables
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("* -ep bypass *", "* -ExecutionPolicy bypass *", "* -w hidden *", "*/e:javascript *", "*/e:Jscript *", "*/e:vbscript *")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "wscript.exe"))) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Temp*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*\\Windows\\Temp*")) or ((CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favorites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Favourites\\*") or (CommandLine:"*:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\Contacts\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Script Execution From Temp Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
description: Detects a suspicious script executions from temporary folder
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Windows\\Temp*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\Temp*", "*%TEMP%*", "*%TMP%*", "*%LocalAppData%\\Temp*"))) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* >*", "*Out-File*", "*ConvertTo-Json*", "*-WindowStyle hidden -Verb runAs*", "*\\Windows\\system32\\config\\systemprofile\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Amazon\\EC2-Windows\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Sensitive File Access Via Volume Shadow Copy Backup
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a command that accesses the VolumeShadowCopy in order to extract sensitive files such as the Security or SAM registry hives or the AD database (ntds.dit)
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\\\\?\\GLOBALROOT\\Device\\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\NTDS.dit*", "*\\SYSTEM*", "*\\SECURITY*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious New Service Creation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects creation of a new service via "sc" command or the powershell "new-service" cmdlet with suspicious binary paths
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\sc.exe" and (CommandLine:"*create*" and CommandLine:"*binPath=*")) or (CommandLine:"*New-Service*" and CommandLine:"*-BinaryPathName*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*powershell*", "*mshta*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*", "*svchost*", "*dllhost*", "*cmd *", "*cmd.exe /c*", "*cmd.exe /k*", "*cmd.exe /r*", "*rundll32*", "*C:\\Users\\Public*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service Binary Directory
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a service binary running in a suspicious directory
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\$Recycle.bin*", "*\\Users\\All Users\\*", "*\\Users\\Default\\*", "*\\Users\\Contacts\\*", "*\\Users\\Searches\\*", "*C:\\Perflogs\\*", "*\\config\\systemprofile\\*", "*\\Windows\\Fonts\\*", "*\\Windows\\IME\\*", "*\\Windows\\addins\\*")) and (ParentImage like~ ("*\\services.exe", "*\\svchost.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Windows Service Tampering
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 , X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of binaries such as 'net', 'sc' or 'powershell' in order to stop, pause, disable or delete critical or important Windows services such as AV, Backup, etc. As seen being used in some ransomware scripts
false_positives: Administrators or tools shutting down the services due to upgrade or removal purposes. If you experience some false positive, please consider adding filters to the parent process launching this command and not removing the entry
query: any where EventID:1 and (((OriginalFileName like~ ("net.exe", "net1.exe", "PowerShell_ISE.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "psservice.exe", "pwsh.dll", "sc.exe", "wmic.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\PowerShell_ISE.EXE", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\PsService.exe", "*\\PsService64.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\sc.exe", "*\\wmic.exe"))) and ((CommandLine like~ ("* delete *", "*.delete()*", "* pause *", "* stop *", "*Stop-Service *", "*Remove-Service *")) or (CommandLine:"*config*" and CommandLine:"*start=disabled*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*143Svc*", "*Acronis VSS Provider*", "*AcronisAgent*", "*AcrSch2Svc*", "*AdobeARMservice*", "*AHS Service*", "*Antivirus*", "*Apache4*", "*ARSM*", "*aswBcc*", "*AteraAgent*", "*Avast Business Console Client Antivirus Service*", "*avast! name: SIGMA - Shadow Copies Creation Using Operating Systems Utilities
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Shadow Copies creation using operating systems utilities, possible credential access
false_positives: Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\vssadmin.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "wmic.exe", "VSSADMIN.EXE"))) and (CommandLine:"*shadow*" and CommandLine:"*create*"))

name: SIGMA - Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
false_positives: Legitimate Administrator deletes Shadow Copies using operating systems utilities for legitimate reason
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\vssadmin.exe", "*\\diskshadow.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "wmic.exe", "VSSADMIN.EXE", "diskshadow.exe"))) and (CommandLine:"*shadow*" and CommandLine:"*delete*")) or ((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*delete*" and CommandLine:"*catalog*" and CommandLine:"*quiet*")) or ((Image:"*\\vssadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"VSSADMIN.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*resize*" and CommandLine:"*shadowstorage*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*unbounded*", "*/MaxSize=*")))))

name: SIGMA - Windows Shell/Scripting Processes Spawning Suspicious Programs
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton
description: Detects suspicious child processes of a Windows shell and scripting processes such as wscript, rundll32, powershell, mshta...etc.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\wmiprvse.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\nslookup.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\mshta.exe"))) and (not (CurrentDirectory:"*\\ccmcache\\*" or (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*\\Program Files\\Amazon\\WorkSpacesConfig\\Scripts\\setup-scheduledtask.ps1*", "*\\Program Files\\Amazon\\WorkSpacesConfig\\Scripts\\set-selfhealing.ps1*", "*\\Program Files\\Amazon\\WorkSpacesConfig\\Scripts\\check-workspacehealth.ps1*", "*\\nessus_*")) or CommandLine:"*\\nessus_*" or (ParentImage:"*\\mshta.exe" and Image:"*\\mshta.exe" and (ParentCommandLine:"*C:\\MEM_Configmgr_*" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\splash.hta*" and ParentCommandLine:"*{1E460BD7-F1C3-4B2E-88BF-4E770A288AF5}*") and (CommandLine:"*C:\\MEM_Configmgr_*" and CommandLine:"*\\SMSSETUP\\BIN\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\autorun.hta*" and CommandLine:"*{1E460BD7-F1C3-4B2E-88BF-4E770A288AF5}*")))))

name: SIGMA - Process Creation Using Sysnative Folder
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects process creation events that use the Sysnative folder (common for CobaltStrike spawns)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*:\\Windows\\Sysnative\\*" or Image:"*:\\Windows\\Sysnative\\*") and (not ((Image like~ ("*C:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework64\\v*", "*C:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\Framework\\v*", "*C:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm\\v*", "*C:\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\FrameworkArm64\\v*")) and Image:"*\\ngen.exe" and CommandLine:"*install*")) and (not (CommandLine:"*\"C:\\Windows\\sysnative\\cmd.exe\"*" and CommandLine:"*\\xampp\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\catalina_start.bat*")))

name: SIGMA - System File Execution Location Anomaly
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a Windows system binary that is usually located in the system folder from an uncommon location.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\atbroker.exe", "*\\audiodg.exe", "*\\bcdedit.exe", "*\\bitsadmin.exe", "*\\certreq.exe", "*\\certutil.exe", "*\\cmstp.exe", "*\\conhost.exe", "*\\consent.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\csrss.exe", "*\\dashost.exe", "*\\defrag.exe", "*\\dfrgui.exe", "*\\dism.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe", "*\\dllhst3g.exe", "*\\dwm.exe", "*\\eventvwr.exe", "*\\logonui.exe", "*\\LsaIso.exe", "*\\lsass.exe", "*\\lsm.exe", "*\\msiexec.exe", "*\\ntoskrnl.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\runonce.exe", "*\\RuntimeBroker.exe", "*\\schtasks.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\sihost.exe", "*\\smartscreen.exe", "*\\smss.exe", "*\\spoolsv.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\taskhost.exe", "*\\taskhostw.exe", "*\\Taskmgr.exe", "*\\userinit.exe", "*\\wininit.exe", "*\\winlogon.exe", "*\\winver.exe", "*\\wlanext.exe", "*\\wscript.exe", "*\\wsl.exe", "*\\wsmprovhost.exe")) and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\$WINDOWS.~BT\\*", "C:\\$WinREAgent\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SoftwareDistribution\\*", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SystemTemp\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "C:\\Windows\\uus\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")) or ((Image like~ ("*C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\*", "*C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7-preview\\*", "*C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.PowerShellPreview*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.PowerShellPreview*")) and Image:"*\\pwsh.exe") or ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\MicrosoftCorporationII.WindowsSubsystemForLinux*", "C:\\Program Files\\WSL\\*")) and Image:"*\\wsl.exe") or (Image:"C:\\Users\\'*" and Image:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\WindowsApps\\*" and Image:"*\\wsl.exe"))) and (not Image:"*\\SystemRoot\\System32\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious SYSTEM User Process Creation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), David ANDRE (additional keywords)
description: Detects a suspicious process creation as SYSTEM user (suspicious program or command line parameter)
false_positives: Scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and ((((IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384")) and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*"))) and ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\forfiles.exe", "*\\hh.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\ping.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (CommandLine like~ ("* -NoP *", "* -W Hidden *", "* -decode *", "* /decode *", "* /urlcache *", "* -urlcache *", "* -e* JAB*", "* -e* SUVYI*", "* -e* SQBFAFgA*", "* -e* aWV4I*", "* -e* IAB*", "* -e* PAA*", "* -e* aQBlAHgA*", "*vssadmin delete shadows*", "*reg SAVE HKLM*", "* -ma *", "*Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*", "*.downloadstring(*", "*.downloadfile(*", "* /ticket:*", "*dpapi::*", "*event::clear*", "*event::drop*", "*id::modify*", "*kerberos::*", "*lsadump::*", "*misc::*", "*privilege::*", "*rpc::*", "*sekurlsa::*", "*sid::*", "*token::*", "*vault::cred*", "*vault::list*", "* p::d *", "*;iex(*", "*MiniDump*", "*net user *")))) and (not ((CommandLine:"*ping*" and CommandLine:"*127.0.0.1*" and CommandLine:"* -n *") or (Image:"*\\PING.EXE" and ParentCommandLine:"*\\DismFoDInstall.cmd*") or ParentImage:"*:\\Packages\\Plugins\\Microsoft.GuestConfiguration.ConfigurationforWindows\\*" or ((ParentImage like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Java\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\Java\\*")) and ParentImage:"*\\bin\\javaws.exe" and (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Java\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\Java\\*")) and Image:"*\\bin\\jp2launcher.exe" and CommandLine:"* -ma *"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious SYSVOL Domain Group Policy Access
author: Markus Neis, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
description: Detects Access to Domain Group Policies stored in SYSVOL
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\SYSVOL\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\policies\\*")

name: SIGMA - Tasks Folder Evasion
author: Sreeman
description:
The Tasks folder in system32 and syswow64 are globally writable paths.
Adversaries can take advantage of this and load or influence any script hosts or ANY .NET Application
in Tasks to load and execute a custom assembly into cscript, wscript, regsvr32, mshta, eventvwr
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*echo *", "*copy *", "*type *", "*file createnew*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* C:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "* C:\\Windows\\SysWow64\\Tasks\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Userinit Child Process
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden (idea)
description: Detects a suspicious child process of userinit
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\userinit.exe" and (not (CommandLine:"*\\netlogon\\*" or (Image:"*\\explorer.exe" or OriginalFileName:"explorer.exe" or CommandLine:"C:\\Windows\\Explorer.EXE") or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - Malicious Windows Script Components File Execution by TAEF Detection
author: Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community
description:
Windows Test Authoring and Execution Framework (TAEF) framework allows you to run automation by executing tests files written on different languages (C, C#, Microsoft COM Scripting interfaces
Adversaries may execute malicious code (such as WSC file with VBScript, dll and so on) directly by running te.exe
false_positives: It's not an uncommon to use te.exe directly to execute legal TAEF tests
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\te.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\te.exe" or OriginalFileName:"\\te.exe")

name: SIGMA - Malicious PE Execution by Microsoft Visual Studio Debugger
author: Agro (@agro_sev), Ensar Şamil (@sblmsrsn), oscd.community
description:
There is an option for a MS VS Just-In-Time Debugger "vsjitdebugger.exe" to launch specified executable and attach a debugger.
This option may be used adversaries to execute malicious code by signed verified binary.
The debugger is installed alongside with Microsoft Visual Studio package.
false_positives: The process spawned by vsjitdebugger.exe is uncommon.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\vsjitdebugger.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*\\vsimmersiveactivatehelper*.exe", "*\\devenv.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Velociraptor Child Process
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the suspicious use of the Velociraptor DFIR tool to execute other tools or download additional payloads, as seen in a campaign where it was abused for remote access and to stage further attacks.
false_positives: Legitimate administrators or incident responders might use Velociraptor to execute scripts or tools. However, the combination of Velociraptor spawning these specific processes with these command lines is suspicious. Tuning may be required to exclude known administrative actions or specific scripts.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\Velociraptor.exe" and ((CommandLine:"*code.exe*" and CommandLine:"*tunnel*" and CommandLine:"*--accept-server-license-terms*") or (CommandLine:"*msiexec*" and CommandLine:"*/i*" and CommandLine:"*http*") or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\powershell_ise.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-WebRequest *", "*IWR *", "*.DownloadFile*", "*.DownloadString*")))))

name: SIGMA - Weak or Abused Passwords In CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects weak passwords or often abused passwords (seen used by threat actors) via the CLI.
An example would be a threat actor creating a new user via the net command and providing the password inline
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the passwords by users via commandline (should be discouraged)
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*123456789*", "*123123qwE*", "*Asd123.aaaa*", "*Decryptme*", "*P@ssw0rd!*", "*Pass8080*", "*password123*", "*test@202*"))

name: SIGMA - Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets
author: James Pemberton / @4A616D6573, Endgame, JHasenbusch, oscd.community, Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via CommandLine
false_positives: Use of Get-Command and Get-Help modules to reference Invoke-WebRequest and Start-BitsTransfer.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine like~ ("*[System.Net.WebRequest]::create*", "*curl *", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "* irm *", "*iwr *", "*Resume-BitsTransfer*", "*Start-BitsTransfer*", "*wget *", "*WinHttp.WinHttpRequest*"))

name: SIGMA - WhoAmI as Parameter
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious process command line that uses whoami as first parameter (as e.g. used by EfsPotato)
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"*.exe whoami*"

name: SIGMA - Execution via WorkFolders.exe
author: Maxime Thiebaut (@0xThiebaut)
description: Detects using WorkFolders.exe to execute an arbitrary control.exe
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the uncommon Windows Work Folders feature.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\control.exe" and ParentImage:"*\\WorkFolders.exe") and (not Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\control.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Suspect Svchost Activity
author: David Burkett, @signalblur
description: It is extremely abnormal for svchost.exe to spawn without any CLI arguments and is normally observed when a malicious process spawns the process and injects code into the process memory space.
false_positives: Rpcnet.exe / rpcnetp.exe which is a lojack style software. https://www.blackhat.com/docs/us-14/materials/us-14-Kamlyuk-Kamluk-Computrace-Backdoor-Revisited.pdf
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*svchost.exe" and Image:"*\\svchost.exe") and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\rpcnet.exe", "*\\rpcnetp.exe")) or (?CommandLine == null))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Masquerading As SvcHost.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description:
Detects a suspicious process that is masquerading as the legitimate "svchost.exe" by naming its binary "svchost.exe" and executing from an uncommon location.
Adversaries often disguise their malicious binaries by naming them after legitimate system processes like "svchost.exe" to evade detection.

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\svchost.exe" and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\svchost.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"svchost.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Terminal Service Process Spawn
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a process spawned by the terminal service server process (this could be an indicator for an exploitation of CVE-2019-0708)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentCommandLine:"*\\svchost.exe*" and ParentCommandLine:"*termsvcs*") and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\rdpclip.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\csrss.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\wininit.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\winlogon.exe")) or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Svchost Parent Process
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an uncommon svchost parent process

subtechnique.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\svchost.exe" and (not ((ParentImage like~ ("*\\Mrt.exe", "*\\MsMpEng.exe", "*\\ngen.exe", "*\\rpcnet.exe", "*\\services.exe", "*\\TiWorker.exe")) or (?ParentImage == null) or (ParentImage like~ ("-", "")))))

name: SIGMA - Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of the "Accesschk" utility, an access and privilege audit tool developed by SysInternal and often being abused by attacker to verify process privileges
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Product:"*AccessChk" or Description:"*Reports effective permissions*" or (Image like~ ("*\\accesschk.exe", "*\\accesschk64.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"accesschk.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*uwcqv *", "*kwsu *", "*qwsu *", "*uwdqs *")))

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\ADExp.exe", "*\\ADExplorer.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64a.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"AdExp" or Description:"Active Directory Editor" or Product:"Sysinternals ADExplorer") and CommandLine:"*snapshot*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database to a suspicious directory. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\ADExp.exe", "*\\ADExplorer.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64a.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"AdExp" or Description:"Active Directory Editor" or Product:"Sysinternals ADExplorer") and CommandLine:"*snapshot*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools
author: Markus Neis
description: Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools
false_positives: Programs that use the same command line flag
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine like~ ("* -accepteula*", "* /accepteula*", "* –accepteula*", "* —accepteula*", "* ―accepteula*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Memory Dumping Activity Via LiveKD
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of LiveKD based on PE metadata or image name
false_positives: Administration and debugging activity (must be investigated)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\livekd.exe", "*\\livekd64.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"livekd.exe")

name: SIGMA - Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of LiveKD with the "-m" flag to potentially dump the kernel memory
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\livekd.exe", "*\\livekd64.exe")) or OriginalFileName:"livekd.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("* -m*", "* /m*", "* –m*", "* —m*", "* ―m*"))

name: SIGMA - Procdump Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the SysInternals Procdump utility
false_positives: Legitimate use of procdump by a developer or administrator
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*\\procdump.exe", "*\\procdump64.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uses of the SysInternals ProcDump utility in which ProcDump or its output get renamed, or a dump file is moved or copied to a different name
false_positives: False positives are expected in cases in which ProcDump just gets copied to a different directory without any renaming
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*copy procdump*", "*move procdump*")) or ((CommandLine:"*copy *" and CommandLine:"*.dmp *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*2.dmp*", "*lsass*", "*out.dmp*"))) or (CommandLine like~ ("*copy lsass.exe_*", "*move lsass.exe_*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump.
This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers.
LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory.
Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
false_positives: Unlikely, because no one should dump an lsass process memory
Another tool that uses command line flags similar to ProcDump
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* -ma *", "* /ma *", "* –ma *", "* —ma *", "* ―ma *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -mm *", "* /mm *", "* –mm *", "* —mm *", "* ―mm *") or CommandLine like~ ("* -mp *", "* /mp *", "* –mp *", "* —mp *", "* ―mp *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* ls*", "* keyiso*", "* samss*")))

name: SIGMA - Psexec Execution
author: omkar72
description: Detects user accept agreement execution in psexec commandline
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\psexec.exe" or OriginalFileName:"psexec.c")

name: SIGMA - PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious commandline flags used by PsExec and PAExec to escalate a command line to LOCAL_SYSTEM rights
false_positives: Admins that use PsExec or PAExec to escalate to the SYSTEM account for maintenance purposes (rare)
Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* -s cmd*", "* /s cmd*", "* –s cmd*", "* —s cmd*", "* ―s cmd*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s -i cmd*", "* -s /i cmd*", "* -s –i cmd*", "* -s —i pwsh*", "* /s pwsh*", "* –s pwsh*", "* —s pwsh*", "* ―s pwsh*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s -i pwsh*", "* -s /i pwsh*", "* -s –i pwsh*", "* -s —i pwsh*", "* -s ―i pwsh*", "* /s -i pwsh*", "* /s /i powershell*", "* /s powershell*", "* –s powershell*", "* —s powershell*", "* ―s powershell*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s -i powershell*", "* -s /i powershell*", "* -s –i powershell*", "* -s —i powershell*", "* -s ―i powershell*", "* /s -i powershell*", "* /s /i powershell*", "* /s –i powershell*", "* /s —i powershell*", "* /s ―i powershell*", "* –s -i powershell*", "* –s /i powershell*", "* –s –i powershell*", "* –s —i powershell*", "* –s ―i powershell*", "* —s -i powershell*", "* —s /i powershell*", "* —s –i powershell*", "* —s —i powershell*", "* —s ―i powershell*", "* ―s -i powershell*", "* ―s /i powershell*", "* ―s –i powershell*", "* ―s —i powershell*", "* ―s ―i powershell*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -i -s powershell*", "* -i /s powershell*", "* -i –s powershell*", "* -i —s powershell*", "* -i ―s powershell*", "* /i -s powershell*", "* /i /s powershell*", "* /i –s powershell*", "* /i —s powershell*", "* /i ―s powershell*", "* –i -s powershell*", "* –i /s powershell*", "* –i –s powershell*", "* –i —s powershell*", "* –i ―s powershell*", "* —i -s powershell*", "* —i /s powershell*", "* —i –s powershell*", "* —i —s powershell*", "* —i ―s powershell*", "* ―i -s powershell*", "* ―i /s powershell*", "* ―i –s powershell*", "* ―i —s powershell*", "* ―i ―s powershell*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*psexec*", "*paexec*", "*accepteula*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential PsExec Remote Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential psexec command that initiate execution on a remote systems via common commandline flags used by the utility
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"*accepteula*" and CommandLine:"* -u *" and CommandLine:"* -p *" and CommandLine:"* \\\\*") and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*\\\\localhost*", "*\\\\127.*"))))

name: SIGMA - PsExec Service Execution
author: Thomas Patzke, Romaissa Adjailia, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects launch of the PSEXESVC service, which means that this system was the target of a psexec remote execution
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"C:\\Windows\\PSEXESVC.exe" or OriginalFileName:"psexesvc.exe")

name: SIGMA - PsExec Service Child Process Execution as LOCAL SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious launch of the PSEXESVC service on this system and a sub process run as LOCAL_SYSTEM (-s), which means that someone remotely started a command on this system running it with highest privileges and not only the privileges of the login user account (e.g. the administrator account)
false_positives: Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\PSEXESVC.exe" and (User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Use of PsLogList
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the PsLogList utility to dump event log in order to extract admin accounts and perform account discovery or delete events logs
false_positives: Another tool that uses the command line switches of PsLogList
Legitimate use of PsLogList by an administrator
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"psloglist.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\psloglist.exe", "*\\psloglist64.exe"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("* security*", "* application*", "* system*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -d*", "* /d*", "* –d*", "* —d*", "* ―d*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -x*", "* /x*", "* –x*", "* —x*", "* ―x*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s*", "* /s*", "* –s*", "* —s*", "* ―s*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -c*", "* /c*", "* –c*", "* —c*", "* ―c*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -g*", "* /g*", "* –g*", "* —g*", "* ―g*")))

name: SIGMA - Sysinternals PsService Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Sysinternals PsService which can be abused for service reconnaissance and tampering
false_positives: Legitimate use of PsService by an administrator
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"psservice.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\PsService.exe", "*\\PsService64.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Sysinternals PsSuspend Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of Sysinternals PsSuspend which can be abused to suspend critical processes
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"pssuspend.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\pssuspend.exe", "*\\pssuspend64.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Sysinternals PsSuspend Suspicious Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious execution of Sysinternals PsSuspend, where the utility is used to suspend critical processes such as AV or EDR to bypass defenses
query: any where EventID:1 and ((OriginalFileName:"pssuspend.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\pssuspend.exe", "*\\pssuspend64.exe"))) and CommandLine:"*msmpeng.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Potential File Overwrite Via Sysinternals SDelete
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of SDelete to erase a file not the free space
query: any where EventID:1 and (OriginalFileName:"sdelete.exe" and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* -h*", "* -c*", "* -z*", "* /\?*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Privilege Escalation To LOCAL SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects unknown program using commandline flags usually used by tools such as PsExec and PAExec to start programs with SYSTEM Privileges
false_positives: Weird admins that rename their tools
Software companies that bundle PsExec/PAExec with their software and rename it, so that it is less embarrassing
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("* -s cmd*", "* /s cmd*", "* –s cmd*", "* —s cmd*", "* ―s cmd*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s -i cmd*", "* -s /i cmd*", "* -s –i cmd*", "* -s —i cmd*", "* -s ―i cmd*", "* /s -i cmd*", "* /s /i cmd*", "* /s –i cmd*", "* /s —i cmd*", "* /s ―i cmd*", "* –s -i cmd*", "* –s /i cmd*", "* –s –i cmd*", "* –s —i cmd*", "* –s ―i cmd*", "* —s -i cmd*", powershell*", "* /s powershell*", "* –s powershell*", "* —s powershell*", "* ―s powershell*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -s -i powershell*", "* -s /i powershell*", "* -s –i powershell*", "* -s —i powershell*", "* -s ―i powershell*", "* /s -i powershell*", "* /s /i powershell*", "* /s –i powershell*", "* /s —i powershell*", "* /s ―i powershell*", "* –s -i powershell*", "* –s /i powershell*", "* –s –i powershell*", "* –s —i powershell*", "* –s ―i powershell*", "* —s -i powershell*", "* —s /i powershell*", "* —s –i powershell*", "* —s —i powershell*", "* —s ―i powershell*", "* ―s -i powershell*", "* ―s /i powershell*", "* ―s –i powershell*", "* ―s —i powershell*", "* ―s ―i powershell*") or CommandLine like~ ("* -i -s powershell*", "* -i /s powershell*", "* -i –s powershell*", "* -i —s powershell*", "* -i ―s powershell*", "* /i -s powershell*", "* /i /s powershell*", "* /i –s powershell*", "* /i —s powershell*", "* /i ―s powershell*", "* –i -s powershell*", "* –i /s ―i -s powershell*", "* ―i /s powershell*", "* ―i –s powershell*", "* ―i —s powershell*", "* ―i ―s powershell*")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*paexec*", "*PsExec*", "*accepteula*"))))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Configuration Update
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects updates to Sysmon's configuration. Attackers might update or replace the Sysmon configuration with a bare bone one to avoid monitoring without shutting down the service completely
false_positives: Legitimate administrators might use this command to update Sysmon configuration.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\Sysmon64.exe", "*\\Sysmon.exe")) or Description:"System activity monitor") and CommandLine like~ ("*-c*", "*/c*", "*–c*", "*—c*", "*―c*"))

name: SIGMA - Uninstall Sysinternals Sysmon
author: frack113
description: Detects the removal of Sysmon, which could be a potential attempt at defense evasion
false_positives: Legitimate administrators might use this command to remove Sysmon for debugging purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\Sysmon64.exe", "*\\Sysmon.exe")) or Description:"System activity monitor") and CommandLine like~ ("*-u*", "*/u*", "*–u*", "*—u*", "*―u*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Binary Impersonating Sysinternals Tools
author: frack113, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects binaries that use the same name as legitimate sysinternals tools to evade detection.
This rule looks for the execution of binaries that are named similarly to Sysinternals tools.
Adversary may rename their malicious tools as legitimate Sysinternals tools to evade detection.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image like~ ("*\\accesschk.exe", "*\\accesschk64.exe", "*\\AccessEnum.exe", "*\\ADExplorer.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64.exe", "*\\ADInsight.exe", "*\\ADInsight64.exe", "*\\adrestore.exe", "*\\adrestore64.exe", "*\\Autologon.exe", "*\\Autologon64.exe", "*\\Autoruns.exe", "*\\Autoruns64.exe", "*\\autorunsc.exe", "*\\autorunsc64.exe", "*\\Bginfo.exe", "*\\Bginfo64.exe", "*\\Cacheset.exe", "*\\Cacheset64.exe", "*\\Clockres.exe", "*\\Clockres64.exe", "*\\Contig.exe", "*\\Contig64.exe", "*\\Coreinfo.exe", "*\\Coreinfo64.exe", "*\\CPUSTRES.EXE", "*\\CPUSTRES64.EXE", "*\\ctrl2cap.exe", "*\\Dbgview.exe", "*\\dbgview64.exe", "*\\Desktops.exe", "*\\Desktops64.exe", "*\\disk2vhd.exe", "*\\disk2vhd64.exe", "*\\diskext.exe", "*\\diskext64.exe", "*\\Diskmon.exe", "*\\Diskmon64.exe", "*\\DiskView.exe", "*\\DiskView64.exe", "*\\du.exe", "*\\du64.exe", "*\\efsdump.exe", "*\\FindLinks.exe", "*\\FindLinks64.exe", "*\\handle.exe", "*\\handle64.exe", "*\\hex2dec.exe", "*\\hex2dec64.exe", "*\\junction.exe", "*\\junction64.exe", "*\\ldmdump.exe", "*\\listdlls.exe", "*\\listdlls64.exe", "*\\livekd.exe", "*\\livekd64.exe", "*\\loadOrd.exe", "*\\loadOrd64.exe", "*\\loadOrdC.exe", "*\\loadOrdC64.exe", "*\\logonsessions.exe", "*\\logonsessions64.exe", "*\\movefile.exe", "*\\movefile64.exe", "*\\notmyfault.exe", "*\\notmyfault64.exe", "*\\notmyfaultc.exe", "*\\notmyfaultc64.exe", "*\\ntfsinfo.exe", "*\\ntfsinfo64.exe", "*\\pendmoves.exe", "*\\pendmoves64.exe", "*\\pipelist.exe", "*\\pipelist64.exe", "*\\portmon.exe", "*\\procdump.exe", "*\\procdump64.exe", "*\\procexp.exe", "*\\procexp64.exe", "*\\Procmon.exe", "*\\Procmon64.exe", "*\\psExec.exe", "*\\psExec64.exe", "*\\psfile.exe", "*\\psfile64.exe", "*\\psGetsid.exe", "*\\psGetsid64.exe", "*\\psInfo.exe", "*\\psInfo64.exe", "*\\pskill.exe", "*\\pskill64.exe", "*\\pslist.exe", "*\\pslist64.exe", "*\\psLoggedon.exe", "*\\psLoggedon64.exe", "*\\psloglist.exe", "*\\psloglist64.exe", "*\\pspasswd.exe", "*\\pspasswd64.exe", "*\\psping.exe", "*\\psping64.exe", "*\\psService.exe", "*\\psService64.exe", "*\\psshutdown.exe", "*\\psshutdown64.exe", "*\\pssuspend.exe", "*\\pssuspend64.exe", "*\\RAMMap.exe", "*\\RAMMap64.exe", "*\\RDCMan.exe", "*\\RegDelNull.exe", "*\\RegDelNull64.exe", "*\\regjump.exe", "*\\ru.exe", "*\\ru64.exe", "*\\sdelete.exe", "*\\sdelete64.exe", "*\\ShareEnum.exe", "*\\ShareEnum64.exe", "*\\shellRunas.exe", "*\\sigcheck.exe", "*\\sigcheck64.exe", "*\\streams.exe", "*\\streams64.exe", "*\\strings.exe", "*\\strings64.exe", "*\\sync.exe", "*\\sync64.exe", "*\\Sysmon.exe", "*\\Sysmon64.exe", "*\\tcpvcon.exe", "*\\tcpvcon64.exe", "*\\tcpview.exe", "*\\tcpview64.exe", "*\\Testlimit.exe", "*\\Testlimit64.exe", "*\\vmmap.exe", "*\\vmmap64.exe", "*\\Volumeid.exe", "*\\Volumeid64.exe", "*\\whois.exe", "*\\whois64.exe", "*\\Winobj.exe", "*\\Winobj64.exe", "*\\ZoomIt.exe", "*\\ZoomIt64.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*\\accesschk64a.exe", "*\\ADExplorer64a.exe", "*\\ADInsight64a.exe", "*\\adrestore64a.exe", "*\\Autologon64a.exe", "*\\Autoruns64a.exe", "*\\autorunsc64a.exe", "*\\Clockres64a.exe", "*\\Contig64a.exe", "*\\Coreinfo64a.exe", "*\\Dbgview64a.exe", "*\\disk2vhd64a.exe", "*\\diskext64a.exe", "*\\DiskView64a.exe", "*\\du64a.exe", "*\\FindLinks64a.exe", "*\\handle64a.exe", "*\\hex2dec64a.exe", "*\\junction64a.exe", "*\\LoadOrd64a.exe", "*\\LoadOrdC64a.exe", "*\\logonsessions64a.exe", "*\\movefile64a.exe", "*\\notmyfault64a.exe", "*\\notmyfaultc64a.exe", "*\\pendmoves64a.exe", "*\\pipelist64a.exe", "*\\procdump64a.exe", "*\\procexp64a.exe", "*\\Procmon64a.exe", "*\\PsExec64a.exe", "*\\psfile64a.exe", "*\\PsGetsid64a.exe", "*\\PsInfo64a.exe", "*\\pskill64a.exe", "*\\psloglist64a.exe", "*\\pspasswd64a.exe", "*\\psping64a.exe", "*\\PsService64a.exe", "*\\pssuspend64a.exe", "*\\RAMMap64a.exe", "*\\RegDelNull64a.exe", "*\\ru64a.exe", "*\\sdelete64a.exe", "*\\sigcheck64a.exe", "*\\streams64a.exe", "*\\strings64a.exe", "*\\sync64a.exe", "*\\Sysmon64a.exe", "*\\tcpvcon64a.exe", "*\\tcpview64a.exe", "*\\vmmap64a.exe", "*\\whois64a.exe", "*\\Winobj64a.exe", "*\\ZoomIt64a.exe"))) and (not (((Company like~ ("Sysinternals - www.sysinternals.com", "Sysinternals")) or Product:"Sysinternals*") or ((?Company == null) or (?Product == null)))))

name: SIGMA - Sysprep on AppData Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious sysprep process start with AppData folder as target (as used by Trojan Syndicasec in Thrip report by Symantec)
false_positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\sysprep.exe" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Execution of Systeminfo
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of the "systeminfo" command to retrieve information
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\systeminfo.exe" or OriginalFileName:"sysinfo.exe")

name: SIGMA - Potential Signing Bypass Via Windows Developer Features
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when a user enable developer features such as "Developer Mode" or "Application Sideloading". Which allows the user to install untrusted packages.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe" or OriginalFileName:"SystemSettingsAdminFlows.EXE") and CommandLine:"*TurnOnDeveloperFeatures*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*DeveloperUnlock*", "*EnableSideloading*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Recursive Takeown
author: frack113
description: Adversaries can interact with the DACLs using built-in Windows commands takeown which can grant adversaries higher permissions on specific files and folders
false_positives: Scripts created by developers and admins

subtechnique.name: Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\takeown.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/f *" and CommandLine:"*/r*"))

name: SIGMA - Tap Installer Execution
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Ian Davis, oscd.community
description: Well-known TAP software installation. Possible preparation for data exfiltration using tunneling techniques
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\tapinstall.exe" and (not ((Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\SecureLine VPN\\*", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\SecureLine VPN\\*")) or Image:"*:\\Program Files\\OpenVPN Connect\\drivers\\tap\\*" or Image:"*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Proton Technologies\\ProtonVPNTap\\installer\\*")))

name: SIGMA - Compressed File Creation Via Tar.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), AdmU3
description:
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to create a compressed file.
Adversaries may abuse various utilities to compress or encrypt data before exfiltration.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tar.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bsdtar") and (CommandLine like~ ("*-c*", "*-r*", "*-u*")))

name: SIGMA - Compressed File Extraction Via Tar.EXE
author: AdmU3
description:
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to extract compressed file.
Adversaries may abuse various utilities in order to decompress data to avoid detection.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tar.exe" or OriginalFileName:"bsdtar") and CommandLine:"*-x*")

name: SIGMA - Taskkill Symantec Endpoint Protection
author: Ilya Krestinichev, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects one of the possible scenarios for disabling Symantec Endpoint Protection.
Symantec Endpoint Protection antivirus software services incorrectly implement the protected service mechanism.
As a result, the NT AUTHORITY/SYSTEM user can execute the taskkill /im command several times ccSvcHst.exe /f, thereby killing the process belonging to the service, and thus shutting down the service.
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*taskkill*" and CommandLine:"* /F *" and CommandLine:"* /IM *" and CommandLine:"*ccSvcHst.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Loaded Module Enumeration Via Tasklist.EXE
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description:
Detects the enumeration of a specific DLL or EXE being used by a binary via "tasklist.exe".
This is often used by attackers in order to find the specific process identifier (PID) that is using the DLL in question.
In order to dump the process memory or perform other nefarious actions.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tasklist.exe" or OriginalFileName:"tasklist.exe") and CommandLine like~ ("*-m*", "*/m*", "*–m*", "*—m*", "*―m*") and CommandLine:"*rdpcorets.dll*")

name: SIGMA - Taskmgr as LOCAL_SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of taskmgr.exe process in context of LOCAL_SYSTEM
query: any where EventID:1 and ((User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and Image:"*\\taskmgr.exe")

name: SIGMA - New Process Created Via Taskmgr.EXE
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a process via the Windows task manager. This might be an attempt to bypass UAC
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\taskmgr.exe" and (not (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\mmc.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\resmon.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Taskmgr.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Command Targeting Teams Sensitive Files
author: @SerkinValery
description:
Detects a commandline containing references to the Microsoft Teams database or cookies files from a process other than Teams.
The database might contain authentication tokens and other sensitive information about the logged in accounts.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Cookies*", "*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Local Storage\\leveldb*")) and (not Image:"*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\current\\Teams.exe"))

name: SIGMA - New Virtual Smart Card Created Via TpmVscMgr.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "Tpmvscmgr.exe" to create a new virtual smart card.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tpmvscmgr.exe" and OriginalFileName:"TpmVscMgr.exe") and CommandLine:"*create*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious TSCON Start as SYSTEM
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a tscon.exe start as LOCAL SYSTEM
query: any where EventID:1 and ((User like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")) and Image:"*\\tscon.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious RDP Redirect Using TSCON
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious RDP session redirect using tscon.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and CommandLine:"* /dest:rdp-tcp#*"

name: SIGMA - Potential RDP Session Hijacking Activity
author: @juju4
description: Detects potential RDP Session Hijacking activity on Windows systems
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\tscon.exe" or OriginalFileName:"tscon.exe") and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("System", "S-1-16-16384")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using ChangePK and SLUI
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an UAC bypass that uses changepk.exe and slui.exe (UACMe 61)
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\changepk.exe" and ParentImage:"*\\slui.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using Disk Cleanup
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using scheduled tasks and variable expansion of cleanmgr.exe (UACMe 34)
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\"\\system32\\cleanmgr.exe /autoclean /d C:" and ParentCommandLine:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p -s Schedule" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC via CMSTP
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
description: Detect commandline usage of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer (cmstp.exe) to install specially formatted local .INF files
false_positives: Legitimate use of cmstp.exe utility by legitimate user
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\cmstp.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CMSTP.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/s*", "*-s*", "*/au*", "*-au*", "*/ni*", "*-ni*")))

name: SIGMA - CMSTP UAC Bypass via COM Object Access
author: Nik Seetharaman, Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects UAC Bypass Attempt Using Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer Autoelevate-capable COM Objects (e.g. UACMe ID of 41, 43, 58 or 65)
false_positives: Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\DllHost.exe" and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("* /Processid:{3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7}*", "* /Processid:{3E000D72-A845-4CD9-BD83-80C07C3B881F}*", "* /Processid:{BD54C901-076B-434E-B6C7-17C531F4AB41}*", "* /Processid:{D2E7041B-2927-42FB-8E9F-7CE93B6DC937}*", "* /Processid:{E9495B87-D950-4AB5-87A5-FF6D70BF3E90}*")) and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Tools Using ComputerDefaults
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects tools such as UACMe used to bypass UAC with computerdefaults.exe (UACMe 59)
query: any where EventID:1 and (((IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")) and Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\ComputerDefaults.exe") and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32*", "*:\\Program Files*"))))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using Consent and Comctl32 - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using consent.exe and comctl32.dll (UACMe 22)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\consent.exe" and Image:"*\\werfault.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using DismHost
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using DismHost DLL hijacking (UACMe 63)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*C:\\Users\\*" and ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" and ParentImage:"*\\DismHost.exe*") and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using Event Viewer RecentViews
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Event Viewer RecentViews
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*\\Event Viewer\\RecentViews*", "*\\EventV~1\\RecentViews*")) and CommandLine:"*>*")

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC via Fodhelper.exe
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community
description: Identifies use of Fodhelper.exe to bypass User Account Control. Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.
false_positives: Legitimate use of fodhelper.exe utility by legitimate user
query: any where EventID:1 and ParentImage:"*\\fodhelper.exe"

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijack
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects attempts to bypass User Account Control (UAC) by hijacking the Microsoft Management Console (MMC) Windows Firewall snap-in
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\mmc.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*WF.msc*") and (not Image:"*\\WerFault.exe"))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass via ICMLuaUtil
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using ICMLuaUtil Elevated COM interface
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\dllhost.exe" and (ParentCommandLine like~ ("*/Processid:{3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7}*", "*/Processid:{D2E7041B-2927-42FB-8E9F-7CE93B6DC937}*"))) and (not (Image:"*\\WerFault.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WerFault.exe")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using IDiagnostic Profile
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the "IDiagnosticProfileUAC" UAC bypass technique
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\DllHost.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"* /Processid:{12C21EA7-2EB8-4B55-9249-AC243DA8C666}*" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using IEInstal - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using IEInstal.exe (UACMe 64)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")) and ParentImage:"*\\ieinstal.exe" and Image:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" and Image:"*consent.exe")

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using MSConfig Token Modification - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a msconfig GUI hack (UACMe 55)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")) and ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\pkgmgr.exe" and CommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\msconfig.exe\" -5")

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using NTFS Reparse Point - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using NTFS reparse point and wusa.exe DLL hijacking (UACMe 36)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\wusa.exe\"  /quiet C:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\update.msu" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288"))) or (ParentCommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\dism.exe\" /online /quiet /norestart /add-package /packagepath:\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\pe386\" /ignorecheck" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System")) and (CommandLine:"*C:\\Users\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\dismhost.exe {*") and Image:"*\\DismHost.exe"))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using PkgMgr and DISM
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using pkgmgr.exe and dism.exe (UACMe 23)
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\pkgmgr.exe" and Image:"*\\dism.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - Potential UAC Bypass Via Sdclt.EXE
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: A General detection for sdclt being spawned as an elevated process. This could be an indicator of sdclt being used for bypass UAC techniques.
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*sdclt.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - TrustedPath UAC Bypass Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects indicators of a UAC bypass method by mocking directories
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image like~ ("*C:\\Windows \\System32\\*", "*C:\\Windows \\SysWOW64\\*"))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Abusing Winsat Path Parsing - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a path parsing issue in winsat.exe (UACMe 52)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")) and ParentImage:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\system32\\winsat.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*C:\\Windows \\system32\\winsat.exe*")

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass Using Windows Media Player - Process
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Windows Media Player osksupport.dll (UACMe 32)
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Windows Media Player\\osk.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"\"C:\\Windows\\system32\\mmc.exe\" \"C:\\Windows\\system32\\eventvwr.msc\" /s")) and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - Bypass UAC via WSReset.exe
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community, Florian Roth
description: Detects use of WSReset.exe to bypass User Account Control (UAC). Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\wsreset.exe" and (not (Image:"*\\conhost.exe" or OriginalFileName:"CONHOST.EXE")))

name: SIGMA - UAC Bypass WSReset
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass via WSReset usable by default sysmon-config
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\wsreset.exe" and (IntegrityLevel like~ ("High", "System", "S-1-16-16384", "S-1-16-12288")))

name: SIGMA - Use of UltraVNC Remote Access Software
author: frack113
description: An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software,to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks
query: any where EventID:1 and (Description:"VNCViewer" or Product:"UltraVNC VNCViewer" or Company:"UltraVNC" or OriginalFileName:"VNCViewer.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious UltraVNC Execution
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects suspicious UltraVNC command line flag combination that indicate a auto reconnect upon execution, e.g. startup (as seen being used by Gamaredon threat group)
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*-autoreconnect *" and CommandLine:"*-connect *" and CommandLine:"*-id:*")

name: SIGMA - Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by uninstalling Crowdstrike Falcon
false_positives: Administrator might leverage the same command line for debugging or other purposes. However this action must be always investigated
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*\\WindowsSensor.exe*" and CommandLine:"* /uninstall*" and CommandLine:"* /quiet*")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Userinit Child Process
author: Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure), Tim Shelton
description: Detects uncommon "userinit.exe" child processes, which could be a sign of uncommon shells or login scripts used for persistence.
false_positives: Legitimate logon scripts or custom shells may trigger false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\userinit.exe" and (not Image:"*:\\WINDOWS\\explorer.exe") and (not ((CommandLine like~ ("*netlogon.bat*", "*UsrLogon.cmd*")) or CommandLine:"PowerShell.exe" or (Image like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\proquota.exe", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\proquota.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Citrix\\HDX\\bin\\cmstart.exe", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Citrix\\HDX\\bin\\icast.exe", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Citrix\\System32\\icast.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Citrix\\HDX\\bin\\cmstart.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Citrix\\HDX\\bin\\icast.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Citrix\\System32\\icast.exe")) or (?Image == null))))

name: SIGMA - Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
author: frack113
description: List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\VaultCmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"VAULTCMD.EXE") and CommandLine:"*/listcreds:*")

name: SIGMA - Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object
author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
description: Detects when verclsid.exe is used to run COM object via GUID
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\verclsid.exe" or OriginalFileName:"verclsid.exe") and (CommandLine:"*/S*" and CommandLine:"*/C*")) and (not (ParentImage:"*C:\\Windows\\System32\\RuntimeBroker.exe" and (CommandLine:"*verclsid.exe\" /S /C {*" and CommandLine:"*} /I {*"))))

name: SIGMA - Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs
author: Janantha Marasinghe
description: Adversaries can carry out malicious operations using a virtual instance to avoid detection. This rule is built to detect the registration of the Virtualbox driver or start of a Virtualbox VM.
false_positives: This may have false positives on hosts where Virtualbox is legitimately being used for operations
query: any where EventID:1 and ((CommandLine like~ ("*VBoxRT.dll,RTR3Init*", "*VBoxC.dll*", "*VBoxDrv.sys*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*startvm*", "*controlvm*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters
author: Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community
description:
Detect VBoxDrvInst.exe run with parameters allowing processing INF file.
This allows to create values in the registry and install drivers.
For example one could use this technique to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys
false_positives: Legitimate use of VBoxDrvInst.exe utility by VirtualBox Guest Additions installation process
query: any where EventID:1 and (Image:"*\\VBoxDrvInst.exe" and (CommandLine:"*driver*" and CommandLine:"*executeinf*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script to run for a specific VM state
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"toolbox-cmd.exe") and (CommandLine:"* script *" and CommandLine:"* set *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script that's located in a potentially suspicious location to run for a specific VM state
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" or OriginalFileName:"toolbox-cmd.exe") and (CommandLine:"* script *" and CommandLine:"* set *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp*")))

name: SIGMA - VMToolsd Suspicious Child Process
author: bohops, Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects suspicious child process creations of VMware Tools process which may indicate persistence setup
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\vmtoolsd.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or (OriginalFileName like~ ("Cmd.Exe", "cscript.exe", "MSHTA.EXE", "PowerShell.EXE", "pwsh.dll", "REGSVR32.EXE", "RUNDLL32.EXE", "wscript.exe")))) and (not ((Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\VMware\\VMware Tools\\poweron-vm-default.bat*", "*\\VMware\\VMware Tools\\poweroff-vm-default.bat*", "*\\VMware\\VMware Tools\\resume-vm-default.bat*", "*\\VMware\\VMware Tools\\suspend-vm-default.bat*"))) or (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine=="") or (Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (?CommandLine == null)))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes spawning from a VsCode "code.exe" process. This could indicate an attempt of persistence via VsCode tasks or terminal profiles.
false_positives: In development environment where VsCode is used heavily. False positives may occur when developers use task to compile or execute different types of code. Remove or add processes accordingly
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\code.exe" and ((Image like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) or ((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\cmd.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*Invoke-Expressions*", "*IEX*", "*Invoke-Command*", "*ICM*", "*DownloadString*", "*rundll32*", "*regsvr32*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*"))) or (Image like~ ("*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), citron_ninja
description: Detects Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel
false_positives: Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel
query: any where EventID:1 and (((?OriginalFileName == null) and CommandLine:"*.exe tunnel") or (CommandLine:"*.exe tunnel*" and CommandLine:"*--accept-server-license-terms*") or (ParentCommandLine:"* tunnel" and Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/d /c *" and CommandLine:"*\\servers\\Stable-*" and CommandLine:"*code-server.cmd*")))

name: SIGMA - Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a shell (powershell, bash, wsl...) via Visual Studio Code tunnel. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel and execute arbitrary commands on the system.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel and running code from there
query: any where EventID:1 and ((ParentImage:"*\\servers\\Stable-*" and ParentImage:"*\\server\\node.exe" and ParentCommandLine:"*.vscode-server*") and (((Image like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe")) and CommandLine:"*\\terminal\\browser\\media\\shellIntegration.ps1*") or (Image like~ ("*\\wsl.exe", "*\\bash.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Renamed Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects renamed Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel
query: any where EventID:1 and (((((?OriginalFileName == null) and CommandLine:"*.exe tunnel") or (CommandLine:"*.exe tunnel*" and CommandLine:"*--accept-server-license-terms*") or (CommandLine:"*tunnel *" and CommandLine:"*service*" and CommandLine:"*internal-run*" and CommandLine:"*tunnel-service.log*")) and (not (Image like~ ("*\\code-tunnel.exe", "*\\code.exe")))) or ((ParentCommandLine:"* tunnel" and Image:"*\\cmd.exe" and (CommandLine:"*/d /c *" and CommandLine:"*\\servers\\Stable-*" and CommandLine:"*code-server.cmd*")) and (not (ParentImage like~ ("*\\code-tunnel.exe", "*\\code.exe")))))

name: SIGMA - Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the installation of VsCode tunnel (code-tunnel) as a service.
false_positives: Legitimate installation of code-tunnel as a service
query: any where EventID:1 and (CommandLine:"*tunnel *" and CommandLine:"*service*" and CommandLine:"*internal-run*" and CommandLine:"*tunnel-service.log*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "VSDiagnostics.exe" with the "start" command in order to launch and proxy arbitrary binaries.
false_positives: Legitimate usage for tracing and diagnostics purposes
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\VSDiagnostics.exe" or OriginalFileName:"VSDiagnostics.exe") and CommandLine:"*start*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* /launch:*", "* -launch:*")))

name: SIGMA - Proxy Execution via Vshadow
author: David Faiss
description:
Detects the invocation of vshadow.exe with the -exec parameter that executes a specified script or command after the shadow copies are created but before the VShadow tool exits.
VShadow is a command-line tool that you can use to create and manage volume shadow copies. While legitimate backup or administrative scripts may use this flag,
attackers can leverage this parameter to proxy the execution of malware.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\vshadow.exe" or OriginalFileName:"vshadow.exe") and CommandLine:"*-exec*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load
author: bohops
description: Detects Microsoft Visual Studio vsls-agent.exe lolbin execution with a suspicious library load using the --agentExtensionPath parameter
false_positives: False positives depend on custom use of vsls-agent.exe
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\vsls-agent.exe" and CommandLine:"*--agentExtensionPath*") and (not CommandLine:"*Microsoft.VisualStudio.LiveShare.Agent.*"))

name: SIGMA - Use of W32tm as Timer
author: frack113
description: When configured with suitable command line arguments, w32tm can act as a delay mechanism
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\w32tm.exe" or OriginalFileName:"w32time.dll") and (CommandLine:"*/stripchart*" and CommandLine:"*/computer:*" and CommandLine:"*/period:*" and CommandLine:"*/dataonly*" and CommandLine:"*/samples:*"))

name: SIGMA - Wab Execution From Non Default Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) from non default locations as seen with bumblebee activity
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image like~ ("*\\wab.exe", "*\\wabmig.exe")) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Mail\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Mail\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects unusual parent or children of the wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) processes as seen being used with bumblebee activity
query: any where EventID:1 and (((ParentImage like~ ("*\\WmiPrvSE.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe")) and (Image like~ ("*\\wab.exe", "*\\wabmig.exe"))) or (ParentImage like~ ("*\\wab.exe", "*\\wabmig.exe")))

name: SIGMA - All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the deletion of all backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"*delete*" and CommandLine:"*backup*") and CommandLine:"*keepVersions:0*"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Backup Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the deletion of backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe".
This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors.
This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.
false_positives: Legitimate backup activity from administration scripts and software.
query: any where EventID:1 and (((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and (CommandLine:"*delete *" and CommandLine:"*backup*")) and (not CommandLine:"*keepVersions:0*"))

name: SIGMA - Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
false_positives: Legitimate backup operation by authorized administrators. Matches must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and (CommandLine like~ ("*start*", "*backup*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\config\\SAM*", "*\\config\\SECURITY*", "*\\config\\SYSTEM*", "*\\Windows\\NTDS\\NTDS.dit*")))

name: SIGMA - File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe".
Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and (CommandLine:"* recovery*" and CommandLine:"*recoveryTarget*" and CommandLine:"*itemtype:File*"))

name: SIGMA - Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive.
Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\wbadmin.exe" or OriginalFileName:"WBADMIN.EXE") and ((CommandLine:"* recovery*" and CommandLine:"*recoveryTarget*" and CommandLine:"*itemtype:File*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\config\\SAM*", "*\\config\\SECURITY*", "*\\config\\SYSTEM*", "*\\Windows\\NTDS\\NTDS.dit*"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution
author: Micah Babinski
description: Detects possible execution via LNK file accessed on a WebDAV server.
query: any where EventID:1 and (ParentImage:"*\\explorer.exe" and (Image like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\wscript.exe")) and CommandLine:"*\\DavWWWRoot\\*")

name: SIGMA - Chopper Webshell Process Pattern
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), MSTI (query)
description: Detects patterns found in process executions cause by China Chopper like tiny (ASPX) webshells
query: any where EventID:1 and ((Image:"*\\w3wp.exe" or ParentImage:"*\\w3wp.exe") and (CommandLine like~ ("*&ipconfig&echo*", "*&quser&echo*", "*&whoami&echo*", "*&c:&echo*", "*&cd&echo*", "*&dir&echo*", "*&echo [E]*", "*&echo [S]*")))

name: SIGMA - Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects certain parent child patterns found in cases in which a web shell is used to perform certain credential dumping or exfiltration activities on a compromised system
